title: US Tax Manager
description:
This is not a typical tax job.



This is a high-intensity U.S. tax role for someone who wants to become exceptionally strong in federal tax law, return review, technical issue spotting, and written client communication.



We are hiring a Tax Manager whose primary responsibility will be reviewing U.S. tax returns and helping prepare clear, technically accurate written communications for clients.



This role is for someone who wants to compete directly in the U.S. tax market based on skill, judgment, speed, technical excellence, and written communication ability.



It is not easy.

It is not slow-paced.

It is not a role for someone who wants light hours or surface-level tax work.



This is a demanding position with long hours, especially during peak tax season. But for the right person, it is also an opportunity to learn U.S. tax at a very high level from two JD, CPAs, including Jasmine DiLucci, and build a skillset that can place you among the strongest tax professionals in the field.



This is the kind of opportunity that is not usually offered to offshore tax professionals: deep technical review responsibility, written client advisory work, direct exposure to advanced U.S. tax strategy, and a path to become a true leader inside a U.S.-based tax firm.



We are not just hiring someone to process returns. We are looking for the firm’s next technical leader — someone with hunger, discipline, strong tax judgment, and the desire to earn at U.S. professional levels by developing U.S.-level skill.



You can learn more about Jasmine’s approach to U.S. tax law here:

YouTube: 



About the Role



This is a 100% remote technical tax role focused on:



U.S. federal tax return review

Technical issue spotting

Written client explanations

Review notes and internal documentation

Tax compliance quality control

Identifying planning opportunities and reporting risks



Your job is to make sure tax returns are technically accurate, well-supported, clearly reviewed, and aligned with U.S. tax law.



You will also help translate complex tax issues into written client communications that are clear, professional, and technically sound.



Key Responsibilities



U.S. Tax Return Review

Perform detailed reviews of U.S. federal income tax returns for business owners, entrepreneurs, and real estate investors

Review individual, business, and related entity returns for accuracy, completeness, and consistency

Identify errors, omissions, inconsistencies, and unsupported tax positions

Review reporting positions for compliance with current U.S. tax law

Confirm that deductions, credits, basis calculations, entity reporting, and elections are properly handled

Identify missed tax-saving opportunities when reviewing returns

Prepare clear review notes for corrections, questions, or additional documentation needed



Written Client Communications

Draft written explanations of tax issues, filing positions, corrections, and recommendations

Help prepare technically accurate client-facing written responses

Communicate complex U.S. tax concepts in plain English without losing technical precision

Support the client-facing team by preparing strong written tax analysis

Document tax positions clearly so clients understand what was done, why it matters, and what needs to happen next



Technical Quality Control

Review calculations, schedules, elections, entity reporting, depreciation, basis, and income classification

Ensure consistency with firm standards, review processes, and documentation requirements

Identify audit risk, compliance gaps, and aggressive or unsupported positions

Maintain a high level of accuracy across multiple returns and deadlines

Work independently while escalating complex technical issues when needed



This role may be a strong fit if you:



Want to become one of the best in U.S. tax

Want a real path toward leadership inside a U.S.-based tax firm

Want to earn at U.S. professional levels by building U.S.-level technical skill

Enjoy technical return review more than basic preparation work

Can handle demanding hours and high expectations

Are motivated by mastery, not comfort

Want to compete in the U.S. market using real technical skill

Care deeply about accuracy, compliance, and quality

Can write clearly and professionally about tax issues

Are hungry to grow, improve, and take on more responsibility

Are comfortable being tested on your knowledge



Requirements

8+ years of experience with U.S. federal income tax returns

Significant experience reviewing returns for U.S. business owners

Strong knowledge of U.S. individual and business tax law

Experience reviewing entity returns, including S corporations, partnerships, and related individual returns

Strong technical judgment and ability to identify reporting issues

Ability to write clear, professional, technically accurate client communications

Strong attention to detail and analytical ability

Experience with tax preparation and accounting software

Ability to work independently with minimal supervision

Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple deadlines

Willingness to work 60–80 hours per week, especially during peak tax periods

Comfort working later hours when needed

Hunger to grow into a leadership-level technical role within the firm

CPA, EA, or equivalent certification strongly preferred



Interview Process



The first-round interview is highly technical.



You should expect to be tested on U.S. tax concepts, real-world tax scenarios, return review judgment, and written communication ability.



We are looking for candidates who can think through tax issues, explain their reasoning, identify risk, and make technically sound review decisions.



We are not hiring based on résumé language alone. We are looking for demonstrated ability, hunger, precision, and judgment.



Candidates who are early in their tax careers or unable to demonstrate advanced hands-on tax review experience will not be considered.



To show attention to detail, put the word “DiLucci” in your subject line when applying.



Compensation



$50,000 – $100,000 OTE, depending on experience, technical ability, communication skills, leadership potential, and performance.



This compensation range is designed for someone who wants to build toward U.S.-level earning potential by developing U.S.-level tax skill, judgment, and responsibility.

title: B2B Growth Marketer & Lead Generation Specialist
description:
Job Title: B2B Growth Marketer & Lead Gen Specialist (Not an Admin Role!)



The Company:

Porameta is launching as Portugal’s first premium, Agent-Exclusive Commercial Real Estate Marketplace. We are a fast-moving, agile team split between Porto, Portugal, and Manila, Philippines. We are gearing up for a massive public launch on June 15th, and we need a heavy-hitting digital marketer to build our engine.



The Mission 

Before we open our doors to global investors, we need to populate our marketplace with premium commercial properties. Your mission is purely B2B (Business-to-Business): You must build automated systems to acquire, contact, and convert Portuguese real estate agents, convincing them to upload their properties to our platform before launch day.



This is NOT a general Virtual Assistant, Admin, or Organic Social Media role. If your primary skills are calendar management, creating Canva graphics, or writing basic
 posts, please do not apply. We are looking for a highly technical Growth Marketer.



What You Will Actually Do:



B2B Data Scraping & Harvesting: Build pristine, highly targeted lists of independent real estate agents in Portugal using scraping tools (PhantomBuster, Apollo, Lusha, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, etc.).



WhatsApp API & Outbound Automation: We don't use standard cold
; European agents live on WhatsApp. You will set up, manage, and scale automated B2B outreach campaigns using the WhatsApp Business API (via tools like 
, WATI, or GoHighLevel).



Paid B2B Acquisition: Launch and manage highly targeted Meta 
/IG) and LinkedIn Ad campaigns aimed strictly at commercial brokers in Lisbon, Porto, and the Algarve. You will own the ROAS and CPC metrics.



CRM & Funnel Management: Build the lead-routing system so when an agent replies to our automated messages, the warm lead is instantly routed to our local Director in Portugal to close the deal.



The Skills You Must Have:



Proven experience building scalable B2B Lead Generation funnels from scratch.



Deep understanding of Paid Ads (Meta Ads Manager & LinkedIn Campaign Manager).



Experience integrating APIs, CRMs (HubSpot, GoHighLevel, etc.), and messaging platforms.



A highly analytical mindset. You love A/B testing copy, optimizing conversion rates, and making decisions based on data, not guesses.



Perfect written English. (Our local team will handle the Portuguese translations, but you must be able to communicate strategy flawlessly).



Why Work With Us?

You won't be micromanaged doing repetitive tasks. You are being hired to be the architect of our digital growth engine. You will work directly with the Global Project Director and the local team in Europe to launch a platform that disrupts the Portuguese commercial real estate market.



How to Apply:

To prove you have the high attention to detail required for this role, please begin your cover letter/message with the exact phrase: "I am ready to launch Porameta." Applications without this phrase will be instantly deleted. In your application, please include a 2-3 sentence description of a specific B2B outbound campaign or Paid Ad funnel you successfully built in the past.

title: YouTube Thumbnail Designer
description:
You know MrBeast. Now work with the person who helped direct it.



Our founder spent years as a video director at MrBeast, working on content that generated over 10 billion views. He knows what makes someone click. He knows what stops the scroll, what creates curiosity, and what a thumbnail needs to do before a single second of video plays. That knowledge drives everything we do at Biggest Agency.



We produce high-end YouTube shows and podcasts for entrepreneurs and business leaders. We are growing our creative team and looking for a thumbnail designer overseas who understands that a great thumbnail is not decoration. It is the first sale.



What this role will do for you

Most design jobs have you executing someone else’s vision with no context for whether it worked. This one is different.



You will see real performance data. You will know your CTR. You will run A/B tests in YouTube Studio and track what actually makes people click. Over time you will develop an instinct for high-performing thumbnail design that most designers never get the chance to build.



Our standards are high. Our feedback is direct. You will be pushed to think like a strategist, not just a designer.



This role starts part-time with a clear path to full-time as volume scales.



What you will be designing

You design three thumbnails per episode. For each one you will receive a brief, a transcript, and the episode trailer to work from. You pitch your concepts to the team before you build. Once approved, you deliver the finals.



You will start at 9 thumbnails per week (approximately 10 hours), scaling to 36 or more as the agency grows and your capacity expands.



Beyond design, you are part of the performance loop. You will set up A/B tests in YouTube Studio, monitor CTR across shows, and report findings back to the team. If something is not clicking, we need to know why and what to try next. That is part of your job.



You will collaborate closely with our editors and account managers. Thumbnails do not live in isolation. They connect to the episode title, the trailer, and the overall show brand. We expect you to understand that relationship and design accordingly.



What we need from you

Canva (required)

Adobe Photoshop and/or Illustrator (strongly preferred)

Proven experience designing YouTube thumbnails specifically — not just general graphic design

Understanding of CTR, YouTube thumbnail best practices, and what drives clicks

Ability to concept, pitch, and execute — not just take direction

Comfortable working inside YouTube Studio for A/B testing and analytics

Available during US business hours for feedback and collaboration

Fast, reliable internet



What great looks like in this role

You study thumbnails obsessively. You know which channels are winning on YouTube right now and why. You can look at a transcript and immediately see the angle that will make someone click. You take feedback without ego and apply it faster than expected. You track your own performance and bring data to conversations, not just opinions.



You treat every thumbnail like it is the most important one you have ever made. Because for that client, it is.



To apply

Record a 2 to 3 minute video. Introduce yourself, show us your best YouTube thumbnail work, and walk us through one thumbnail you are proud of... what was the brief, what was your thinking, and what did it perform like if you have the data. Upload it to YouTube or Google Drive and include the link.



Also include a link to your portfolio. The work matters more than your resume.

title: Short Form Video Editor
description:
You know MrBeast. Now work with the person who helped direct it.



Our founder spent years as a video director at MrBeast, working on content that generated over 10 billion views. He understands what stops the scroll, what keeps people watching, and what makes short-form content actually perform. That knowledge drives everything we do at Biggest Agency.



We produce high-end YouTube shows and podcasts for entrepreneurs and business leaders. We are growing our editing team and looking for a short-form editor overseas who is fast, precise, and obsessed with the craft.



What this role will do for you

Short-form editing is not just cutting a clip and slapping on captions. Done right, it is one of the most technical and creative skills in content production.



Our standards are high. Our feedback is direct. You will be pushed to understand why certain 
nts work and others do not, and you will get better faster here than almost anywhere else.



This role starts part-time with a clear path to full-time as the relationship grows.



What you will be editing

You do not pick the clips. Your Senior Editor or Account Manager identifies the 
nts from each episode. Your job is to make them hit.



Each clip is 60 seconds or under with clean captions, tight cuts, minimal motion graphics. The standard is high.



You will start with approximately 15 clips per week, growing to 30 or more as volume scales. At the production level required for this role, that is very achievable. We will grow your hours with you.



Finished clips are delivered through 
 for review before anything goes to the client. Revisions are part of the job and are not paid separately. Study the feedback. Apply it. Raise your standard before you submit.



One honest note: understanding what resonates with an American business audience takes time. The way a 
nt needs to be framed, paced, and hooked for this audience is specific. Your early cuts will often come back with feedback. That is expected and built into our process. The editors who grow fastest here are the ones who study American short-form content, pay attention to what performs, and treat every round of feedback as a lesson rather than a correction.



What we need from you

CapCut (primary tool for this role)

Adobe Premiere Pro (required)

Fast, reliable internet

Available during US business hours for communication and revisions



A note on your computer

Short-form editing at this production level is not as hardware-intensive as long-form, but your machine still needs to handle it cleanly. Before you apply, confirm your setup is solid.



Minimum specs:

Processor: Intel i5/i7, AMD Ryzen 5/7, or Apple M1 or newer

RAM: 16GB minimum

Storage: 512GB SSD minimum

Stable internet for file transfers and 



In your application video, show us your setup or walk us through your specs.



To apply

Record a 2 to 3 minute video. Introduce yourself, tell us how long you have been editing, show us short-form work you are proud of, and tell us why this role interests you. Upload it to YouTube or Google Drive and include the link.



The video matters more than your resume.

title: Podcast Video Editor
description:
You know MrBeast. Now work with the person who helped direct it.



Our founder spent years as a video director at MrBeast, working on content that generated over 10 billion views. He knows what makes people watch. He knows pacing, storytelling, and how to build a video that holds attention from start to finish. That knowledge drives everything we do at Biggest Agency.



We produce high-end YouTube shows and podcasts for entrepreneurs and business leaders. We are growing our editing team and looking for someone overseas who is hungry, talented, and ready to be pushed.



What this role will do for you

Most editing jobs have you cutting the same content on autopilot every week. This one is different.



Our standards are high. Our feedback is honest and direct. Our process has been shaped by billions of views of content that actually performed.



After one year with us, you will be a significantly better editor. You will understand long-form storytelling and podcast pacing at a level most editors never reach. You will have the work to prove it.



We do not just want to hire you. We want to develop you.



This role starts part-time with a clear path to full-time as the relationship grows.



What you will be editing

You own the long-form episode from start to finish. Each week you will deliver fully edited podcast episodes — multicam footage, professional audio, color, and pacing. Finished files are delivered through Google Drive.



You will start with 3 stringouts per week, growing to 6 or more as the relationship develops and capacity expands.



Your Senior Editor reviews every cut and gives direct feedback before anything goes to the client. Revisions are part of the job and are not paid separately. The editors who thrive here study the feedback, apply it fast, and hold themselves to a high standard before they submit anything.



What we need from you

Adobe Premiere Pro

Fast, reliable internet

Available during US business hours for communication and revisions



A note on your computer

Editing multicam podcast footage with professional audio is demanding. A slow machine will slow down your delivery and make this job hard. Before you apply, confirm your setup can handle this work.



Minimum specs:

Processor: Intel i7/i9, AMD Ryzen 7/9, or Apple M1 Pro / M2 / M3 or newer

RAM: 32GB minimum, 64GB preferred

Storage: 1TB SSD minimum — you will regularly receive and store large project files

GPU: Dedicated card with 8GB+ VRAM (PC) or Apple Silicon



In your application video, show us your setup or walk us through your specs.



To apply

Record a 2 to 3 minute video. Introduce yourself, tell us how long you have been editing, show us your best long-form work, and tell us why this role interests you. Upload it to YouTube or Google Drive and include the link.



The video matters more than your resume.

title: Essay Editor/Academic Writer
description:
We’re an academic consulting firm that helps high school students gain admission to top-tier universities in the U.S., and we’re currently seeking a part-time Essay Editor/Academic Writer with strong writing, editing, and critical thinking skills to join our team.



This role is ideal for someone who can take rough or underdeveloped student essays and turn them into polished, well-structured pieces that read naturally and professionally. The position is part-time and flexible, but it’s also deadline-driven and requires strong reliability, responsiveness, and attention to detail.



This is not a general content writing role. We’re specifically looking for someone who can revise and rewrite essays so they are clearer, stronger, and more compelling while still aligning with the original prompt, intended message, and required word count.



Key Responsibilities:

• Edit and rewrite high school-level essays based on specific prompts and word count requirements, improving structure, clarity, grammar, tone, and overall impact.

• Manage multiple essay assignments at once while meeting tight turnaround times, typically within 24-48 hours, and communicate proactively regarding deadlines or issues that may affect delivery.



Applicants should be able to:

• Rewrite and strengthen weak or underdeveloped drafts without losing the original intent, while elevating flow, structure, readability, and overall quality.

• Demonstrate excellent grammar, sentence control, and word choice, and write with a level of polish that reads as fluent and natural in English.



To proceed with your application, please send a message with your resume, 2-3 writing samples for review, and an estimate of how many hours you can commit each week. Candidates who don’t follow these instructions will not be considered for this role or next steps.

SALARY INCLUDES SALES BONUS AVERAGE

, social, digital) to generate leads and build brand awareness

title: GoHighLevel (GHL) Team Lead — Elite Level Only
description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Candor Marketing is a done-for-you digital marketing agency based in the United States. We serve clients across multiple industries including health and wellness clinics, home services, property management, gyms and fitness, food service, and more. We help our clients generate leads, book appointments, and grow their businesses using paid advertising, organic content, and GoHighLevel automation.



GoHighLevel is the backbone of everything we deliver. We build full backend systems for our clients inside GHL every single day — lead capture, CRM pipelines, automations, follow-up sequences, and everything in between. We are looking for someone who doesn't just know GHL well. We are looking for someone who is genuinely elite at it.



READ THIS FIRST

We are not looking for someone decent at GoHighLevel. We are not looking for someone good or even great. We are looking for someone who is among the best in the world at this platform — someone who has built hundreds of automations, troubleshot every integration imaginable, and can walk into any client account and immediately know what needs to happen.

Before you read any further, understand this: this role requires you to work American business hours, Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM Eastern Standard Time. You must be available and active on Slack during those hours every single workday. This is the most important operational requirement of this role. If you cannot commit to this, please do not apply.



THE ROLE

The GHL Team Lead owns all GoHighLevel builds, systems, and client account management across our agency. You are the internal expert. You set the standard. You make sure every lead form connects to the right backend workflow, every automation fires correctly, every tag is applied with intention, and every client account is running like a machine.



You will work closely with our media buyer to make sure the backend systems you build are aligned with what's happening on the ad side — so leads don't fall through the cracks and every dollar we spend on ads converts as efficiently as possible. This is a collaborative, results-driven role. You own the backend. You protect the results.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

Lead Form & Backend Integration



Connect lead forms from
, Google, landing pages, and other sources directly into GHL sub-accounts

Map form fields to CRM contacts, pipelines, and trigger conditions with precision

Ensure every lead that comes in is captured, tagged, routed, and followed up with automatically

Troubleshoot broken form connections, missed webhooks, and integration failures fast



SMS &
 Automations



Build multi-step SMS automation sequences for lead follow-up, appointment reminders, reactivation campaigns, and more

Build
 automation workflows including nurture sequences, confirmation flows, and re-engagement campaigns

Write or refine automation copy that actually converts — not just placeholder text

A/B test messaging cadences and optimize based on response and booking rates



Lead Tagging & CRM Logic



Build and manage a clean, logical tagging system across all client accounts

Apply tags based on lead source, behavior, pipeline stage, funnel entry point, and campaign

Use tags to trigger the right automations, segment contacts correctly, and keep CRM data clean

Audit tag logic regularly to prevent bloat, conflicts, and misfires



Pipeline & Funnel Management



Build and maintain sales pipelines and opportunity stages for each client

Set up landing pages, VSL funnels, opt-in pages, and booking flows inside GHL

Ensure calendar integrations, availability settings, and booking confirmations work perfectly

Configure Zapier, Webhooks, and native integrations to connect GHL with outside platforms



Media Buyer Coordination



Work directly with our media buyer to align backend automations with active ad campaigns

Ensure lead forms, tracking, UTMs, and CRM pipelines are set up before campaigns go live

Communicate clearly when backend issues are affecting lead flow or campaign performance

Proactively flag anything on the GHL side that could be costing us conversion efficiency



Multi-Industry Client Management



Manage GHL sub-accounts across a diverse client base including clinics, home service companies, gyms, property management, food service businesses, and general service clients



Understand that different industries have different follow-up logic, sales cycles, and automation needs — and build accordingly

Set up new client sub-accounts from scratch and audit existing ones regularly



Document all builds clearly inside ClickUp so the team always knows what's been done and why



Team Leadership



Serve as the internal subject matter expert for all things GHL

Maintain SOPs and build documentation so nothing is dependent on one person

Help train and onboard future GHL support hires as the team scales

Attend the weekly team call and communicate clearly on progress, blockers, and priorities





WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Non-Negotiable Skills:



Elite-level GoHighLevel experience — agency sub-account management at scale

Mastery of GHL workflows, triggers, conditions, pipelines, and automation logic

Deep experience connecting lead forms from multiple sources into GHL backends

Expert-level SMS and
 automation build experience

Sophisticated understanding of lead tagging systems and CRM data hygiene

Experience building and optimizing landing pages and funnels inside GHL

Full command of GHL integrations:
, Google, Twilio, Mailgun, Zapier, Webhooks

EXTREME proficiency in English and clear, proactive communication

Detail-obsessed — you QA everything before it goes live and you catch your own mistakes



Highly Preferred:



Experience working inside a digital marketing agency managing multiple client accounts simultaneously

Familiarity with Meta Ads lead integrations and how ad backend connects to GHL

ManyChat or chat automation experience

ClickUp or equivalent project management workflow

Experience across multiple industries — the more diverse the better

Basic HTML or CSS for custom GHL page edits



What Elite Looks Like to Us:



You can build a full lead-to-booked-appointment automation from scratch without instructions

You have a system for how you think about tagging before you touch a single account

You have caught automation failures before a client or tea
mber noticed

You can build masterful landing pages using elite sales psychology and not basic landing pages

You have coordinated directly with an ads team to make sure front end and back end were aligned

You can look at a broken GHL workflow and diagnose the problem in minutes

Your builds don't just work on day one — they hold up for months without breaking





AVAILABILITY & COMMUNICATION REQUIREMENTS — THIS IS NON-NEGOTIABLE

This is not a flexible guideline. This is a firm operational requirement. Read it carefully.

Working Hours: Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM Eastern Standard Time (EST). These are your required working hours. No exceptions. No flexibility on this. If your time zone makes this a hardship, please do not apply.



Slack Availability: You must be active on Slack during every working hour of every workday. Slack is our primary communication tool. Being logged in is not enough — you must be present, attentive, and responsive throughout the day.

Response Time: You are expected to reply to any Slack message within 20 minutes or less during working hours. If something urgent comes up and you need more time, you acknowledge the message immediately and give a timeframe. Silence is not acceptable.



Going Offline: If you need to step away during working hours for any reason, you must notify the team in Slack before you go. Disappearing without notice — even briefly — is not acceptable.



Going dark without warning during working hours is grounds for immediate termination of contract. We have had issues with this in the past and we will not tolerate it. We are a fast-moving agency and our clients depend on us being reachable and responsive. We expect the same from every member of our team.



Weekly Team Call: You are required to attend a one-hour team call once per week. The day and time will be agreed upon together at the time of hire and will fall within working hours.



WHY WORK WITH CANDOR



100% remote — work from wherever you are

Consistent, long-term work — we do not hire and churn contractors

Clear expectations — you will always know what is needed and when

Diverse client base — you will work across multiple industries and never get bored

Direct collaboration with the media buyer and leadership team

Room to grow — high performers get expanded scope and better pay over time

Real ownership — you will be trusted to lead, not just execute tickets





HOW TO APPLY

To be considered, submit all of the following:



Your resume or a written summary of your GHL experience including how many client accounts you have managed and what industries you have worked in



Written confirmation that you are available 9 AM to 5 PM Eastern Standard Time, Monday through Friday, and that you understand the Slack response requirement

Your preferred monthly or hourly rate



Applications without a Loom video will not be reviewed. We are specifically looking for someone who can show their work, not just describe it.

We respond to every complete application. If you are a strong fit, we will schedule a video interview within a few business days.

title: Virtual Assistant – Bookkeeping & Job Costing
description:
We are a growing HVAC & Plumbing company based in Houston, TX, looking for a highly detail-oriented Virtual Assistant specialized in bookkeeping and financial tracking.



This role is NOT a general VA position. We are looking for someone with strong bookkeeping experience who understands how to track expenses and profitability per project/job.



---



### ???? Responsibilities:



- Manage bookkeeping using QuickBooks Online

- Track and categorize all expenses daily (materials, labor, subcontractors, gas, tools, etc.)

- Assign every expense to the correct project (by property address)

- Maintain a clean and accurate financial system down to the exact dollar

- Reconcile bank accounts and credit cards regularly

- Review transactions (5–10 per day) and ensure proper classification

- Collect and organize receipts and supporting documents



---



### ???? Reporting:



- Create weekly reports in Excel including:

  - Total expenses per project

  - Total income per project

  - Profit per job/project

- Reports must be clear, organized, and highly detailed

- Ability to identify discrepancies or missing information



---



### ???? Requirements:



- Strong experience with QuickBooks Online

- Experience with job costing / project-based accounting

- Advanced Excel skills (must be comfortable building reports)

- Extremely detail-oriented (every expense must be tracked correctly)

- Ability to work independently and stay organized

- Good English communication skills



---



### ? Schedule:



- Full-time in the beginning (training & system setup)

- After stabilization, role can become more flexible as long as all work is completed and reports are delivered on time



---



### ???? Access:



- Will be given access to company bank accounts and credit cards (viewing for bookkeeping purposes only)



---



### ???? Compensation:



- Based on experience

- Opportunity to move to a fixed monthly salary after proving performance



---



### ? Ideal Candidate:



Someone who:

- Has worked with service companies (HVAC, plumbing, construction, etc.)

- Understands how to track profit per job

- Can keep financial records clean and organized without supervision

- Takes ownership and responsibility for accuracy



---



If you are detail-oriented and experienced in bookkeeping with a strong understanding of project-based tracking, we would like to hear from you.



Please apply with your experience and examples of reports you have created.

-Revise plans based on feedback

-Coordinate with the team to keep projects on track



What I’m Looking For

-Experience using CAD tools (LandFX, AutoCAD, or similar)

-Background in landscape or irrigation design

-Understanding of layouts, measurements, and scaling

-Strong attention to detail and accuracy

-Ability to work independently and meet deadlines



Nice to Have (Not Required)

:

Landscape CAD Designer Application

Updated resume

“I have 2+ years of experience in CAD design, focusing on landscape and irrigation layouts. I have worked with AutoCAD and LandFX to create detailed plans and material takeoffs. I am detail-oriented and comfortable working remotely with structured project workflows.”

title: Video Editor
description:
About Tend Productions:



Tend Productions is a video production company based in Austin, Texas. We produce content for a diverse roster of clients across industries including combat sports, food and restaurant, real estate, CPG brands, and more. Our work spans short-form social content, branded commercial videos, cinematic food and lifestyle content, and paid ad creative.

We’re looking for a talented video editor to join us on a part-time basis and grow with the company. This is not a one-off gig — it’s an opportunity to become an integral part of a small, fast-moving production team where your work directly impacts the brand and bottom line for real clients.



What You’ll Do:



- Edit raw footage (sometimes SLOG3) into polished final deliverables based on detailed creative briefs

- Create short-form social content (Reels, TikToks, YouTube Shorts) optimized for engagement and platform best practices

- Edit listing videos, branded content, cinematic food videos, and personality-driven content across multiple clients

- Reformat and repurpose long-form content into multiple platform-specific cuts (16:9, 9:16, 1:1, 4:5)

- Manage and organize project files in shared cloud storage (Google Drive)

- Communicate proactively on timelines, questions, and revision status



Who You Are:



Required

- Fluent in English — written and spoken. You’ll be on weekly video calls and communicating semi-daily via messaging

- Proficient in Adobe Premiere Pro or DaVinci Resolve

- 2+ years of professional video editing experience with a portfolio or reel you can share

- Ability to edit for multiple different brands

- Strong understanding of pacing, storytelling, and what makes short-form content perform on social platforms

- Solid color correction and audio mixing skills

- Reliable high-speed internet and your own editing workstation capable of handling 4K footage

- Available for a weekly sync call during U.S. Central Time business hours (flexible on exact time)

- Self-motivated and organized — you can manage your own schedule and meet deadlines without being micromanaged



Nice to Have:



- After Effects skills for motion graphics, transitions, and animated text

- Experience editing food/restaurant content, real estate listing videos, or combat sports highlights

- Familiarity with social media trends, hooks, and platform-specific best practices (TikTok, Instagram, YouTube Shorts)

- Experience working with creative briefs and feedback/revision workflows

- Basic sound design and SFX skills

title: Virtual Hotel Experience Specialist
description:
The Opportunity: Bring Your 5-Star Service Home

Are you a hospitality professional from a hotel or fine dining background who wants a stable, long-term remote career?



TruPorch Homes (PillowPM) is looking for you. We manage premium short-term rental properties that offer the comfort of a home with the service of a boutique hotel. We are not a generic call center. We do not use robotic scripts. We are looking for true hospitality professionals who have "Service DNA" and know how to make guests feel welcomed, heard, and cared for.



What You Will Do (The "Virtual Front Desk")

You will be the voice and face of our brand. You will handle the guest journey from inquiry to checkout, acting as a "Digital Concierge."



Guest Communication: Handle inbound calls (via VoIP) and messages (Airbnb, Vrbo, 
) with warmth, perfect grammar, and professional etiquette.

Problem Solving: Swiftly resolve guest issues (e.g., smart lock trouble, Wi-Fi issues, heating/cooling) by troubleshooting or dispatching our ground team.

Coordination: Act as the central hub between guests and our cleaners/maintenance staff to ensure properties are perfect for every arrival.

Revenue & Reviews: Assist with reservation modifications and follow up with guests to secure 5-star reviews.

Who We Are Looking For



Hospitality Veterans: You have 2+ years of experience in Guest Services, Front Desk, or Concierge roles at reputable hotels (e.g., Shangri-La, Marriott, Accor) or high-end dining establishments. 

Communication Mastery: You possess a neutral English accent for phone calls and near-native written English for messaging. You know how to de-escalate upset guests with empathy, not scripts.

Tech-Savvy: You can navigate multiple browser tabs, learn new software (PMS, Slack, Airtable) quickly, and type fast.

Reliability: You are disciplined enough to work from home without supervision.

Technical Requirements (Non-Negotiable)



Internet: Reliable Fiber connection (Minimum 25 Mbps download/upload) with a backup connection plan.

Hardware: A working PC/Laptop capable of handling VoIP calls and a noise-canceling headset.

Environment: A dedicated, quiet home office space. (No roosters, barking dogs, or background noise during shift).

Schedule



This is a US Hours / Graveyard Shift role (e.g., EST or PST hours). You must be willing to work nights and weekends/holidays on a rotating schedule, as hospitality never sleeps.

What We Offer



100% Remote: Work from the comfort of your home.

Stable Career: We are looking for long-term tea
mbers, not temporary freelancers.

Supportive Team: We provide training and treat our remote team with the same respect as our local team.

Apply Here:

title: Shopify Web Developer and CRO Specialist
description:
Full-Time Shopify Web Developer & CRO Specialist (Dedicated In-House Role – No Agencies or Side Clients)



Job Description:

We are looking for a full-time, dedicated Shopify Web Developer and Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) expert to join our in-house team permanently.



This is not a freelance or agency position. We want one person who will own our entire Shopify store and treat it like their own business — someone who lives and breathes profit-driven CRO the same way top specialists do.



You will build, manage, and continuously optimize a high-converting Shopify store from the ground up or take an existing store to the next level. Your focus will be on creating beautiful, brand-cohesive, high-converting interfaces that look premium and actually sell. No internal creative team — you will be the one aligning design, UX, and conversion strategy.



Key Responsibilities:

Build and manage the entire Shopify website (themes, Liquid code, custom sections, checkout optimization, etc.)

Design and optimize high-converting landing pages, product detail pages (PDPs), collection pages, and full sales funnels.



Execute true CRO: A/B testing, heatmaps, analytics-driven changes, offer strategy, copy that converts, mobile-first UX, and urgency/scarcity elements



Create visually appealing designs that maintain strong brand cohesion while maximizing conversion rate and average order value



Handle full site management: ongoing maintenance, speed optimization, new feature rollouts, and quick fixes



Deliver measurable results (we track revenue/profit lifts, not just “looks nice”)



What We’re Looking For (Must-Have):



-3+ years of hands-on Shopify experience (Liquid, theme customization, page builders like Replo, PageFly, GemPages, etc.)

-Proven CRO expertise with before/after examples and real results (CVR lifts, revenue increases, AOV growth)

-Strong design eye — you can create clean, modern, brand-aligned interfaces without hand-holding

-Ability to work independently and make decisions that balance aesthetics with conversion

-Excellent English communication and availability for US business hours overlap

-Portfolio that shows profit-driven work (not just pretty mockups)



Bonus Points If You:



-Have worked with $1M–$25M+ DTC/eCom brands

-Share or study CRO content similar 
 (before/afters, swipe files, funnel optimization)

-Know how to turn product offers, copy, and layout into actual sales



What We Offer:



Full-time, long-term dedicated role (8 hours/day, 5 days/week)

Competitive hourly rate

Opportunity to grow with the brand and take ownership of a real eCommerce store

Direct impact on revenue



Important:

This is a dedicated full-time position only. Please do not apply if you are running an agency, working with multiple clients, or looking for part
 work.



How to Apply:



Reply with:

Your updated resume/portfolio link

2–3 examples of Shopify landing pages or PDPs you’ve built + the results they achieved (CVR or revenue lift)

Confirmation that you are available for full-time dedicated work with no other clients



We review applications quickly and will message strong candidates within 24–48 hours.

title: Order Fulfillment Specialist
description:
Full-Time • Remote



Company Overview

Pearl West Group is a hyper-growth DTC e-commerce company that owns and operates six

health, wellness, and pet brands — a mix of homegrown and acquired — running at $40M+ in

combined annual revenue and growing fast. Think mini Unilever, but lean, operator-led, and

built to move.



We run a centralized shared-services model across growth, media, creative, product, and ops.

We’re well-capitalized, actively acquiring brands, and each brand follows a 3-year scale-and-exit

playbook. Our roots are in Amazon, and TikTok Shops is our next breakout channel.



Our mission: To build brands that leave lasting impressions — and exit them at their peak.

Our values: Competitive Greatness • Speed to Execution • Team Empowerment • Relentless

Ownership



Role Overview

As Pearl West scales across multiple brands and fulfillment channels, we’re creating a

dedicated Order Fulfillment Specialist to own the critical last mile: from the 
nt a product

leaves our warehouse to the 
nt it lands in a customer’s hands. This role is the operational

bridge between Supply Chain and Customer Service — eliminating ownership gaps, reducing

fulfillment failures, and keeping both teams focused on what they do best.



This is a high-ownership, execution-first role. You’ll monitor orders in real time, identify stalled or

failed shipments before customers do, escalate issues to the right people, and hold 3PL

partners accountable. You won’t wait to be told there’s a problem — you’ll be the one who finds

it first.



T H E H A N D O F F L I N E

Supply Chain owns: Procurement ? Warehouse ? 3PL Pick-Up

This role owns: 3PL Dispatch ? Carrier Tracking ? Confirmed Delivery to Customer

Customer Service owns: Post-Delivery ? Customer Contact ? Resolution



Key Responsibilities

Fulfillment Monitoring

• Monitor all open orders across fulfillment channels in real time, with a focus on tracking

movement, carrier handoffs, and delivery confirmation.

• Proactively identify stalled orders (no tracking movement for 48+ hours) before they

generate CS tickets or customer complaints.

• Flag fulfillment patterns — bulk carrier failures, pick-up delays, wrong items shipped —

to Supply Chain leadership and CS within defined SLAs.

• Serve as the primary point of contact between 3PL vendors and internal teams for all

post-dispatch, pre-delivery order issues.

Escalation Ownership

• Own the escalation process when fulfillment failures are identified: document the issue,

quantify the impact (order count, date range, failure type), and route to the right

stakeholder with urgency.

• Provide CS with accurate, timely status updates on affected orders so agents can

confidently respond to customers still waiting.

• Maintain a log of escalations and resolutions to support pattern recognition and process

improvement.

3PL & Carrier Accountability

• Track 3PL performance against defined SLAs and flag recurring failures (pick-up delays,

scan gaps, misroutes) to Supply Chain leadership.

• Lead chargeback recovery efforts with 3PL partners for fulfillment failures within your

scope, documenting affected orders and coordinating with Supply Chain for formal

claims.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

• Act as the daily operational bridge between Supply Chain and Customer Service —

ensuring both teams have what they need without crossing into each other’s lanes.

• Participate in weekly L10 standups and report on key fulfillment metrics with real

numbers — not “metrics stable” summaries.

• Support OMS monitoring and translate data into actionable insight for leadership.



Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

• Stalled Order Rate — Target: < 1> 95% of orders picked up within agreed carrier

window. Upstream failure prevention.

• Order Issue Resolution Rate — Target: > 90% of flagged orders resolved or rerouted

within 48 hours. Customer experience and chargeback reduction.

• Chargeback Recovery Rate — Target: 100% of eligible 3PL failures documented and

submitted. Direct cost recovery tied to operations.



Success Benchmarks (
Days)

First 30 Days — Learn the Landscape

• Gain full access to and working knowledge of OMS and all active fulfillment channels

across brands.

• Complete a full audit of current open orders and identify any existing stalled shipments

or unresolved 3PL issues.

• Build working relationships with Supply Chain lead and CS Team Lead to understand

current pain points and communication gaps.

• Shadow existing escalation workflows and document where handoffs currently break

down.



First 60 Days — Own the Process

• Fully own daily order monitoring across all active fulfillment channels with zero reliance

on CS or SC to surface issues.

• Establish the escalation protocol with CS and SC — both teams know exactly when and

how to loop you in.

• Begin tracking and presenting stalled order rate, resolution time, and 3PL SLA

compliance at weekly L10.

• Identify the first chargeback recovery opportunity and initiate documentation with Supply

Chain.



First 90 Days — Drive the Number

• Stalled order rate consistently below 1% with a documented system for daily monitoring

and early detection.

• Fulfillment escalations no longer land in CS’s lap — agents are responding to customers

with accurate info already provided.

• At least one completed chargeback recovery cycle submitted with full documentation.

• Leadership has full visibility into fulfillment health through weekly reports — no P&L

surprises.



Required Skills & Qualifications

Must-Have

• E-commerce operations experience — DTC or marketplace, not brick and mortar.

• Amazon platform fluency — Seller Central, FBA/FBM, or MCF at working proficiency.

• Order management proficiency — comfortable navigating OMS platforms and

interpreting tracking data at volume.

• Google Sheets — strong working proficiency for tracking, reporting, and flagging data

independently.

• 3PL coordination experience — you’ve held logistics partners accountable, not just

monitored them.

• Clear written communication — you escalate concisely and without fluff.



Nice-to-Have

• TikTok Shop fulfillment experience — a strong plus as this is our fastest-growing

channel.

• Experience in health, wellness, or pet product categories.

• Familiarity with chargeback recovery processes with 3PL or carrier partners.

• Startup or high-growth DTC background — you build systems, you don’t just follow

them.



Experience

• 2+ years in an e-commerce operations, fulfillment coordination, or supply chain support

role.

• Direct hands-on experience with 3PL providers and carrier networks — not just visibility

tools.

• No specific degree required. Relevant supply chain or logistics certifications are a plus.



Who Should Apply?

? Apply if you…

? Spot a stalled order in a dashboard before anyone asks you to look.

? Thrive in high-volume, fast-moving environments where data tells you what needs fixing

today.

? Can escalate a 3PL failure at 7am and have a clear update to CS by 10am without being

chased.

? Understand the stakes of fulfillment failures on DTC revenue — because you’ve lived

them.

? Build your own tracking systems rather than waiting for one to be handed to you.



???? Do not apply if you…

? Come from brick and mortar or traditional retail with no e-commerce experience.

? Need daily direction on what to monitor or fix — this role requires self-driven execution.

? Are more comfortable escalating problems than owning their resolution.

? Prefer slow-moving corporate environments with clearly defined processes for every

scenario.



Why Join Pearl West?

• Real ownership — your stalled order rate shows up on the P&L. You’ll see the direct

impact of your work every month.

• High-growth environment — six brands, multiple channels, active acquisitions. You’ll

never be bored.

• A team that moves — no approval chains for common-sense decisions. If you see a

problem, you own it.

• Build the role — this position is new. You’ll define how it runs and grow with it as we

scale.



Compensation & Benefits

• Salary Range: $
depending on experience

• Work Arrangement: Fully remote

• Health & Wellness Benefits: Per Pearl West standard benefits package • Growth Path: Natural track toward Fulfillment Manager or SC Operations Lead as Pearl West acquires more brands.



How to Apply

- Submit your application through this link:

title: Senior Graphic Designer with Modern Skill with AI Prompting Master Skils and proven experience with Amazon
description:
Since 2013, Prodigy Media has built more than brands — we've created an Amazon-native empire. With 12 years of proven success and 28+ product lines under our belt, we don't play small. Our mission? Launch culturally resonant, design-led, emotionally sticky products that dominate Amazon and beyond.



Now, we're leveling up — and we want a design assassin who’s mastered AI workflows, commands Midjourney like a wizard, and turns prompts into visual gold.



WHO WE WANT



This is not your average graphic design role. We're looking for a hybrid Creative Director / Prompt Engineer / Graphic Design savant who can:



Think like a marketer. Design like an artist. Prompt like an AI god.



You’ll own the visual storytelling for our brands, using both traditional tools and bleeding-edge AI systems to pump out Amazon-optimized assets that convert.



WHAT YOU’LL DO



Craft Ultra-High-Converting Amazon Ads – static, lifestyle, 
-tier, and scroll-stopping. Own A+ Content Design – full module execution with story arcs that build obsession. Design Labels & Packaging – pixel-perfect, regulatory-ready, and unforgettable on the shelf. Prompt, Edit & Produce AI-Generated Visuals – using tools like Midjourney, DALL•E, Runway, Spline, Veo3, Sora, and others. Run 3D Product Renders – via Blender, KeyShot, or AI-powered 3D tools. Develop Scroll-Hook Videos – using After Effects, Premiere, Runway AI, etc.



Systematize Brand Visuals Across 30+ Products – while keeping it all aligned, emotional, and conversion-focused.



WHO YOU ARE



5+ years in design, at least 2+ years in Amazon/e-commerce brand creative

Fluent in Adobe Creative Suite, Figma, Blender, After Effects

Pro-level Midjourney / AI prompt engineer – this is non-negotiable

Obsessed with Amazon visual psychology, packaging psychology, and design that sells

Able to juggle 10 brands and still hit deadlines

Operate with a Creative Director mindset — not just execution, but strategy

Bonus: Experience creating creative systems, art direction templates, or leading a team



MUST-HAVES

Portfolio with Amazon ads, A+ pages, labels, 3D renders, videos, and AI-gen examples

Real-world use of AI tools integrated into your workflow

Available Mon-Fri 7:00am–3:30pm PST

title: Full-Time ISO Operations Coordinator — Sales Channel Support (Graveyard Shift, Permanent)
description:
We're hiring a full-time ISO Operations Coordinator to support our US-based 1099 sub-agent sales network. Smart access control company (hardware + SaaS). The pipeline is real: 24,000-unit retail order confirmed, national retail meeting at the end of the month, FCC-certified hardware. Sub-agents are selling, and you keep the back-end clean.



This is not a sales role. You don't dial. You support the people who do.



COMPENSATION

- Base: $700 USD per month flat 

- Bonus: $50 per sub-agent who passes 60-day certification

- Performance bonus: $100/month if all reps stay current on activity logs and commission reconciliation

- Top performer Month 3+ earnings: ~$1,400/month USD

- Pass 30-day trial ? permanent seat + raise + expanded bonus



SCHEDULE

- Full-time, 40 hours/week, Monday–Friday

- 7:00 AM–3:00 PM MST OR 9:00 AM–5:00 PM MST (you pick one and commit)

- That's 10:00 PM–6:00 AM PHT or 12:00 AM–8:00 AM PHT — confirm you can do graveyard shift before applying



WHAT YOU DO EVERY DAY

- Onboard new sub-agents: send PandaDoc contracts, track signatures, file W-9s

- Manage 60-day Training & Cert pipeline — track progress, schedule cert checkpoints, flag at-risk reps

- Audit GoHighLevel daily: rep pipelines, activity logs, flag reps below minimums

- Weekly commission reconciliation — pull GHL deal data, cross-check fulfillment, prep payout sheet

- Maintain Notion knowledge base — SOPs, scripts, ICP docs, objection handlers

- EOD report submitted unprompted by 5 PM MST

- Friday 1:1 with your manager



REQUIRED

- 2+ years of real B2B sales operations or sales support, not just admin VA work

- Fluent professional English, written and spoken (Slack with US reps daily)

- Worked with US clients before

- GoHighLevel experience required — pipelines, smart lists, custom fields, automations

- PandaDoc or DocuSign experience

- Notion or similar knowledge base tool

- Excel/Google Sheets — pivot tables, vlookups, basic formulas

- Quiet home office, professional headset

- Reliable internet: primary ISP + backup (Globe, PLDT, Smart, mobile hotspot — name them)



THE TRIAL

30 days paid. Day 15: KPI alignment with your manager. Day 30 permanent seat decision. Pass the trial and the role is permanent with a raise and an expanded bonus.



HOW TO APPLY (incomplete applications are ignored)

1. Send your resume

2. GHL proof: screenshot of a pipeline, smart list, or report you built (redact sensitive info)

3. Excel proof: screenshot of a tracker, dashboard, or commission sheet you built

5. Highest number of contractors or reps you've supported simultaneously (be honest  we verify)

6. ID-verified status on OnlineJobs.ph (badge required)

7. Speedtest.net screenshot of your home internet

8. Confirm: "I can work the full graveyard shift, MST hours, starting within 5 business days."



PAYMENT

Direct via PayPal. Permanent seat post-Day 30 includes a raise and an expanded bonus.



Generic applications and copy-paste cover letters will be ignored.

title: Experienced Associate Brand Manager to support 12+ Amazon brands for growth, execution, drive performance and operation
description:
Role Overview



We're hiring 2 Associate Brand Manager who will work directly under the Senior Brand Manager to execute day-to-day brand operations. This is a hands-on role focused on the “legwork” that drives performance: optimizing listings, managing keyword research, coordinating creative projects, and pulling actionable data.



If the Senior Brand Manager sets the playbook, the Associate Brand Manager runs the plays — ensuring everything gets executed on time, correctly, and at a high level.



Key Responsibilities

1. Listing Management & Optimization

Create, update, and fully optimize Amazon product listings (titles, bullets, descriptions, images, videos, A+ content).

Conduct keyword research and competitor analysis to improve rankings and visibility.

Ensure all listings remain Rufus and Cosmos compliant with Amazon’s latest requirements.

Test content variations (A/B testing) to improve CTR and conversions.



2. Data Research & Analysis

Pull performance data from Amazon Seller Central and external tools (Data Dive, Helium 10, Jungle Scout).

Identify trends, keyword shifts, and competitor activity.

Provide actionable recommendations to the Senior Brand Manager to improve conversions, CTR, and sales velocity.



3. Cross-Team Collaboration

Work closely with the graphic design team to request, coordinate, and deliver images, infographics, and A+ creatives.

Partner with the marketing team to support product launches, promotions, and review-generation initiatives.

Ensure all projects are aligned with deadlines, strategy, and brand guidelines.



4. Conversion-Focused Execution

Continuously update and refine listings to improve conversion rates and customer engagement.

Maintain Amazon Brand Stores, A+ content, and ad creatives for consistency and performance.

Assist in preparing assets for influencer campaigns and external marketing pushes.



5. Project & Compliance Support

Own the administrative “heavy lifting” of flat files, bulk uploads, and catalog troubleshooting.

Flag compliance issues, unauthorized sellers, or listing errors for quick resolution.

Support market expansion efforts by preparing research and draft listings for new product opportunities.



Skills & Qualifications



2+ years experience in Amazon FBA operations, brand management, or digital marketing.

Proficient in Amazon Seller Central, flat files, listing creation, and troubleshooting.

Strong skills in SEO, keyword research, and listing optimization.

Data-driven mindset; confident in Google Sheets and reporting.

Strong communication skills to work cross-functionally with design, marketing, and operations teams.

Highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of managing multiple deadlines.



What Success Looks Like

Every listing across assigned brands is fully optimized, compliant, and performing.

Senior Brand Manager receives clear, accurate reports and recommendations.

Creative assets are delivered on time because you coordinated smoothly with design.



Product launches happen seamlessly with marketing and review strategies in place.

Conversions and rankings consistently improve due to your execution. If you feel you are the right fit, please respond to the job with the title " The Best Supporting Branding Manager Out There!"



Please respond directly to :

----------



Kind Regards,

Tam Ma

Co Founder

Prodigy Media

Qualifications

-Familiarity with analytics, reporting, and digital marketing support such as
 and blog content

-Able to work independently, move quickly, manage priorities, and meet deadlines in a remote environment

-Organized, dependable, proactive, and comfortable owning projects from idea to final delivery

Ideal Candidate

title: Motion Design Video Editor Needed ASAP

title: Junior Virtual Process Engineer / Operations Support
description:
About Us:

We are a US-based property management company operating with a deep commitment to Continuous Improvement and Lean principles, modeled after the Toyota Production System. We use specific training curriculums (including Moodle and Gemba concepts) to guide our operations. We are looking for a dedicated tea
mber to become an expert in our systems and help us improve our daily processes.



The Role:

This is a Junior-level virtual position ideal for recent graduates in Engineering, IT, or Computer Science. We know you may not have work experience yet; we are looking for someone with a logical mind who is ready to learn.



You will begin with a 90-Day Process Engineering Training Program. During this time, you will receive a training allowance while you learn our systems and methods.



Once trained, you will help us solve operational problems, research solutions, and organize our data. This is a hands-on role for a highly organized individual who enjoys structure and troubleshooting.



Key Responsibilities:



Learning & Research (First 90 Days): Your main focus initially is to complete our Moodle-based training curriculum. You will need to research concepts and apply them to our specific business case to show you understand how we operate.

Database Administration: Manage our NocoDB operational dashboard (similar to Airtable or SmartSuite). You will use your technical background to keep our data organized and efficient.

Problem Solving: Help us identify bottlenecks in our work. You will research why a problem is happening and propose logical solutions to the management team.

Process Documentation: Work with our US-based team to document how they currently do tasks, then help create Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) to make those tasks easier and faster.

Performance Tracking: Help us track Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and prepare simple reports that show how our improvements are working.

Qualifications:



Education: Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Industrial, Civil, etc.), Computer Science, or Information Technology is preferred.

Experience: Fresh graduates are encouraged to apply. We are happy to train the right person.

Technical Skills: Comfortable learning new software quickly. Experience with databases or low-code platforms (like Airtable/NocoDB) is a plus.

Mindset: You should be curious, detailed-oriented, and enjoy fixing things.

Communication: Clear written English skills are essential for creating documentation and communicating with the team.

Discipline: As this is a remote role, you must be self-motivated and able to focus on your training modules from home.

Why Apply?



Training: Gain experience with Lean/Toyota Production Syste
thodologies—valuable skills for any future career in operations or engineering.

Growth: This is a career-track role. If you progress well, there is room to grow within the company.

Impact: You will be helping us solve real problems for a fast-paced real estate company.



Apply Here:

title: Influencer & Social Media Specialist | DTC E-commerece
description:
We’re looking for an Influencer & Social Specialist to take ownership of our ambassador and creator program while supporting social engagement and customer experience.

This is a hybrid role centered on influencer and ambassador program management, combined with hands-on community engagement and customer support. You’ll be responsible for building relationships with creators while actively engaging with our audience across Instagram and TikTok.

This is a high-ownership role for someone who brings both execution and strategic thinking. You won’t just follow tasks—you’ll contribute ideas, refine processes, and help shape how this function evolves as we grow.

This role is especially well suited to someone with experience in DTC wellness, herbal medicine, beauty, health, or Traditional Chinese Medicine.



Responsibilities



Source and build your own lists of influencers, creators, and ambassadors within the wellness space

Lead outreach with a quality-first approach, focusing on alignment, conversion, and long-term value

Manage the ambassador pipeline end-to-end: sourcing, outreach, onboarding, and ongoing relationship management

Track outreach performance and continuously optimize for conversion into active ambassadors

Build authentic, long-term relationships with creators and identify strong brand partners

Propose and develop campaign, collaboration, and activation ideas—not just execute existing ones

Own and improve the influencer program over time

Monitor and engage with the community across Instagram and TikTok

Respond to comments, DMs, and interactions in a timely, thoughtful, and brand-aligned way

Practice active social listening to identify trends, sentiment, and opportunities

Write natural, non-generic responses that build trust and connection

Stay up to date with platform trends and apply them in a way that fits the brand

Support customer inquiries across
, chat, and social channels

Handle tickets clearly and empathetically while following internal processes

Use CX tools and systems to ensure timely, high-quality support

Share insights from customer conversations to inform community and influencer strategy





Requirements



2+ years of experience in influencer marketing, ambassador programs, social media engagement, and/or community management

Experience working with DTC e-commerce brands (wellness preferred; beauty or health also relevant)

Hands-on experience sourcing influencers and managing creator relationships end-to-end

Experience engaging directly on Instagram and TikTok (comments, DMs, community interactions)

Experience in customer support across
, chat, or social channels

Strong understanding of how outreach performance ties to conversion and program outcomes

Nice to have:



Experience in wellness, herbal medicine, hormone health, or Traditional Chinese Medicine

Background in beauty or health brands with DTC experience

Familiarity with CX and e-commerce workflows in a fast-paced environment





Who you are



Proactive and self-directed

Strong sense of ownership—you don’t wait to be told what to do next

Analytical, with a focus on quality and conversion over volume

Collaborative and comfortable working within a team

Clear, polished English communication skills

Organized and detail-oriented

Warm, thoughtful, and empathetic in customer and community interactions

Adaptable and excited to grow with an evolving role





What this role is not

• Not just influencer outreach

 This isn’t about sending mass messages. It’s about building meaningful relationships that drive results.

• Not a community-only role

 Community engagement is important, but you’ll also own influencer sourcing, outreach, onboarding, and program management.

• Not a content creation role

 You won’t be responsible for designing posts or acting as the face of the brand. The focus is on creator partnerships and engagement.

• Not for entry-level candidates

 This role requires hands-on experience in influencer marketing and social engagement.

title: Finance Recruiter
description:
We’re looking for an experienced Finance Recruiter with a strong track record of sourcing and hiring finance and accounting professionals for U.S.-based companies.

This role requires deep familiarity with finance functions across different business stages, from startups to established organizations. You will own the full recruitment lifecycle—from intake and sourcing to interview coordination and offer closure—while partnering closely with internal stakeholders to ensure business-aligned, high-quality hires.

If you thrive in a fast-paced environment and excel at identifying top finance talent, this role is for you.

Core Responsibilities



Source, screen, and recruit finance and accounting professionals for U.S.-based companies.

Manage the full-cycle recruiting process, including job intake, candidate outreach, interviews, and offer negotiation.

Build and maintain strong finance talent pipelines using LinkedIn Recruiter, Workable, and other sourcing platforms.

Develop tailored sourcing strategies for finance roles such as Accounting, FP&A, Corporate Finance, Payroll, Tax, and Audit.

Conduct structured interviews to assess technical expertise, analytical skills, and cultural fit.

Partner closely with hiring managers and cross-functional teams to understand financial team needs, reporting structures, and hiring priorities.

Collaborate with the People & Compliance team to ensure smooth onboarding and documentation.

Track recruiting metrics and provide regular pipeline and progress updates.

Stay current on finance hiring trends, compensation benchmarks, and market demand.

Requirements

Must-Haves for the Role



3–5 years of experience recruiting finance and accounting roles for U.S.-based companies.

Strong understanding of finance positions such as Accountant, Senior Accountant, Financial Analyst, FP&A Analyst, Controller, Bookkeeper, Payroll Specialist, Tax Associate, or Finance Manager.

Proven ability to manage multiple requisitions simultaneously.

Hands-on experience with ATS platforms (Workable preferred) and sourcing tools like LinkedIn Recruiter.

Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

Fluency in English required.

Full-time availability aligned with U.S. business hours (PST overlap required).

Nice-to-Haves for the Role



Experience recruiting remote finance talent in LATAM or the Philippines.

Familiarity with independent contractor hiring models and compliance considerations.

Experience recruiting for startups or high-growth companies.

Exposure to finance roles supporting SaaS, e-commerce, or professional services companies.

title: Apparel Production Specialist
description:
We are seeking a highly execution-driven Apparel Production Specialist to own and manage end-to-end production across our Global DTC and Amazon channels. This role is responsible for the full production lifecycle—from purchase order issuance through final delivery—ensuring speed, accuracy, compliance, and operational excellence across a global vendor network.

This is a highly cross-functional role requiring strong vendor management, systems expertise, and operational discipline in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

Responsibilities

Production Ownership



Manage the complete production lifecycle (PO issuance ? bulk production ? final delivery).

Maintain and execute detailed production calendars across DTC and Amazon programs.

Track WIP, monitor milestones, and proactively resolve delays or risks.

Translate sales forecasts into clear, executable production plans.

Ensure accuracy in costing, production timelines, and delivery commitments.

Global Vendor and Outsourcing Management



Manage and develop international vendor partnerships across Asia, Africa, and multi-region sourcing networks.

Oversee capacity planning, pricing alignment, and vendor performance.

Ensure compliance with PP, TOP, and final inspection standards.

Proactively mitigate production delays, quality risks, and compliance issues.

Amazon & DTC Channel Execution



Manage production requirements specific to Amazon Marketplace.

Ensure compliance with Amazon packaging, labeling, bundling, and hard-pack requirements.

Support complex packaging programs, including bundle configurations.

Coordinate with fulfillment partners and 3PLs to meet DTC launch calendars and delivery deadlines.

Cross-Functional Collaboration



Partner closely with Product Development, Sales, QA, Logistics, Planning, and 3PL teams.

Provide accurate WIP reporting and production updates to internal stakeholders.

Utilize Slack and project management tools (e.g., Asana) for transparent communication.

Ensure operational alignment from development handoff through final shipment.

Requirements



5–8 years of apparel production experience in high-volume environments.

Strong experience managing end-to-end production.

Hands-on experience with NetSuite, Centric PLM, and Anvyl (Sage).

Advanced Excel skills (costing, WIP reporting, production calendars).

Proven ability to manage compressed timelines and evolving forecasts.

Experience managing multi-region vendor networks.

Deep knowledge of production compliance and inspection processes.

Demonstrated ability to navigate complex international production environments.

Experience supporting Amazon Marketplace production requirements.

Strong understanding of Amazon packaging compliance standards.

Familiarity with DTC fulfillment models and launch cadence planning.
