title: Customer Service Team Lead | Full-Time
description:
We are a fast-growing e-commerce company in the health and wellness space, managing multiple successful online brands. We are hiring a Customer Service Team Lead to work closely with the CEO in overseeing daily customer support operations, leading a small team, and ensuring consistently high-quality customer experience.

The ideal candidate has strong written communication skills, excellent problem-solving abilities, high empathy, and proven leadership experience. Experience in outreach or cold
 communication is also required, as the role may involve supporting customer and wholesale communications. This role begins as a CEO support position focused on
-based sales support. Once you are fully settled and consistently performing well in this role, it will transition into a customer service team lead position.





Key Responsibilities:

Manage and execute
-based outreach and sales support activities

Support daily administrative and operational tasks as needed

Lead, coach, and support Customer Service agents to ensure performance and accountability

Conduct daily check-ins, team huddles, and performance reviews

Handle escalated customer issues and ensure proper resolution. 

Assign tasks, manage schedules, and ensure adequate coverage

Monitor key performance metrics (response time, CSAT, productivity) and drive improvements

Provide feedback, coaching, and corrective action when needed

Oversee daily
 and chat support operations 

Help document, improve, and refine SOPs, workflows, and customer service processes

Handle complex or escalated customer concerns. Ensure timely and proper resolution of high-priority cases

Act as the bridge between customers and higher management when needed

Ensure consistency in customer service quality across the team

Support hiring, onboarding, and continuous upskilling of tea
mbers.

Conduct ongoing skill development sessions

Ensure the team is updated on product, policy, and system changes

Review customer interactions to ensure compliance with service standards and quality metrics.

Work closely with other departments (Ops, Sales, Tech) to resolve issues and improve customer experience

Assist the CEO with Cafely wholesale inquiries, including
-based sales support tasks



Requirements

3–5 years of customer service experience 

1–3 years of leadership or team lead experience in a customer support environment

Strong English communication skills (written & verbal) 

Hands-on experience with CRM and customer support tools (e.g., Zendesk, Gorgias, Freshdesk, or similar)

Strong problem-solving, decision-making, and leadership skills

Ability to work independently in a remote setup with minimal supervision

Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities



Schedule (Strict)

9:00 AM – 5:00 PM PST (12:00 AM – 8:00 AM PH Time) 

Full 8-hour graveyard shift required 

Must be fully available for US hours 



Nice to Have

E-commerce experience 

Experience with AI tools or automation tools 

Experience in scaling support teams 

Familiarity with SOP creation and process improvement

Experience supporting both customer service and basic sales/wholesale inquiries

Exposure to AI tools or automation for customer support





What We Offer

Full-time remote role 

Training provided 

Growth opportunity into higher leadership roles



HOW TO APPLY:



Please submit your cover letter (must include the keyword “I love coffee”) to confirm that you have read the entire job post.

Also include:

CV/Resume

Portfolio (optional)



Then answer the following:



1. How many years of experience do you have in customer service, and how many years were in a team lead or supervisory role? Please briefly describe your key responsibilities. 

2. Do you have experience handling both customer support and outbound outreach? Please briefly summarize your roles in each. 

3. What tools, CRM platforms, or systems have you used to manage customer communication or outreach at scale?





Only complete applications will be considered. Applicants must be willing to go through a multi-step hiring process, including tests and interviews. If you advance, you will receive an
 invitation for the next steps.

title: Graphic Design VA
description:
Job Title: Part-Time Graphic Design VA for Long-Term Personal Brand Growth (Faith-Forward)

Hours: 15 to 20 hours per week

Rate: $400 to $600/month (negotiable for the right fit, with room to grow)

Type: Part-time, long-term (3+ year relationship expected)

Schedule: Weekly deliverables, flexible hours, available for weekly check-ins during US Eastern Time business hours



About Me and the Role:

Hello! I'm Kamia, a speaker, author, and coach in the United States. I'm building a luxury, editorial-style personal brand on Instagram for high-responsibility women. My brand pillars are Faith, Family, and Freedom, and they guide everything I create. My content speaks to women who are overextended in every area of their lives and are looking for permission to steward their capacity well instead of constantly pushing for more. My work is faith-forward, which means it often includes scripture, faith themes, and Christian values alongside topics like boundaries, burnout, and capacity.

I'm looking for a dedicated, long-term graphic design VA to help me build a content engine that drives visibility, grows my audience, and attracts my ideal clients.



The Goal of This Role:

This is not about posting pretty pictures. This is about using consistent, high-quality content to:



Grow my Instagram following from 2,300 to 5,000+ within 6 months

Increase engagement (saves, shares, and DM conversations) from women who match my ideal client profile

Build a content library and reusable template system that makes our production faster and more sustainable over time



Your design work directly contributes to my business revenue. The better my content performs, the more clients I attract, the more we both grow.



What You'll Do:

Daily:



Check the shared content board for new copy and creative direction



Weekly:



Design and deliver a batch of Instagram graphics based on provided copy

Schedule approved content through Canva Content Planner

Send a short progress update (what's done, what's coming, any questions)



Monthly:



Deliver 20 to 25 Instagram pieces: 6 to 8 carousels (5 to 7 slides each), 8 to 10 single-post graphics or reel covers

Design 4 to 6 YouTube thumbnails for long-form videos

Build 3 to 5 new editable Canva templates I can reuse (Instagram and YouTube)

Design occasional
 Live and Instagram Live promo graphics as needed

Share a short report on engagement trends and what's working



Month 1 Setup Sprint (One-Time Project):



YouTube channel banner

 page cover

LinkedIn banner

Instagram Highlight covers (up to 8)





What I'll Provide:



All copy and content direction (you do not need to write)

Brand colors (hex codes) and fonts (Playfair Display and Montserrat)

Canva Pro account access

Personal photo library from past shoots

Stock photo sites to pull from (CreateHER Stock, Nappy.co, Canva library)

Clear feedback and weekly review time





Key Performance Indicators (KPIs):



100% on-time delivery of approved monthly content

Month-over-month Instagram follower growth

Increase in content engagement (saves, shares, DM conversations)

Consistent quality and brand alignment across all pieces

Monthly growth of the reusable template library





Growth Opportunities:

I'm not looking for a task doer. I'm looking for a creative partner who wants to grow with my brand. As revenue scales and the brand expands, real opportunities include:



Expanded role into full social media management

Additional compensation for video editing 
 Live to Instagram Reels

title: Vibe Coding Specialist (Base44) – Owner-Facing Role
description:

This is a hands-on, collaborative role focused on rapidly turning ideas into functional tools that support business operations.



---

* Work closely with the company owner to translate ideas into working applications using **Base44**

* Build, test, and iterate internal tools, dashboards, and lightweight apps

* Ensure applications are user-friendly and aligned with business needs

* Provide input on system design, structure, and scalability

---

title: Experienced GoHighLevel EXPERT VA for Backend Automation & Ongoing Support
description:
I’m looking for a highly experienced VA who knows GoHighLevel (GHL) inside out to support backend setup, automations, data migration, and ongoing optimisation for my business: builds done for you GHL builds (also on white labelled jobs).



This is a support role for someone who takes initiative, communicates well, and is confident building and managing systems for online coaches.You need to be able to communicate with Australian businesses. If you send me anything with a Z or em dash, I will know you are not used to communicating with this industry.



Tasks include:

-CRM and funnel builds in GoHighLevel

-Automating client journeys (forms, calendars, pipelines)


 and SMS follow-up automation

-Technical set up (tell me about some skills that set you apart in this space)

-Creating dashboards and tagging systems

-Importing, cleaning, and mapping data from other platforms

-Ongoing support, maintenance, and optimisation



You must:

-Have strong GHL experience with proof of past work

-Be proactive and reliable, with attention to detail

-Be available for communication on normal Australian working hours

-Be comfortable working independently

-Be willing to sign an NDA

-Be open to a paid trial task



Bonus if you:

-Have experience working with online coaches

-Can share client testimonials or references

-Offer monthly retainer or support packages



To be considered, you must answer every question below clearly and honestly. If you skip any, you will not be shortlisted.



1.How many GoHighLevel builds have you completed end to end?

Not partial edits. I want full setups that you managed start to finish.



2.What is the biggest GHL challenge you’ve solved on your own?

Explain the situation and what you did to fix it.



3.Have you worked with online coaches making $10K to $30K per month?

Yes or no. If yes, describe the work you handled.



4.Can you provide direct links, screenshots or screen recordings of GHL work you have done?

Generic portfolios will not be accepted.



5.What are your non-negotiable work habits or standards?

I want to know how you operate under pressure or with deadlines.



6.How do you handle unclear briefs or vague requests?

This role requires initiative. I need to know how you work with limited instruction.



7.What is your real hourly rate and what kind of support packages do you offer?



8.Can you start with a paid trial task this week?

Just say yes or no.



9.What is your time zone and weekday availability in AEST?



10.Have you read this full post and are you ready to sign an NDA before starting?

Reply with: “Yes, and I’m ready.”



11.What is one area of improvement you see for GoHighLevel as a platform?

I want honest feedback, not sales talk.



12What is your favourite feature in GHL right now and why?



I am also open to agencies that can offer other services like social media management, content creation, youtube editing for all inclusive support.

title: Amazon PPC & Profitability Strategist (Senior Level)
description:
Who We Are

Southshore Fine Linens is a fast-growing home textiles brand with over 3,500 SKUs across sheets, quilts, duvet sets, comforters, and seasonal collections.

We operate at scale across Amazon, Shopify, and wholesale—and we take profitability seriously.

We are not looking for someone to “run ads.”

We are looking for someone who understands how to deploy capital inside Amazon.

The Mission of This Role

Drive profitable growth across our Amazon catalog.

Not just more sales.

Not just lower ACOS.

Growth that improves contribution margin and long-term ranking.

What You Will Own

:moneybag: 

PPC Strategy & Profitability



Own total ad spend and performance across the catalog

Manage and improve TACOS, ACOS, and contribution margin

Define break-even ACOS targets per SKU

Allocate budget based on profitability and growth potential

Identify where to scale aggressively vs reduce spend

:jigsaw: 

Campaign Structure & Architecture



Build and maintain structured campaigns:

Discovery (Auto / Broad)

Ranking (Exact / High-intent)

Branded defense

Competitor targeting



Segment campaigns by intent and performance stage

Continuously restructure accounts for efficiency and scale

:chart_with_upwards_trend: 

Scaling & Optimization



Scale winning campaigns while maintaining profitability

Optimize bids, placements, and budgets based on data—not guesswork

Identify high-performing search terms and isolate them

Reduce wasted spend and eliminate non-performing keywords

Improve CTR and CVR through collaboration with listing team

:mag: 

Data Analysis & Decision Making



Analyze:

TACOS trends

Conversion rates

CPC inflation

Placement performance

Search term profitability



Make decisions based on profit, not vanity metrics

Provide clear insights—not just reports

:hammer_and_wrench: 

Execution Excellence



Manage campaigns inside Amazon Advertising Console

Use bulk sheets and structured workflows for efficiency

Implement bid adjustments and placement strategies

Maintain clean, scalable campaign structures

:handshake: 

Cross-Team Collaboration



Work closely with Listing Growth Specialists to:

Align keyword strategy

Improve conversion rates

Support ranking initiatives



Provide input on:

Product launches

Pricing strategy

Promotional planning



:bar_chart: 

Reporting & Accountability

Provide weekly reporting on:



TACOS and profitability trends

Campaign performance breakdown

Budget allocation decisions

Scaling opportunities

Risks and issues

Non-Negotiable Requirements



4–6+ years hands-on Amazon PPC experience

Proven experience managing large-scale catalogs (500+ SKUs minimum)

Strong understanding of:

TACOS vs ACOS

Contribution margin

Break-even ACOS

Campaign structure strategy



Deep experience inside Amazon Advertising Console

Strong analytical and decision-making skills

Ability to work PST hours full-time

What We Are NOT Looking For



Task-based PPC VAs

Candidates focused only on ACOS

People who “optimize bids” without understanding profitability

Anyone without a clear campaign structure strategy

Who You Are

You:



Think in terms of profit, not spend

Understand when to scale vs protect margin

Can diagnose performance issues quickly

Make decisions using data, not opinions

Take ownership of results—not just tasks

What Success Looks Like (First 60–90 Days)



Clear understanding of account structure and performance

Campaigns restructured for efficiency and scalability

Wasteful spend reduced

High-performing keywords identified and scaled

Improved TACOS and overall profitability

Strong alignment with listing and SEO strategy

How to Apply (IMPORTANT)



Send:



Your resume

A short case study of a PPC account you managed:

What was the problem?

What actions did you take?

What was the impact on profitability?



Answer this question:

A product sells for $40 with a landed cost of $18.

Current ACOS is 30%.

What would you do?

Applications without this will not be considered.



Compensation & Growth



Competitive salary based on experience

Performance-based incentives tied to profitability improvements

Opportunity to manage and scale a large, complex catalog

Long-term growth into senior leadership role

title: Remote Architectural Drafter/Drafting Assistant
description:
About Us

We design and build steel-framed decks and outdoor living spaces for residential clients across the U.S. Our team moves quickly, values craftsmanship, and depends on clear, reliable communication. We’re looking for a remote Drafting Assistant who can take rough sketches, photos, or engineer notes and turn them into accurate, permit-ready drawings with minimal oversight.



Role Overview

This is a remote, full-time drafting position working directly with our lead designer in Chief Architect. You’ll be responsible for producing clear, buildable drawings and supporting a fast-paced design and construction workflow.



Key Responsibilities:

-Produce permit-ready drawings (site plans, layouts, elevations, sections, details, title blocks)

-Create fast concept drawings for client presentations and sales layouts

-Build 3D models and camera views for clients, plan review, and fabrication

-Translate structural and engineering notes into buildable drawings (steel and wood)

-Implement redlines from PDFs, photos, or sketches with accurate revision tracking

-Generate material takeoffs and square-foot calculations



Required Qualifications:

-Strong experience with U.S. residential construction, especially decks and outdoor structures

-Fully proficient in Chief Architect (this is our primary and required software)

-Ability to work fully remote and independently within our drafting system

-Solid understanding of wood framing, composite decking, steel connections, and deck code requirements

-Ability to produce clear construction details (including cross-sections) that crews can build from

-Strong organizational skills with version control and revision workflows

-Clear and reliable English communication (verbal and written)

-Stable, high-quality internet connection for consistent collaboration

-Proficiency in U.S. customary units (feet and inches)



Deliverables:

-PDFs (permit-ready and presentation)

-Editable Chief Architect files

-DWG/DXF exports

-3D views and renderings



Ideal Candidate:

-Detail-oriented and solutions-focused

-Comfortable working with minimal supervision

-Communicates clearly and consistently with tea
mbers, engineers, and fabricators

-Able to meet fast turnaround times without sacrificing accuracy

-Proactively ensures files are organized and accessible for team collaboration



Important Considerations:

-You must be able to clearly explain how you share working files and collaborate on editable drawings (e.g., cloud sharing, file handoff process, version control)

-Your portfolio should demonstrate relevant project experience, including at least one deck/outdoor structure and a cross-section drawing



Apply here:

title: Bookkeeper
description:
Join Our Remote Accounting Team!

We are a fast-growing, boutique outsourced accounting firm supporting high-growth e-commerce and service-based businesses. While our name remains confidential for now, we’ve been behind the scenes helping some of the most exciting DTC brands scale with confidence through expert bookkeeping, financial systems, and strategic insights.



We are a remote-first company with a collaborative, client-first culture — and we’re looking for exceptional talent to grow with us.



We’re seeking a detail-oriented, tech-savvy E-commerce Bookkeeper to manage financial records, process transactions, and generate critical reports to support client decision-making. You’ll play a key role in ensuring books are accurate, clean, and up-to-date — and bring clarity to fast-moving businesses.



What You’ll Be Doing

As our Bookkeeper, you’ll support multiple clients with day-to-day accounting operations, reconciling accounts, and managing both AP and AR functions.



Your Core Responsibilities:

- Record and categorize financial transactions using QuickBooks Online

- Reconcile bank, credit card, and merchant accounts

- Prepare and analyze financial reports (P&L, Balance Sheets, Cash Flow Statements)

- Perform invoicing, collections, and accounts receivable tracking

- Process bills and manage accounts payable workflows

- Process payroll and ensure timely tax remittances

- Track and maintain accurate inventory accounting records

- Communicate with clients via dedicated channels (Slack,
, Meetings)

- Support ad hoc bookkeeping and administrative needs as required



What You’ll Bring to the Table

**Experience & Skills:

- 3+ years of experience as a Bookkeeper

- Advanced QuickBooks Online skills are required

- Strong grasp of inventory tracking and accounting best practices

- Experience reconciling financial data across multiple platforms

- Proficient with Google Workspace, Excel, and Word

- Experience working with e-commerce or service-based businesses is a plus

**Communication & Work Environment:

- Advanced English skills (written and spoken – C1 level preferred)

- Strong attention to detail and time management

- Clear communicator who can work independently and manage deadlines

- Tech-savvy and comfortable with remote collaboration tools



What’s In It For You?

- 100% Remote Work - Work from anywhere in the Philippines. No more long commutes or stressful traffic. Spend more quality time with your loved ones and thrive in the comfort of your own home.

- Long-Term Career Growth – With opportunities to scale into higher leadership roles.

Competitive Pay – $10/hr + benefits as an independent contractor

- Supportive Culture – Work alongside a leadership team that values transparency, innovation, and your ideas.

- Positive, People-First Vibes – We believe in balance, fun, and thriving together.

- Annual Performance Reviews – Your growth gets recognized and rewarded.



Ready to Keep the Books Clean & Clients Confident?

If you’re obsessed with accuracy, enjoy structure, and love bringing order to financial chaos — this role is for you. Join our growing team of professionals who value excellence, clarity, and results.

title: Senior Virtual Assistant Guest Operations | Executive Support | Marketing & Brand
description:
Xenia Stays is a luxury short-term rental management company delivering 5-star experiences to guests and premium service to high-net-worth property owners. We operate across Airbnb, VRBO, Guesty for Pros, and our direct booking website — and we hold ourselves to the highest standards of hospitality, communication, and professionalism.

We are fast-growing and need a right-hand person who can run with us — not just follow a checklist.

"We don't want task-takers. We want strategic thinkers who anticipate needs, solve problems before they escalate, and take ownership as if this is their own business."



THE ROLE

This is not a standard VA position. You will operate as a hybrid Senior Guest Operations Manager, Executive Assistant to our CEO, and Luxury Brand Manager. You will own your responsibilities end-to-end and be expected to identify gaps, propose solutions, and execute without being micromanaged.



Guest Operations & Communications

Prepare operational reports, identify patterns, and suggest improvements to leadership

Own and manage all company projects in 
 — creating boards, tracking progress, assigning tasks, and following up with tea
mbers

Manage vendor relationships and owner communications with a luxury-brand tone



Executive Assistant to CEO



Support the CEO with scheduling,
 management, meeting preparation, and follow-ups

Draft high-level communications on behalf of the CEO to partners, owners, and vendors

Conduct research, compile reports, and prepare briefings as needed

Act as a trusted right-hand who can represent the CEO's standards and voice in all communications



Marketing & Brand Growth



Manage and grow our luxury STR social media accounts (Instagram,
, linkedin)

Create or coordinate content that positions our properties and brand as premium, aspirational experiences

Engage authentically with followers, respond to comments and DMs, and build community

Identify trends and implement strategies to grow reach, engagement, and direct bookings

Support
 marketing campaigns and direct booking funnel efforts



REQUIREMENTS

Non-Negotiables:



Minimum 3 years of VA, operations, or hospitality management experience

Demonstrated expertise with 
 — board creation, automations, and project tracking

Exceptional written English — guest-facing and executive-level communications

Experience managing Airbnb and/or VRBO listings and guest communications

A track record of working independently without daily oversight

Reliable internet, professional home office setup, available during US business hours (EST or PST)

Work graveyard shift 7am-3pm PST



Strong Pluses:



Experience with luxury hospitality or high-end STR companies

Social media content creation and brand management (Instagram,
, TikTok)

Background in executive assistant or C-suite support roles

Familiarity with breezeway, pricelaps, or similar STR tools

Experience creating or managing
 marketing campaigns



WHO YOU ARE



You see problems before they happen and act without being told

You write with clarity, warmth, and professionalism — your messages make people feel taken care of

You treat every guest issue like it's happening at your own home

You own your work entirely — no excuses, no hand-holding

You are obsessively organized and nothing falls through the cracks on your watch

You are as comfortable managing a 
 sprint board as crafting a luxury Instagram caption

You thrive in fast-paced environments and adapt quickly to new tools and processes



WHAT WE OFFER



Competitive pay: $3.75–$4.50/hour depending on experience (30 hrs/week)

A stable, long-term role with real opportunities for growth into senior leadership

A direct working relationship with our CEO — your work matters and will be recognized

A collaborative, high-standards culture that values excellence and initiative

Access to premium tools, SOPs, and a well-organized operational foundation



HOW TO APPLY

To be considered, please submit ALL of the following. Applications missing any item will not be reviewed.



An updated resume with relevant experience highlighted

A short video introduction (2–3 minutes): Tell us about your Guesty and 
 experience, about your experience as an executive assistant and marketing expert, and give one example of a time you solved a problem without being asked to

Your written response to this prompt: "A guest messages at 11pm saying the hot tub isn't working and threatens to leave a 1-star review. Walk me through exactly what you do."

title: Outbound Cold Calls — B2B Appointment Setter Salary + Commission
description:
Outbound Cold Calls — B2B Appointment Setter Salary + Commission | Remote | 3+ Years Experience Required



Why strong setters join us



We built a role where your results are directly reflected in what you earn — with a guaranteed base, clear performance bonuses, and a team that genuinely invests in your growth.



If you're experienced, consistent, and take your craft seriously, this is a place where you can build something real.



01 — Uncapped earning potential Your compensation grows with your performance. The structure is transparent, the targets are achievable, and there's no ceiling on what you can earn each month.



02 — Experienced leadership that develops you You'll work closely with a team that knows this business inside and out. We share what's working, coach on what isn't, and help you sharpen your skills continuously — tone, objection handling, conversation flow.



03 — A supportive, high-performing team We hold a high standard here, and we support each other in reaching it. You'll be working alongside people who are serious about their work and their growth.



Compensation — paid every week via Wise



Base salary: $1,200 USD/month — guaranteed

Per qualified appointment: $50 USD — every one, on top of base

Status bonus: 10–20% of closed deals the following month

The status bonus — how it works: Set 12+ qualified appointments in a month and you unlock a percentage of every deal that closes the following month — regardless of when you originally set those leads. Hit 12 = 10%, 16 = 15%, 20 = 20%. The more consistent you are, the more it compounds. At Bronze level that's an extra ~$375/mo. At Gold level an extra $1,250/mo — all on top of your base and per-appointment pay.



Working hours



9:00am – 5:30pm Eastern Time (ET) That is 10:00pm – 6:30am Philippine Time (PHT).



This is a night shift role. We are fully transparent about that. There is a strong and experienced community of Filipino professionals who work US hours — if that's you, and you're comfortable with it, the compensation reflects the commitment.



Onboarding



Your first 10 days are structured onboarding. We walk you through everything — the script, the CRM, the process — until you feel fully confident. To join the core team, the target during onboarding is a minimum of 3 qualified appointments. This is a realistic and achievable bar, and we'll be coaching you throughout to help you get there.



What you'll actually do



Make 150–200 outbound cold calls per day to businesses that may need commercial janitorial services

Identify genuine needs and book qualified appointments for businesses ready to receive a cleaning quote

Deliver a proven script naturally and conversationally — not mechanically

Keep your pipeline and call logs updated accurately in our CRM each day

Receive ongoing coaching and access to the strategies that have proven to work

Who we're looking for



2+ years in B2B sales or outbound cold calling

A natural, professional phone presence — you sound like a consultant, not a caller

Ability to internalize a script and deliver it as a real conversation

Stable setup: 80+ Mbps internet, a reliable computer, and a quality headset

Comfortable navigating a CRM independently — logging calls, updating records, managing your pipeline

What you're walking into



A company with a clear system, transparent compensation, and leadership that takes your development seriously. You'll know exactly what's expected, exactly what you'll earn, and exactly how to grow. We want people who stay, improve, and build with us long term.



Ready to apply? If this sounds like the right fit, we'd love to hear from you. Please apply with a brief introduction and any relevant experience you'd like to share.

title: Multi-Site Project Manager
description:
Multi-Site Project Manager (Customer Success Expert) is responsible for understanding the customer’s expectations, and for advocating for the successful oversite of order management, production of orders, execution of operations within Service Level Agreements (SLA), inventory management of raw materials, reporting and billing. Project Managers are key representatives in 1Vision’s objective to deliver “Best in Class” service. Meeting customers’ needs through quality production and on time delivery, providing responsive internal and external communication through a “Can Do” approach will create an environment where customers will continue to view 1Vision as their partner.



Key Responsibilities:

1. Project Management:

-Monitor and manage the end-to-end production and fulfillment process, ensuring that all orders are completed accurately and within program SLA.

-Monitor and manage all Manufacture On Demand (MOD) and Print On Demand (POD) production orders to be produced within program SLA.

-Escalate any production, or order issues to operations management that arise on a timely basis to maintain program SLA.

-Ensure project deadlines and quality standards are maintained throughout the process.

-Ensure all orders are entered correctly via API or manually if needed.

-Facilitate weekly invoicing to client specific requirements.



2.Team Coordination and Leadership:

-Work closely with cross-functional internal teams (e.g., data prep, scheduling, print operations, mailing and logistics) across all producing plants to ensure all objectives are met.

-Attend daily production meetings to monitor production of all orders. 



3.Order & Inventory Management:

-Direct consolidation of orders (e.g. “ganging” of jobs) on a daily basis by producing plant location.

-Oversee inventory levels of raw materials (e.g. envelopes and stamps, etc.) to ensure the availability of materials for timely production of orders.

-Manage order tracking and ensure the accuracy of data in job costing systems.

-Address and resolve any discrepancies in completion/delivery of orders within SLA metrics.



4.Customer Satisfaction:

-Address customer inquiries and issues related to production processes.

-Facilitate reporting and accurate data information being presented to the customer.

Required Qualifications:



The ideal candidate will possess strong project management skills, knowledge of printing and mailing processes, attention to detail, and the ability to work cross-functionally with various teams to ensure the efficient delivery of products and services to customers.

•Strong knowledge of printing, mailing, and logistics processes.

•Proven ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines.

•Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.

•Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.

•Proficiency in project management software systems.

•Ability to lead cross-functional teams and work collaboratively across departments.



Preferred Qualifications:

•Experience with Monarch job cost system

•Experience with MS Word, Outlook and Excel.



Key Competencies:

•Offset and Digital Printing

•Mailing Processes

•Leadership

•Project Management

•Communication

•Problem Solving

•Analytical Thinking

•Time Management

•Process Improvement

•Collaboration

title: VA Philippines Land & Builder Research
description:
About the Role



We are seeking a highly organized and resourceful **Virtual Assistant with real estate research experience** to support land acquisition and builder sourcing for planned residential development projects in the Philippines, with an initial focus on **Pampanga and Antipolo (Rizal)**.



The VA will identify **development-ready land opportunities**, research **qualified builders/contractors**, and help organize market intelligence to support build-and-sell housing projects.



## Target Areas for Research



### Pampanga (Priority Areas)



Focus on land and builder opportunities in and around:



* Angeles City

* Clark / Mabalacat

* San Fernando



Priority subdivisions/communities to research:



* MetroGate Angeles

* Marquee Place vicinity

* Clarkview area

* Greenfields

* Celadon Village area



### Antipolo / Rizal (Priority Areas)



Focus on land and builder opportunities in and around:



* Mayamot

* San Isidro

* Cupang

* San Luis

* Mambugan



Priority subdivisions/communities to research:



* Mission Hills

* Maia Alta

* Eastland Heights

* Sun Valley Estates



---



## Responsibilities



### Land Sourcing / Acquisition Research



Research and present:



* Vacant lots and raw land suitable for subdivision or small residential development

* Infill lots inside or near target subdivisions

* Assemblage opportunities (multiple adjoining lots)

* Foreclosure, motivated seller, and off-market opportunities (where possible)

* Zoning/land-use compatibility (residential use)

* Approximate land pricing (per sqm)

* Lot size, title status (if available), and road access

* Utilities availability (water, electric, drainage, internet)

* Flood risk or topography concerns (especially hillside lots)



### Builder / Contractor Research



Identify and pre-screen:



* Licensed general contractors

* Design-build firms

* Small-to-mid-size residential builders

* Subdivision infrastructure contractors (roads/drainage)

* Architects/engineers that support permitting



For each builder provide:



* Company name

* Contact person

* Website / social links

* Portfolio / sample projects

* Estimated construction cost per sqm (if available)

* Reputation indicators (reviews, references, years in business)

* Whether they handle permits or turnkey delivery



### Deliverables



Create and maintain organized trackers in Google Sheets or Excel with:



**Land Pipeline Tracker**

Include:



* Location

* Price per sqm

* Total asking price

* Lot size

* Seller/Broker contact

* Development notes

* Estimated fit for project (High / Medium / Low)



**Builder Comparison Matrix**

title: SENIOR AUTOMATIONS, ZAPIER AND GHL SPECIALIST  — REMOTE | US HOURS
description:
READ THIS FIRST.



This is a hands-on Automations building role, but we are looking for SENIOR-LEVEL skill and judgment. Here is what that means:



You will be the person inside GoHighLevel, Zapier, Make and API's every single day. Building workflows, troubleshooting errors, and maintaining client automations. You will be doing the work. But we need someone who does the work at a senior level. That means:



- You catch your own mistakes before anyone else does

- You do not need to be audited or micromanaged

- You investigate and figure things out before asking for help

- When something breaks, you find the root cause (you do not just guess)

- You follow instructions carefully and execute them accurately



If you need step-by-step hand-holding, this is not your role. We have been burned by that already.



IMPORTANT: WORKING HOURS



This role requires you to work 9 AM to 5 PM EST, Monday through Friday. In Philippine Time, that is roughly 9 PM to 5 AM the next day. You will be working overnight shifts. If you cannot commit to this schedule consistently, please do not apply.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

You are the technical builder and troubleshooter for our agency's automation systems. Your day looks like this:



- Build GoHighLevel workflows, pipelines, custom values, and snapshots for new clients as directed by the Account Managers

- Build multi-step Zaps with paths, filters, formatters, and webhooks

- Run a system health check every morning. Review GHL and Zapier error logs and resolve breaks immediately

- Triage incoming Slack messages and support tickets, resolving 95% of issues within 24 hours

- Investigate and fix broken automations (find the actual root cause, not just the surface symptom)

- Maintain accurate Client Automation Playbooks and cheatsheets so anyone on our team can pick up where you left off

- Build and configure landing pages inside GoHighLevel

- Log your build and support hours in ClickUp in real-time (90%+ accuracy)

- Manage and deliver our monthly client newsletter

- QA-test every workflow you build before handing it off



WHAT YOU MUST ALREADY KNOW 

These are non-negotiable. We will test for them in the interview process.



- GOHIGHLEVEL: You have built complex workflows, automations, pipelines, custom values, snapshots, and landing pages inside GHL. You understand sub-account configurations. You can debug a broken workflow without help.

- ZAPIER: You have built multi-step Zaps with paths, filters, formatters, and webhooks. You know when to use Zapier versus GHL native automations versus both together.

- INTEGRATIONS: You have connected platforms like Housecall Pro, ServiceTitan, Jobber or other similar CRM tools through both Zapier and GHL. You understand webhooks, basic API concepts, and JSON.

- TROUBLESHOOTING: When a workflow breaks, you check the logs, isolate the failure, fix it, document what happened, and put a safeguard in place so it does not happen again. You do not panic and you do not guess.

- ATTENTION TO DETAIL: You catch your own mistakes. You test before you hand off. You read the requirements carefully and build to spec.

- DOCUMENTATION: You write clear playbooks and cheatsheets that someone else can actually follow.

- ENGLISH: You can read, write, and speak English clearly. You will be on video calls with our team and occasionally with clients.



WHAT YOU'LL BE MEASURED ON

- Technical error rate of less than 2% per month

- 100?curacy in workflow documentation

- 95% of support issues resolved on the first attempt within 24 hours

- 90?curacy in real-time time tracking inside ClickUp

- Workflows pass QA on the first attempt



WHO YOU ARE

- 4+ years of hands-on experience with GoHighLevel and Zapier (not just "I've used them," you've built complex systems in them)

- Available to work 9 AM to 5 PM EST, Monday through Friday (overnight shift in the Philippines)

- Clear written and spoken English

- Detail-obsessed. You catch the bugs other people miss

- Self-directed. You investigate and figure things out before asking for help

- Calm and methodical when things break

- Comfortable on camera for tea
etings and occasional client calls

- Reliable. You show up, you do the work, you do not need to be reminded twice

- You think outside the box. You know what can go wrong before it does and you present it to the team before mistakes or issues happen



WHO YOU ARE NOT

- Someone who has only built basic Zaps or is "still learning GoHighLevel"

- An automation user, not a builder

- Someone who needs every task explained step by step

- Someone whose work has to be audited regularly

- Unable or unwilling to work overnight US hours





HOW TO APPLY

- If you have gotten this far you are on the right track! To apply please follow this link 
 and submit your application and resume

title: Book Keeper
description:
******MUST SUBMIT A RESUME AND VOICE RECORDING TO BE CONSIDERED FOR THE ROLE************





Position Overview:



We are looking for a dependable and detail-oriented Bookkeeper to join our team and take ownership of our day-to-day financial operations. This role is essential to maintaining the accuracy, organization, and integrity of our financial records. The ideal candidate is someone who enjoys working with numbers, has a strong sense of accountability, and can manage multiple financial tasks with precision and consistency.



As a Bookkeeper, you will play a key role in supporting the financial health of the organization by ensuring that all transactions are recorded properly and that accounts are kept up to date. You will work closely with management to provide clear visibility into financial performance and help ensure that all financial processes run smoothly.



Key Responsibilities:



In this role, you will be responsible for maintaining and updating financial records, including tracking daily transactions such as income, expenses, and payments. You will handle accounts payable and receivable, ensuring invoices are issued accurately and payments are collected and recorded in a timely manner. Regular reconciliation of bank and credit card statements will be part of your routine to ensure all records align correctly.



You will also assist with payroll processing, maintain organized financial documentation, and prepare periodic financial reports that provide insight into the company’s financial position. Attention to detail is critical, as you will be expected to identify discrepancies and resolve them efficiently. Additionally, you will help ensure compliance with applicable financial regulations and internal procedures.



Qualifications and Experience:



The successful candidate will have prior experience in bookkeeping or a similar accounting role, along with a solid understanding of basic accounting principles. Familiarity with accounting software such as QuickBooks, Xero, or similar platforms is important, as well as comfort working with spreadsheets and digital financial tools.



Strong organizational skills and the ability to prioritize tasks are essential in this role, as you will be handling multiple responsibilities at once. You should also demonstrate a high level of discretion and professionalism when working with sensitive financial information.



While not required, a degree in accounting, finance, or a related field, as well as any bookkeeping certifications, would be considered an asset.



Work Environment and Compensation:



This position offers the opportunity to work in a collaborative and supportive environment where accuracy and reliability are highly valued. Compensation will be based on experience and qualifications, and additional benefits may be available depending on employment type.



How to Apply:



If you are a motivated and detail-focused professional looking to contribute to a growing organization, we encourage you to apply. Please submit your resume along with a brief cover letter describing your relevant experience and interest in the role to [Insert
 or application link].

title: Project Coordinator (PC)
description:
Project Coordinator (PC) – PLEASE READ THOROUGHLY



The Project Coordinator owns the full lifecycle of a project from quote through closeout. This role is responsible for building and structuring quotes, managing orders, coordinating across all departments, and ensuring projects are accurate, executable, and profitable.



The PC serves as the central point of coordination between Account Managers (AMs), Project Managers (PMs), Operations, Vendors, and Accounting, while maintaining full ownership of project data and flow within E-Manage.



This is a high-accountability role requiring strong attention to detail, ownership, and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.



Core Responsibilities



- Quote Development and Project Setup

- Build and structure complete project quotes in E-Manage from Action Items provided by AMs

- Review scope, finishes, SIF files, vendor quotes, and all supporting documentation

- Upload and organize Bill of Materials (BOM) and ensure all parts are entered correctly

- Verify vendor pricing manually and ensure accuracy (pricing is not system-reliant)

- Structure quotes by phase, including product descriptions, finishes, quantities, and images as needed

- Add all required services to quotes (shipping, design, project management, installation, tariffs)

- Manage revisions and maintain clean, organized quote versions

- Format, review, and submit finalized quotes to AM for client presentation



PROJECT INTAKE AND GATEKEEPING

1. Serve as the control point before any project is processed or converted

2. Ensure all required information is complete and accurate prior to moving forward:

    - Deposit received or approved override

    - Payment terms aligned across quote and client PO

    - Customer PO reviewed when applicable

    - Correct company, billing, and accounting contacts entered

     - Accurate ship-to and install-to addresses

    - Building requirements and delivery conditions documented

3. Hold, return, or delay any project that does not meet required standards



ORDER MANAGEMENT AND PROCUREMENT

- Convert approved quotes into orders once requirements are met

- Generate and manage purchase orders (POs)

- Submit POs to AM for approval prior to vendor release

- Send approved POs to vendors and maintain vendor communication

- Ensure all POs are accurate and aligned with the project scope



VENDOR COORDINATION AND ORDER TRACKING

1. Track all orders from placement through delivery

2. Monitor vendor acknowledgements and compare against POs for accuracy:

   - Pricing

   - Quantities

   - Addresses

   - Lead times

3. Enter acknowledgement details, including ship dates and tracking

4. Communicate vendor updates, delays, and changes to AMs, PMs, and Ops

5. Maintain consistent follow-up with vendors to ensure timely execution





LOGISTICS AND PROJECT FLOW COORDINATION

- Coordinate with PMs and Operations on order status and project readiness

- Communicate shipping timelines, tracking, and delivery updates

- Coordinate deliveries, will-calls, and scheduling requirements

- Provide all necessary information for Ops to schedule receiving and installation

- Ensure all product is received and accounted for prior to installation

- Flag risks, delays, or missing items early and drive resolution



ACCOUNTING COORDINATION AND COST CONTROL

- Enter and track vendor invoices against POs and acknowledgements

- Submit Action Items to Accounting to trigger vendor payments

- Communicate discrepancies, due dates, and special payment requirements

- Ensure alignment between what was sold, ordered, and invoiced

- Support client invoicing requests and ensure accurate billing information



PROJECT EXECUTION AND CLOSEOUT

1.  Maintain accurate and up-to-date project records throughout the lifecycle

2. Manage issue resolution including:

   - Vendor issues

   - Shipment discrepancies

   - Returns and replacements

   - Warranty claims

3. Coordinate punch list items and final project completion

4. Ensure all documentation is complete and accurate at closeout

5. Support final invoicing and confirm project readiness for closure



SYSTEM OWNERSHIP (E-MANAGE)

- Maintain full accuracy of all project data within E-Manage

- Ensure all quotes, orders, documents, and updates are properly entered and organized

- Track project status, documentation, and communication within the system

- Identify process gaps and inefficiencies and provide recommendations for improvement





Collaboration Structure



WITH ACCOUNT MANAGERS (AM'S)

- Receive Action Items to initiate work

- Build and structure quotes based on provided scope and documentation

- Submit quotes and POs to AM for review and approval

- Provide updates on pricing, lead times, and order status



WITH PROJECT MANAGERS (PM'S) AND OPERATIONS

- Communicate vendor acknowledgements, ship dates, and tracking

- Notify teams of delays or changes impacting schedule

- Provide details for deliveries, will-calls, and installation planning

- Ensure all product is received and ready prior to install



WITH ACCOUNTING

- Enter vendor invoices and supporting documentation

- Submit Action Items to trigger vendor payments

- Communicate payment timelines, discrepancies, and special requirements

-Support invoicing and project financial reconciliation



KEY TRAITS

- Highly detail-oriented and organized

- Strong communicator across multiple teams

- Proactive and solution-focused

- Able to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced environment

- Comfortable navigating ambiguity and problem-solving in real time

- Takes ownership and accountability for project accuracy and execution

- Understands that precision at the quote stage protects the business downstream

 

PLEASE INCLUDE AND SEND ME YOUR RESUME AND YOUR PREVIOUS WORK SAMPLE

title: Virtual Front Desk Agent - Hospitality Background
description:
The Opportunity: Bring Your 5-Star Service Home

Are you a hospitality professional from a hotel or fine dining background who wants a stable, long-term remote career?



TruPorch Homes (PillowPM) is looking for you. We manage premium short-term rental properties that offer the comfort of a home with the service of a boutique hotel. We are not a call center. We do not use robotic scripts. We are looking for true hospitality professionals who have "Service DNA" and know how to make guests feel welcomed, heard, and cared for.



What You Will Do (The "Virtual Front Desk")

You will be the voice and face of our brand. You will handle the guest journey from inquiry to checkout, acting as a "Digital Concierge."



Guest Communication: Handle inbound calls (via VoIP) and messages (Airbnb, Vrbo, 
) with warmth, perfect grammar, and professional etiquette.

Problem Solving: Swiftly resolve guest issues (e.g., smart lock trouble, Wi-Fi issues, heating/cooling) by troubleshooting or dispatching our ground team.

Coordination: Act as the central hub between guests and our cleaners/maintenance staff to ensure properties are perfect for every arrival.

Revenue & Reviews: Assist with reservation modifications and follow up with guests to secure 5-star reviews.

Who We Are Looking For



Hospitality Veterans: You have 2+ years of experience in Guest Services, Front Desk, or Concierge roles at reputable hotels (e.g., Shangri-La, Marriott, Accor) or high-end dining establishments.

Communication Mastery: You possess a neutral English accent for phone calls and near-native written English for messaging. You know how to de-escalate upset guests with empathy, not scripts.

Tech-Savvy: You can navigate multiple browser tabs, learn new software (PMS, Slack, Airtable) quickly, and type fast.

Reliability: You are disciplined enough to work from home without supervision.

Technical Requirements (Non-Negotiable)



Internet: Reliable Fiber connection (Minimum 25 Mbps download/upload) with a backup connection plan.

Hardware: A working PC/Laptop capable of handling VoIP calls and a noise-canceling headset.

Environment: A dedicated, quiet home office space. (No roosters, barking dogs, or background noise during shift).

Schedule



This is a US Hours / Graveyard Shift role (e.g., EST or PST hours). You must be willing to work nights and weekends/holidays on a rotating schedule, as hospitality never sleeps.

What We Offer



100% Remote: Work from the comfort of your home.

Stable Career: We are looking for long-term tea
mbers, not temporary freelancers.

Supportive Team: We provide training and treat our remote team with the same respect as our local team.



Apply Here:

title: Executive Assistant, Investment Operations
description:
About Ridgewood Investments



Ridgewood Investments is a premier investment advisory and tax strategy firm serving high-net-worth individuals, families, and business owners. We help clients build, protect, and preserve wealth through tailored investment solutions, disciplined research, and strategic tax planning. As our practice grows, we’re adding a highly organized executive assistant to support our advisory team and keep our client operations running smoothly.



The Role



We’re hiring an Executive Assistant to support our advisors across client coordination, back office operations, and day-to-day workflow management. You’ll be the connective tissue that keeps our practice moving… managing inboxes and calendars, shepherding client documents/reports and onboarding, maintaining our CRM and filing systems, and making sure nothing falls through the cracks.



This is not a tax preparation role, and it is not an analyst role. We’re looking for someone who thrives on organization, communication, and follow-through… and who takes genuine pride in being the person the team can rely on.

This position starts at 20 hours per week and can transition to full-time based on performance and contribution.

What You’ll Do



Executive & Advisor Support

- Manage advisor calendars, schedule client meetings, and coordinate follow-ups

- Monitor and triage inboxes… flagging priorities, drafting responses, and keeping communications on track

- Prepare meeting materials, agendas, and post-meeting summaries

- Track action items across the team and surface what needs advisor attention

- Coordinate travel, events, and occasional client-facing logistics



Client Coordination & Back Office

- Serve as a first point of contact for clients on document collection, onboarding, and ongoing requests

- Manage client workflows end-to-end: intake forms, IDs, prior tax returns, and account paperwork

- Maintain secure, organized digital filing systems (Google Drive and Dropbox Sign)

- Keep Redtail CRM, Asana and project management tools consistently up to date

- Run e-signature workflows and track documents through to completion



Operations Support

- Support portfolio operations tasks in Orion (reporting, basic reconciliation)

- Help prepare and distribute client portfolio reports and performance summaries

- Assist with account maintenance requests and custodian communications

- Support the team with research document organization, meeting prep materials, and internal knowledge management



What We’re Looking For



Required

- 3+ years as an executive assistant, virtual assistant, or operations coordinator… ideally supporting a professional services firm (RIA, law firm, CPA practice, or similar)

- Exceptional organizational skills… able to juggle multiple client workflows, calendars, and deadlines without dropping details

- Excellent written and verbal English communication… you’ll be client-facing in
 and chat

- Strong proficiency with Google Workspace, Dropbox/Dropbox Sign, and at least one CRM

- Professionalism and discretion handling sensitive financial and personal information

- Self-directed and proactive… comfortable owning tasks and following through without heavy oversight

- Reliable overlap with U.S. business hours (minimum 4 hours daily overlap with 9am–5pm Pacific) and responsive during the U.S. workweek



Preferred

- Prior experience supporting RIAs, CFPs, CPAs, tax planners, wealth advisors or financial advisors

- Familiarity with Redtail CRM

- Exposure to Orion or other portfolio management platforms

- Working knowledge of U.S. tax and investment documents (1031, K-1s, 1099s, W-2s)

- Comfort with e-signature workflows and basic compliance best practices

- Comfortable with Excel or Google Sheets for light data entry, tracking, and reporting (formulas, pivots)

- Interest in finance and investing… a curiosity about what the advisors do, even if you’re not the analyst



Position Details

- Type: Remote, part-time (~20 hrs/week) with a clear path to full-time based on contribution and quality

- Schedule: Flexible, with required daily overlap during U.S. business hours (minimum 4 hours between 9am–5pm Pacific); must meet deadlines and respond promptly during the workweek

- Growth: Strong performers can expand into deeper operations, client service, or team lead roles over time as the firm continues to scale



How to Apply



Please submit your information into our hiring portal: 

Include the word “Orange” in the appropriate field and follow the post-submission instructions.

title: Real Estate Cold Caller — Commercial Property Owners (USA)
description:
I am a commercial real estate broker in Atlanta, GA specializing in retail shopping center investment sales. I need a dedicated, full-time cold caller to call through a list of approximately 11,000 shopping center and retail property owners across the United States. I have been in investment sales throughout the US for over 10 years and have an extensive network of qualified investors that buy commercial real estate. The main task is to identify shopping centers for this group to purchase. 



YOUR ONLY JOB IS TO MAKE PHONE CALLS. This is NOT a sales job. You are simply asking owners one question and passing along to me if they are interested in selling their property now or at some point in the future. 



YOUR DAILY TASKS:

- Call 60-80 property owners per day from my list

- Read my short script asking if they would consider an offer to purchase their property from one of my qualified investors

- If interested — capture their
 address and flag them as a warm lead for me

- Log every call result in a simple Google Sheet (called, no answer, not interested, interested)

- Send me a daily summary of warm leads found



WHAT I PROVIDE:

- Full property owner contact list (name, property name, phone number)

- Word-for-word cold calling script — you do not need to improvise

- Simple Google Sheet call log template

- Training call before you start

- Ongoing feedback and support



REQUIREMENTS:

- Excellent spoken English — clear, professional, and confident on the phone (this is the #1 requirement)

- Previous cold calling experience required (real estate experience a big plus)

- Reliable internet, headset, and quiet work environment

- Available during US business hours (Eastern or Central Time)

- Honest and consistent — this is long-term ongoing work



PAY:

- $600-$800/month base (full time, 40 hrs/week)

- Long-term position for the right person



TO APPLY — you must do all three or your application will not be considered:

1. Tell me your cold calling experience — what did you call about and how many calls per day?

2. Record a 60-second voice introduction and include the link — this is required, no exceptions

3. Tell me your available hours in US Eastern Time

title: Part-Time Virtual Assistant (Operations, Coordination & Event Support)
description:
Part-Time Virtual Assistant (Operations, Coordination & Event Support)



Hi — I’m looking for an exceptional, detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to support a small, fast-moving business with operations, coordination, and event logistics.



This is a part-time role (10–15 hours/week) with room to grow for the right person.



What this role looks like



You’ll be supporting a mix of admin, coordination, and organizational work, including:



 communication and follow-up

Calendar coordination and scheduling

Researching venues and locations for retreats and events

Reaching out to venues, vendors, organizers, and contacts

Gathering information, options, pricing, and logistics for review

Tracking multiple moving pieces clearly so nothing gets missed

Keeping projects organized and moving forward



You will also help with:



Maintaining a simple master tracker across all projects

Coordinating event support (confirming logistics, following up, organizing details)

Sourcing and vetting options for specific needs (vendors, formatter, etc.)

Light support with organizing applicants and initial screening (final hiring decisions remain with me)



This role requires consistent use of Google Docs, Sheets, and Gmail for organization and communication.



You’ll be given clear direction and outcomes, and will take ownership of moving things forward.



The work may include supporting events and content in the personal development, intuitive, and metaphysical space, so comfort with that environment is important.



Event & logistics support



This role includes support for high-level, detail-sensitive events, including:



Researching venue options

Communicating with venues and vendors

Gathering key details (pricing, availability, food, lodging, logistics)

Keeping event planning organized and on track



How we’ll work together



I’ll provide clear direction and the outcome we’re aiming for. From there, I’m looking for someone who can take ownership of execution — researching, coordinating, following up, organizing information, and bringing back what’s needed for decisions.



We value simple, efficient systems and solutions.



AI should be used for speed and efficiency — not to overgenerate, overcomplicate, or create unnecessary work.



This role requires consistent follow-through and clear, timely communication.



What matters most



You are great with people

You are highly proficient in Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Gmail, Calendar) and skilled at using ChatGPT efficiently without overcomplicating or going down unnecessary rabbit holes

You have fluent written and spoken English

You have strong attention to detail

You can organize and keep track of multiple moving pieces clearly and simply

You follow through and get things done

You communicate clearly and directly

You’re comfortable figuring out how to execute once given direction

You can handle logistics in situations where details, timing, and confidentiality matter

You have a proven track record supporting high-level events and logistics coordination



Details



10–15 hours per week

Mostly flexible hours, with some real-time coordination as needed between 12–3 PM Pacific Time

Pay: $6–$8/hour to start, with performance-based increases over time



To apply



Please start your message with READY, and include:



Your availability in Pacific Time, including whether you’re available for real-time coordination as needed between 12–3 PM PT

Your hourly rate

A brief example (3–5 sentences) of a situation where you were given a task or outcome and had to figure out how to get it done

A brief summary of your experience supporting events and logistics coordination, including the types of events you’ve worked on

A short summary of your background and the types of work you’ve been responsible for



If there’s a fit, I’ll follow up with next steps, including a short paid test task.



Thank you.

title: Video Editor Needed for YouTube Animation Channel (Story-Driven, High Retention)
description:
BoundOfficialTV is an animated animal documentary YouTube channel with over 300,000 subscribers. We create engaging, fast-paced videos that combine storytelling, animation, sound design, and visual effects to keep viewers hooked from start to finish.



We are looking for a skilled video editor who can transform scripts and assets into compelling, high-retention videos that match and improve upon our current style.



Sample video style:





???? 

What You’ll Do

Edit full-length YouTube videos (typically 6–12 minutes)



Combine:

Voiceover narration

Animal illustrations / animations

Sound effects

Background music

Maintain fast pacing and strong viewer retention



Add:

Zooms, motion, and subtle animation to static images

Visual emphasis (highlights, arrows, focus effects)

Scene transitions that keep flow smooth and engaging

Sync visuals tightly with narration for maximum impact

Use sound design to enhance tension, humor, and emotion





Structure videos to:

Hook viewers in the first 5–10 seconds

Maintain engagement throughout

Deliver strong endings



???? 

Key Objective

Your job is not just to “edit” — it is to maximize audience retention and engagement.



This means:

Every cut, zoom, and sound should have a purpose

No dead space or slow sections

Visuals should constantly reinforce what’s being said



???? 

Required Technical Skills

???? Editing Software

Adobe Premiere Pro (preferred) or equivalent



Familiarity with:

After Effects (strong bonus)

Final Cut Pro (acceptable alternative)



?? Core Editing Skills

Timeline editing (cutting, trimming, sequencing)

Pacing control (fast, engaging flow)

Jump cuts and rhythm editing

Scene structuring



????? Motion & Animation

Basic motion animation:

Zooms (push-in / pull-out)

Panning across images

Parallax effects (preferred)

Animating static assets to feel dynamic



???? Audio Editing & Sound Design

Voiceover syncing

Background music selection and layering



Sound effects timing for:

Impact

Humor

Tension

Volume balancing and clean audio mixing



???? Visual Effects & Enhancements

Text overlays (emphasis, labels, reactions)

Highlight effects (circles, arrows, glows)

Color correction/grading

Light effects for dramatic scenes



???? Export & Optimization

Exporting for YouTube (1080p/4K)

File compression without quality loss

Maintaining consistent quality across videos



???? Asset Management

Organizing:

Audio files

Visual assets

Project files

Efficient workflow with large asset libraries



???? 

Creative & Analytical Skills (CRITICAL)

Ability to interpret a script and visualize engaging scenes



Strong sense of:

Timing

Emotion

Storytelling flow



Understanding of YouTube retention dynamics

Ability to identify:

Where viewers might drop off

Where to add visual or audio stimulation



? 

Workflow & Productivity

Ability to deliver 1– 2 videos per week (depending on complexity)

Fast turnaround time

Comfortable working with templates and repeatable formats

Open to feedback and iteration



???? 

What Makes a Strong Candidate

Experience editing YouTube content (especially storytelling channels)

Strong sense of pacing (no slow or boring sections)

Ability to make simple assets feel engaging

Understanding of viral-style editing techniques

Attention to detail (clean cuts, precise timing)



???? 

Pay

Per video, $50/ ~ 10-minute video 

Long-term opportunity for the right candidate



???? 

How to Apply

Watch a few  sample video and understand the pacing and style front he youtube channel



Submit:

2–3 examples of your past work (preferably YouTube content)

Be prepared to complete a short paid trial task

To confirm you’ve read this post, include the word “Potato” in your message. Applications without it will not be considered.



If you have ideas on how to improve pacing, engagement, or retention in our videos, include them in your application.

title: Manager of Accounting
description:
Full-Time Employee (Not a Contractor Role)



Join our team as an Accounting Manager, where you’ll play a key role in leading the day-to-day operations of your team while supporting our clients with accuracy, professionalism, and care. This is a remote, full-time position based in the Philippines, with an initial focus on aligning with your manager's time zone.



About the Role



As a Accounting Manager, you’ll be responsible for ensuring the accuracy and timely completion of all accounting tasks owned by your team. You’ll also provide leadership, mentorship, and support to junior staff to help them reach their fullest potential.



Key Responsibilities



Supervisory Duties:



• Manage and mentor junior staff working on account reconciliation, payroll, accounts payable, accounts receivable, sales tax, and more

• Conduct performance evaluations and provide coaching

• Lead training efforts for new hires and ongoing staff development

• Foster a positive, professional, and collaborative team environment

Client & Team Management:



• Own timely and accurate completion of all tasks for assigned clients

• Coordinate internal work assignments and team priorities

• Manage projects and deadlines using our Project Management platform

• Hold the team accountable for quality and timeliness

• Oversee the preparation of financial statements and client reports

Technical & Process Support:



• Verify, allocate, post, and reconcile general ledger accounts

• Produce error-free accounting reports and analyze financial data

• Identify areas for process improvement and lead implementation

• Provide support for audits and tax matters

• Participate in setting standards and improving workflows

• Develop documentation and strengthen internal controls



What We’re Looking For

• Strong analytical skills and thorough knowledge of accounting principles

• Excellent organizational and leadership abilities

• Experience in managing accounting workflows and team supervision

• Ability to adapt, take initiative, and lead process improvements

• Familiarity with project management and accounting software tools

• Must have U.S. accounting experience



Work Schedule

This is a full-time, remote position for candidates based in the Philippines.



• Initial 90 days: Work hours must align with your manager’s time zone, which includes working some hours during the 10:00 PM – 6:00 AM night shift (Philippine time)

• Post-90 days: Based on performance, you’ll work with your director to set a schedule that includes a 4-hour overlap with your manager’s time zone



Compensation & Benefits

• Competitive salary, dependent on experience (DOE)

• Opportunities for internal promotions and career growth

• 15 days of paid time off (PTO) annually

• Paid Philippine holidays off

• Medical, dental, and vision insurance offered

title: Video Editor
description:
Position: Video Editor (Part-Time ? Full-Time Growth Role)

title: Administrative Assistant
description:
Job Description: Virtual Assistant for Economy Rental Car Company



Position: Virtual Assistant



Location: Remote

Type: Full
-Time (Flexible, specify hours)

Reports to: Business Owner/Manager



Company Overview



We are an economy rental car company catering to gig workers, providing affordable and reliable vehicles to support their work in rideshare and delivery services. Our mission is to empower gig workers with hassle-free rental solutions, enabling them to maximize their earnings. We are seeking a proactive and organized Virtual Assistant to support our operations and enhance our customer experience.



Job Summary



The Virtual Assistant will play a critical role in managing daily administrative and customer-facing tasks to ensure smooth business operations. This position requires strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment. The ideal candidate is tech-savvy, customer-oriented, and experienced in social media engagement.



Key Responsibilities



Data Entry: Accurately input and update customer information, rental records, and payment details in our database or CRM system.



Booking Clients: Manage rental bookings via phone,
, or online platforms, ensuring availability and coordinating schedules.



Rental Agreements: Prepare, review, and send rental agreements to clients, ensuring all required information is complete and accurate.



Customer Support: Answer phone calls and
s promptly, addressing customer inquiries, resolving issues, and providing information about rental policies and pricing.



Social Media Engagement: Create and schedule posts on platforms like Instagram,
, and
 to promote services, share updates, and engage with followers. Respond to comments and messages in a professional and timely manner.



Administrative Support: Assist with scheduling vehicle maintenance, tracking rental statuses, and organizing digital files.



Communication: Maintain clear and professional communication with clients, tea
mbers, and third-party vendors.





Qualifications





Experience: 1-2 years of experience as a virtual assistant, administrative assistant, or in a customer service role. Experience in the automotive or gig economy sector is a plus.





Skills:





Proficiency in data entry and CRM tools (e.g., HubSpot, Zoho, or similar).



Strong written and verbal communication skills.



Familiarity with social media platforms and content scheduling tools (e.g., Hootsuite, Buffer).



Ability to draft and review contracts or agreements with attention to detail.



Basic knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite or Google Sheets.



Technical Requirements: Reliable high-speed internet, computer, and phone for remote work.



Attributes: Highly organized, proactive, and able to prioritize tasks. Comfortable working independently and handling sensitive customer information with confidentiality.



Availability: Flexible to work evenings or weekends as needed to accommodate customer inquiries and booking schedules.





Preferred Qualifications





Experience with rental car operations or customer service in a related industry.



Knowledge of basic marketing strategies for social media growth.



Bilingual skills (e.g., Spanish, other languages relevant to target market) are a plus.





Benefits





Competitive hourly rate or salary (commensurate with experience).



Flexible remote work environment.



Raises with proof of ability to competently execute tasks



Opportunity to grow with a dynamic small business in the gig economy.



Potential for performance-based bonuses.



How to Apply



Please submit your resume, a brief cover letter detailing your relevant experience, and any examples of social media content you’ve created (if applicable) to [insert
 or application link]. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled.



We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity in our team. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, or national origin.
