title: Digital Marketing Specialist (SEO / PPC / Email Marketing)
description:
Job Title:

Junior Digital Marketing Strategist

Location:Remote

Employment Type:Full-Time



About the Role

We are looking for a strategically minded Junior Digital Marketing Strategist to support multi-

channel client accounts with oversight, coordination and foundational technical implementation.

This role is not primarily execution-based.



Instead, we are seeking someone who:

• Understands how PPC, SEO,
 and eCommerce channels connect

• Can audit and review agency work to ensure alignment with client goals

• Has foundational technical knowledge across platforms

• Can implement basic technical marketing updates when required

• Acts as a strategic support layer between clients and channel specialists



This is ideal for someone early in their career who understands digital ecosystems and wants to grow

into a strategic marketing lead role.



Core Responsibilities

1. PPC Strategic Oversight

• Review and audit PPC campaign structures (Google & Meta)

• Ensure campaigns align with client objectives and funnel stages

• Identify gaps in tracking, targeting or messaging

• Support client PPC teams with strategic direction (not daily execution)

• Monitor performance trends and highlight improvement areas

2. SEO Foundations & Technical Implementation

• Understand how organic ranking works (on-page, technical, authority signals)

• Support foundational technical SEO tasks including:

o Sitemap submissions

o Schema markup updates

o Meta title & description optimization

o Basic keyword mapping

• Review agency SEO work for consistency and alignment

• Identify technical SEO gaps and escalate where necessary

3. Website & eCommerce Oversight (Shopify & BigCommerce)

• Review site performance and UX for conversion alignment

• Understand Shopify and BigCommerce theme structures

• Make minor no-code edits where required

• Assist with product organization, tagging and collections

• Identify technical or structural issues affecting marketing performance

• Ensure tracking (GA4, Meta Pixel, Google Ads) is correctly implemented

4. Cross-Channel Alignment & Auditing

• Audit marketing activities across PPC, SEO,
 and paid social

• Ensure messaging is consistent across channels

• Review agency deliverables to ensure they align with client goals

• Identify inefficiencies, overlaps or gaps

• Provide strategic improvement recommendations

5.
 & Funnel Awareness

• Understand how
 integrates with paid and organic channels

• Review automation flows (welcome, nurture, abandoned cart)

• Identify alignment gaps between traffic and retention strategies

6. Technical & No-Code Knowledge

• Familiarity with no-code tools (landing page builders, theme editors)

• Basic coding literacy (HTML/CSS fundamentals)

• Ability to understand theme structure

• Comfortable making small structural or formatting edits

• Ability to communicate effectively with developers when escalation is required

7. Analytics & Reporting

• Review performance data from GA4 and ad platforms

• Identify trends and anomalies

• Ensure reporting reflects true performance

• Highlight discrepancies or tracking issues



Required Experience & Skills

Experience

• 2–4 years in digital marketing

• Experience working across multiple channels

• Experience reviewing or coordinating agency work preferred

Foundational Knowledge Required

• PPC strategy principles (Google & Meta)

• SEO fundamentals (on-page + technical basics)

• Shopify (required)

• BigCommerce (advantageous)

• GA4 basics

• eCommerce conversion principles

• Digital funnel strategy

Technical Skills

• Basic understanding of HTML/CSS

• Comfort with no-code tools and theme editors

• Understanding of schema and sitemap structures

• Tracking implementation awareness (pixels, tags, conversions)



Soft Skills

• Strong analytical thinking

• Strategic mindset

• Detail-oriented

• Comfortable reviewing and challenging work constructively

• Clear communicator

• High initiative and ownership

• Able to manage multiple client environments



For serious applicants, please send the following:

- Updated CV 

- Intro Video

- Whatsapp number for phone screening

- Earliest availability

Build and deploy AI agents for:

Customer communication 
, SMS, chat — compliant messaging)

Internal workflows and automation

Data analysis and reporting (payments, accounts, trends)

Integrate AI tools with existing systems (CRM, spreadsheets, payment systems)

Help design systems that improve response rates and operational efficiency

Continuously optimize AI-driven processes

ChatGPT / OpenAI APIs

Claude, Gemini, or similar

Zapier, Make (Integromat), n8n, or similar

Experience building AI agents, chatbots, or workflow automations

Familiarity with CRMs, APIs, Google Sheets

Bonus: Experience in collections, fintech, or compliance-heavy industries

Important Note (Compliance):



This role involves working within a regulated industry (debt collection).

You do not need prior experience in collections, but you must be comfortable:



Following structured guidelines

Building systems that adhere to rules and compliance standards

Avoiding misleading or aggressive messaging in automation



What We're Looking For:

Someone who can think independently and problem-solve

Strong communicator (clear English)

Able to take a general idea and turn it into a working system

Detail-oriented and organized

Reliable and responsive

Interested in long-term work

You’ll be helping build AI systems in an industry where very few companies are doing this well.

There is significant opportunity to create tools that directly impact revenue and performance.

title: Tourism & Corporate Video Editor - ARK Media
description:
ARK Media is a cinematic video production company based in Kelowna, BC. We produce commercial content for national brands (Samsung, TD, Marriott), tourism boards, and agencies across Canada. Our work is story-driven and held to a high visual standard, branded short films, not corporate videos.



Website: 

Please actually watch our work before applying. We can tell when you haven't.



The Role

We're hiring a full-time remote Video Editor to join our small team long-term. You'll be editing commercial deliverables in DaVinci Resolve: Brand films, tourism campaigns, social cutdowns and behind-the-scenes content. Our Lead Editor and Videographer/Editor will bring you into the workflow and hand off projects as you ramp up.



We are NOT looking for someone who cuts to the beat and drops in transitions. We're looking for an editor with taste, someone who understands pacing, restraint, emotional rhythm, and how to let a shot breathe.



What You'll Do

Edit long-form commercial pieces (60s–3min brand films, tourism videos, case studies)

Edit short-form social content (15s–60s Reels, TikToks, Shorts) from provided footage and briefs

Sync, organize, and manage media in DaVinci Resolve / Blackmagic Cloud

Colour correction and grading

Sound design and music sync: clean mixes, tasteful SFX, no cheese

Light motion graphics (lower thirds, end cards, simple type animation)

Proxy management, project organization, delivery prep (
 uploads, export specs)



Must-Haves

5+ years editing professional commercial or branded content

Expert in DaVinci Resolve Studio - this is where you'll work

Strong sense of pacing and rhythm. Your edits should feel intentional, not templated

Excellent written and spoken English for async communication with our team and clients

Stable high-speed internet capable of large proxy files and cloud workflows

Reliable computer capable of editing 4K footage

A few hours of weekly live overlap with Pacific Time (we're flexible, but calls need to happen)



Nice-to-Haves:

Motion design in After Effects or Fusion

Experience editing for tourism, lifestyle, or outdoor brands

Photography or cinematography background — you understand what we shot and why



How to Apply:

Read this section carefully. We receive a lot of templated applications and throw them out immediately. If any of these pieces are missing or clearly recycled, we won't respond.

1. A selfie video, recorded specifically for this application. Not a reused intro. Tell us who you are, and, this is the part that matters, tell us which piece on our site stood out to you and why. If you reference our work in a way that shows you actually watched it, you're already ahead of 90% of applicants. One to two minutes is plenty.



2. Your reel, plus links to three full project examples. Not sizzle cuts, full pieces you edited, so we can see pacing and structure across a whole story. Tell us what you did on each one (some applicants send team reels and take credit for everything).



3. A cover letter written by you. Do not use ChatGPT or Claude to write this. We use these tools every day and we can tell. A short, honest letter in your own voice beats a polished AI one every time. Tell us why ARK specifically, not "your company."



4. Your resume.



5. Computer specs and internet speed test results.



6. Your availability, hours per week you're looking for, and your timezone / overlap with PT.



Process

Shortlisted applicants will be asked to edit a paid sample video. We choose one editor from the sample round. We read every application personally and will get back to you.

Behance or Vimeo portfolio

Experience with flat motion graphics and icon animation

title: Senior Procurement Specialist | Hiring ASAP
description:
Full-Time | Remote | Philippines | Reports to: Chief Operating Officer



About the role

We are a fast-growing pet products company selling direct-to-consumer and through retail channels. Our products include manufactured and finished goods sourced primarily from China, with additional vendors in the US.



This is a newly created role — the first dedicated procurement hire in our company. The successful candidate will consolidate purchasing currently spread across multiple people, establish supplier relationships, create spend visibility, and build the systems and discipline that will support our growth.



Key responsibilities

• Consolidate all purchasing activity into a single, managed process covering raw materials, components, and finished goods

• Own the full supplier lifecycle: identification, vetting, onboarding, negotiation, and ongoing relationship management

• Manage USD 10–14M in annual spend across the supplier base, primarily in China

• Negotiate pricing, lead times, payment terms, and volume contracts with existing and new suppliers

• Issue and track purchase orders end-to-end, from requisition to receipt

• Align purchasing with inventory targets using our WMS (Finale Inventory) and demand planning inputs

• Build and maintain an Approved Vendor List (AVL) and supplier scorecard

• Identify cost savings opportunities and report on spend vs. budget to leadership

• Coordinate with freight forwarders and manage import logistics for inbound shipments from Asia

• Proactively flag supply chain risks and develop contingency plans



Required qualifications:

• Minimum 5 years of experience in procurement, sourcing, or supply chain management

• Proven experience sourcing from Chinese factories or trading companies

• Strong written and spoken English communication skills

• Demonstrated experience building or significantly improving a purchasing process

• Ability to work independently in a fast-paced, low-structure environment

• Solid understanding of import logistics, Incoterms, and freight coordination

• Prior experience working for a US-based or global company with remote/async work expectations



Preferred qualifications:

• Experience with WMS or inventory management tools (Finale Inventory training provided)

• Background in consumer products, pet products, or e-commerce supply chains

• Familiarity with quality control processes for manufactured goods

• CPSM or CSCP certification

• Experience configuring an ERP or procurement module

• Mandarin language skills (written or spoken)



Work setup:

This is a fully remote, asynchronous-first position. Written communication is the default. You will document decisions, maintain purchasing status in shared trackers, and communicate proactively without relying on calls.



The Philippines time zone (UTC+8) aligns with Chinese business hours, enabling real-time supplier communication during your regular workday. You will have a defined spend authority threshold for routine purchases and will report directly to the COO with a weekly async check-in.



90-day success milestones:

• All active suppliers identified and documented in a centralized supplier register

• All purchasing activity consolidated under this role

• A standardized purchase order process established and consistently followed

• Full spend visibility by category delivered to leadership

• Top supplier contracts reviewed with renegotiation initiated on a minimum of three



How to apply:

Please submit your resume via Google Drive link to be considered for this position.

title: STR Operations VA
description:
The Casa Collective is hiring someone who understands how short-term rentals actually operate, including guest experience, listing performance, and how daily decisions impact bookings, reviews, and revenue.



Your role is not just to manage tasks. Your role is to help keep properties booked, optimized, and running smoothly.

If you do not have direct STR or Airbnb experience, this role is not the right fit.



About The Casa Collective

The Casa Collective is a design-forward short-term rental brand focused on creating unique, experience-driven stays.

Our properties are designed for:

Getaways. Girls trips. Couples retreats

Small events and micro-weddings

Strong branding. High-performing listings. Standout guest experiences



**To Apply - send us a message with your 

Resume & portfolio with STR or hospitality experience



What You’ll Own

STR Operations & Guest Experience

Manage guest communication across Airbnb and booking platforms

Ensure fast, professional, and consistent responses

Handle guest issues, complaints, and resolution professionally

Navigate cancellations, refunds, and platform policies

Coordinate cleaners, maintenance, and vendors

Ensure smooth turnovers, check-ins, and stays



Listing Management & Optimization

Maintain and update listings across all platforms

Optimize titles, descriptions, photo order, and amenities

Monitor listing performance and identify improvements

Apply STR SEO and platform ranking best practices

Ensure listings stay competitive and aligned with seasonality



Demand & Market Awareness

Research local events, tourism trends, and seasonal demand

Identify high-demand dates and booking opportunities

Flag pricing, availability, and minimum stay opportunities

Support positioning properties for peak seasons, events, and group stays



New Listing Setup

Assist with launching new properties from start to finish

Build and optimize listings for visibility and conversion

Apply proven STR setup strategies



Light Bookkeeping & Tracking

Track rental income and expenses in spreadsheets

Maintain organized financial records

Support reporting and internal tracking



Platform Experience (Preferred)

Experience with one or more of the following:

Airbnb | VRBO or 

Google Vacation Rentals

Property management systems such as Hostex, Hostaway, Hospitable

Happy Guest (not required but would need to learn)

HostCo (not required but would need to learn)



What Success Looks Like

Guest communication is fast, professional, and consistent

Listings remain optimized and competitive

Properties stay well booked and performing

Guest issues are handled smoothly and confidently

Opportunities for improvement are proactively identified

You help turn operations into a reliable, revenue-generating system.



Tools & Systems

Airbnb, OTAs and STR platforms

Google Workspace

Spreadsheets


 or similar CRM preferred

Social media platforms



Compensation

$3 to $6 per hour

20 to 25 hours per week



**To Apply - send us a message with your 

Resume & portfolio with STR or hospitality experience



Pre-interview Questions in a video:

If a listing is not getting bookings, what steps would you take to improve it?

How do you research local demand or events that impact bookings?

Have you helped launch or optimize a listing? Explain your process

How would you prioritize guest messages, a maintenance issue, and updating a listing?



Final Note

This is a long-term role for someone who wants to grow beyond basic VA work and become a key part of operations.

If you understand how to manage properties while improving performance and guest experience, we want to hear from you.

title: Full-Time Virtual Assistant (Bookkeeping + Operations Support)
description:
**Overview**

We’re looking for a highly reliable, detail-oriented full-time Virtual Assistant to support a fast-moving online business in the live events/ticketing space. This role is primarily focused on bookkeeping and financial organization, but also includes a wide range of operational and ad-hoc tasks depending on business needs.



If you’re someone who is organized, proactive, and comfortable switching between structured financial work and flexible “get-it-done” tasks, this will be a great fit.



---



**Core Responsibilities**



**1. Bookkeeping & Financial Tracking (Primary Focus)**



* Maintain accurate records of transactions (income, expenses, transfers)

* Categorize and reconcile transactions regularly

* Prepare simple financial summaries and reports

* Track invoices, payments, and outstanding balances

* Work with spreadsheets and/or accounting tools (QuickBooks, Google Sheets, etc.)



**2. Data Entry & Organization**



* Input and clean data across spreadsheets and internal tools

* Organize large datasets for reporting and analysis

* Maintain structured, easy-to-navigate documentation



**3. Web Research & Manual Tasks**



* Manually scrape or collect data from websites (e.g., questions, listings, or other structured info)

* Conduct online research and compile findings in clean formats

* Follow detailed instructions to gather and organize information



**4. General Virtual Assistant Tasks**



* Assist with various operational needs as they come up

* Handle repetitive but important tasks with high accuracy

* Help improve internal processes over time

* Be available for flexible assignments based on business priorities



---



**Requirements**



* Strong attention to detail (this is critical)

* Solid experience with bookkeeping or accounting basics

* High proficiency in Google Sheets / Excel

* Ability to follow instructions precisely and work independently

* Comfortable with repetitive tasks and manual data work

* Reliable internet connection and consistent availability

* Strong written English communication



---



**Nice to Have**



* Experience with QuickBooks or similar accounting tools

* Experience with data scraping or structured data collection

* Familiarity with online businesses, marketplaces, or ticketing industry

* Basic technical understanding (not required, but helpful)



---



**Work Details**



* Full-time (40 hours/week)

* Flexible schedule, but must overlap a few hours with U.S. time zones

* Long-term role with room to grow



---



**Compensation**



* Competitive based on experience

* Performance-based raises over time



---



**How to Apply**

Please include:



1. A short introduction about yourself

2. Your experience with bookkeeping and spreadsheets

3. Any experience with data entry, scraping, or research tasks

4. Example of a spreadsheet or system you’ve worked on (if available)



---



**Important:**

Attention to detail matters. Include the word **“Precision”** in your application so we know you read everything.



---



We’re looking for someone dependable who can become a core part of the team long-term. If that’s you, apply now.

title: Social Media Manager (Content + Podcast Lead)
description:
This brand is built around one idea: if your body doesn't feel safe holding more, the mind cannot maintain expansion. The work sits at the intersection of nervous system regulation, wealth identity, and embodied leadership — and it's resonating with high-achieving women who are already successful but know they're ready for a bigger container.



We're growing. We need someone who can keep up — and help lead the way.



What this role actually is:

This is not a "schedule posts and clock out" job. This is a content leadership role on a brand that has a very specific voice, a very specific audience, and zero tolerance for generic.



You'll work directly with the Founder — a wealth and identity coach who speaks on money, power, nervous system capacity, and truth — to make sure that message lands consistently across every platform, every week.



What you'll own:

Content repurposing + distribution



Turn podcast episodes, trainings, and raw recordings into polished content for
, Instagram, and LinkedIn. Brand voice is intelligent, grounded, and powerful — not fluffy, not generic. In your first week, you'll develop a Brand Bible and train Claude to write and correct brand-specific content.



Podcast production (end to end).



Episode pipeline management, audio editing, show notes, RSS publishing, and cross-platform promotion. You own this from recording to distribution.



Content calendar + planning



A rolling 2-week calendar at all times, built around launches, offers, and messaging priorities. Consistency without sacrificing quality or strategic intent.



Performance tracking + optimization



Track what's working and why — then do more of it. Test new hooks, formats, and angles. Stay current on platform changes. Metrics accessible through native social platforms.



You're the right person if...

—You think like a content strategist, not just an executor

—You understand what it means to position someone as an authority — not just grow their follower count

—You're naturally curious — you test, learn, and iterate fast

—You don't need to be chased for deadlines — you set the pace

—You're comfortable owning outcomes, not just completing tasks



Required



Social media management for a personal brand, coach, or expert

Strong content repurposing skills — extracting the gold from long-form

Podcast production: editing, publishing, show notes, RSS

, Instagram, LinkedIn best practices

Basic audio + light video editing (Opus, CapCut, or similar)



A bonus if you have...



Experience in coaching, personal development, or the wealth space

An eye for clean, elevated visuals — graphic design skills are a real plus

Understanding of buyer psychology and authority-driven content



What success looks like

We're consistently 2+ weeks ahead on content. Podcast episodes go out clean and on time. The brand looks and sounds like a trusted authority in every piece of content — not like a template was used. You're bringing ideas to the table, not waiting to be told what to do.



If that sounds like how you naturally operate, this is your role.

description:

Company and Vision



PlanetArt’s vision is to be the leading seller of personalized and make-on-demand products worldwide. We provide consumers with unmatched tools and content and an unparalleled end-to-end customer experience that results in high-quality, meaningful finished products and memorable celebrations of life events.

We have more than 500 tea
mbers across multiple offices, primarily in Calabasas CA, San Diego CA, Woodridge IL, Minneapolis, MN and Pleasanton, CA. We also have tea
mbers in two company-owned offices in China, as well as in Europe.





Job Overview



PlanetArt is looking for a Amazon Marketing Assistant as a contractor to support our paid retail media marketing team with day-to-day execution and optimization tasks. This is an ideal role for someone early in their PPC marketing career who wants hands-on exposure to multiple retail platforms and a strong foundation in data-driven marketing.



This is an entry-level, remote, work-from-home position.





What You’ll Do

Key Responsibilities



Support campaign creation, adding products to the campaigns.

QA campaigns, ads, and landing pages

Perform ongoing search term reviews and negative keyword scrubbing

Assist with keyword research and keyword optimization

Help manage ad assets and creative requests in partnership with the design team

Pull performance reports and help spot growth opportunities

Monitor campaign performance and flag anomalies or opportunities



What You Should Have

Skills, Qualifications, and Requirements



Bachelor’s degree in marketing, Business, Communications, or related field (or equivalent experience)

1 year of experience in paid advertising (PPC).

Familiarity with Excel and Google Sheets. Experience with Vlookups, pivot tables is a plus.

High attention to detail and organizational skills

Excellent written and verbal communication skills

Keyword research and search term analysis

Campaign setup and optimization

Time management and multitasking

Familiarity with e-commerce marketplaces is a plus.





What You Can Expect

Working Conditions



Position requires fully remote work scheduled from 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM US Pacific Time

Position requires regular, continuous use of computers.

Position requires regular interaction with tea
mbers through the following methods: phone, Webex, Slack, Zoom or
.



Work From Home Requirements



Reliable high-speed internet access

Noise-cancelling headset (if applicable)

Private, distraction-free environment (if applicable)



Pay Range

Pay Rate: PHP 50,000 to PHP 70,000 per month



Perks

Paid Time Off

Paid US Holidays

title: Jr Accountant
description:
Job Description:



At ERPS GROUP we are seeking a detail-oriented and motivated Junior Accountant to join our team. The ideal candidate will have a strong foundation in accounting principles, fluent English and a desire to learn and grow in the field. This is an excellent opportunity for an entry-level candidate looking to kick-start their career in accounting. The working hours are 8am - 5pm EST.



Responsibilities:



- Assist with day-to-day accounting tasks, including accounts payable, accounts receivable, and general ledger entries.

- Prepare and process invoices, expense reports, and other financial documents accurately and in a timely manner.

- Reconcile bank statements and credit card transactions to ensure accuracy and completeness of financial records.

- Assist with month-end and year-end close processes, including preparing journal entries and reconciliations.

- Support the preparation of financial statements and reports for management and external stakeholders.

- Assist with audit preparation and provide support during audit procedures.

- Maintain organized and up-to-date financial records and documentation.

- Collaborate with tea
mbers to streamline processes and improve efficiency within the finance department.

- Stay informed about relevant accounting regulations and compliance requirements.



Qualifications:



- Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field.

- Strong understanding of accounting principles and practices.

- Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software Quickbooks Desktop.

- Excellent attention to detail and accuracy in data entry and financial reporting.

- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

- Ability to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.

- Effective communication skills, both written and verbal.

- Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment.

- Previous internship or work experience in accounting or finance is preferred



How to Apply



If you are a motivated Jr Accountant with a passion for delivering high-quality client service and making a meaningful impact, we’d love to hear from you. Please submit your resume and a 1-min video explaining why you’re the perfect fit for this role at our company

title: Copywriter
description:
LinkedIn Copywriter - Wanted (Full Time, Long Term)



We are a personal branding agency serving founders, CEOs and business owners across the UK and UAE. We are hiring multiple LinkedIn copywriters to support our growth.



You will be responsible for:

Writing LinkedIn posts for founders, CEOs and business owners across multiple industries

Working across short form posts, long form posts, carousels and personal brand storytelling

Planning and managing content calendars across TOFU, MOFU and BOFU stages

Writing in the client's voice, not your own

Producing any other written content required by the business



Requirements

Professional LinkedIn copywriter with a strong portfolio of posts that have performed on the platform

Deep understanding of proven frameworks including Problem Agitate Solution, AIDA, Before After Bridge and listicle structures

Strong grasp of hooks, pacing, line breaks and the writing styles that actually work on LinkedIn

Able to plan and manage content strategies across multiple clients without being chased

A strong writer in your own right, you write because you enjoy it, not because you have to

Resilient, reliable and able to work to deadlines

Takes direction well and delivers to a high standard



What we offer

Starting wage £500 per month, following probation to £750 per month

Working hours 3pm to 12.30am Philippines time, Monday to Friday

Wage increase after probation

Bonuses on client work

Overtime available, not compulsory

Tools and subscriptions provided, open to additional tools if needed for the role



How to apply

Link to your portfolio covering LinkedIn posts you have written, ideally with performance data where possible

A short note on why you are the right fit

A sample post written in response to this brief: write a TOFU post for a founder who used to be invisible online and is now booking inbound calls from LinkedIn



Your available start date

Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.

Only apply if you are a professional LinkedIn copywriter who knows the platform inside out.

title: Senior AI Digital Marketing Creative Manager
description:
This role is ideal for a leader who thrives on blending creative vision, data intelligence, and AI technologies to drive next-generation digital marketing strategies.



What You’ll Own

You’ll take full ownership of our digital marketing efforts, from strategy to execution to results.



Digital Marketing & Growth

• Plan and execute multi-channel campaigns (social,
, paid ads, content)

• Drive leads, conversions, and revenue through optimized funnels

• Work closely with sales to align marketing with business goals



Website & Conversion Optimization

• Manage and optimize website content, SEO, and performance

• Build high-converting landing pages and funnels

• Track user behavior and continuously improve conversion rates



Creative & Brand Direction

• Create or oversee high-quality designs for ads, social media, and web

• Maintain a strong, consistent, and conversion-focused brand presence



AI & Automation

• Use AI tools for content creation, analytics, and campaign optimization

• Build automation workflows for lead nurturing and follow-ups

• Leverage data insights to improve efficiency and ROI



Social Media Growth

• Manage and grow company and leadership social media accounts

• Create, schedule, and publish engaging content consistently

• Drive engagement, reach, and audience growth



Performance & Scaling

• Track campaign performance and optimize what works

• Use analytics to improve results and scale campaigns



Leadership & Team Building

• Lead and manage the marketing team

• Set KPIs, workflows, and ensure accountability

• Oversee execution and timelines across campaigns



Work Schedule

• Follows EST or PST hours (typically 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM)

• Schedule may vary based on business needs and client`s requirements.



What You Bring

Experience

• 3–5+ years in digital marketing with leadership or managerial experience

• Proven experience managing websites and campaigns

• Experience handling business or executive social media accounts

• Team management experience is a strong advantage



Skills

• Strong digital marketing and growth strategy knowledge

• Solid graphic design skills (Canva, Adobe, or similar)

• Experience with AI tools (ChatGPT, automation, analytics)

• Strong understanding of funnels and lead generation

• Excellent communication and time management



Technical Requirements

• Intel i5 (10th gen+) or Ryzen 5 (3000+)

• 16 GB RAM (recommended)

• 256 GB SSD

• Windows 11 or macOS

• Dual-monitor setup

• HD webcam and noise-cancelling headset

• Reliable high-speed internet

• Backup for power, internet, and device



What You’ll Get

• Salary: PHP 40,000–60,000+ (based on experience)

• Full-time remote role

• Long-term opportunity with career growth

• Direct collaboration with leadership

• Opportunity to build and lead a marketing team

• Paid Time Off, Sick Leave, HMO, and other benefits



Important

Be ready to share 2–3 of your best work samples during the interview (websites, landing pages, campaigns, or similar projects).





Apply here (quick and easy):

title: Virtual Assistant (LinkedIn Recruiter + Webinar Lead Generation Specialist)
description:
We are a fast-growing marketing and consulting firm supporting one of the nation’s top franchise consultants, helping professionals and investors transition into business ownership.



Our mission is simple: Connect the right people with the right franchise opportunities—changing lives through business ownership. We are building a scalable lead generation engine and are looking for a highly organized, detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to help power our outreach and webinar funnel strategy.



Role Overview:

This is not a basic VA role. You will be responsible for executing a LinkedIn-based outbound strategy + webinar funnel system designed to generate qualified leads for franchise consultations. You will work directly with leadership and play a key role in building a predictable pipeline of high-quality prospects.



Core Responsibilities



1. LinkedIn Recruiter Outreach (Primary Focus)

Use LinkedIn Recruiter / Sales Navigator to identify ideal prospects:

Corporate professionals

Executives

High-income earners

Individuals seeking career transitions or investment opportunities

Send daily connection requests and follow-up messages

Personalize outreach based on profiles and triggers

Manage and track conversations inside CRM (HighLevel or similar)

Book qualified prospects into calendar appointments or webinars



2. Webinar Funnel Execution

Invite prospects to live and automated webinars

Manage webinar registration lists and reminders

Coordinate outreach tied to upcoming webinar dates

Follow up with:

Registrants who didn’t attend

Attendees who didn’t book calls

Help optimize show-up rates and conversions



3. CRM & Pipeline Management

Update lead statuses daily

Tag, segment, and organize contacts

Ensure all leads are properly tracked and followed up with

Maintain clean data and reporting



4. Messaging & Campaign Execution

Execute pre-written scripts for:

Connection requests

Follow-ups

Webinar invites

Provide feedback on what’s working and what’s not

Help test new messaging angles



5. Performance Tracking

Track and report weekly on:

Messages sent

Connections made

Responses

Booked calls

Webinar registrations

Identify opportunities to improve conversion rates



Ideal Candidate: We’re looking for someone who is:

Highly organized and detail-oriented

Comfortable with repetitive daily execution

Strong written communicator (clear, natural, conversational)

Familiar with LinkedIn outreach or willing to learn fast

Tech-savvy (CRM systems, spreadsheets, basic automation tools)

Self-motivated and able to hit daily KPIs

Coachable and performance-driven

title: Bookkeeper QuickBooks Online Experienced Contractor
description:
Join Our Team at Solvency Now Bookkeeping as a Bookkeeper! 



Fully Remote 



Are you a QuickBooks Online superstar with a passion for numbers? We’re on the lookout for a Bookkeeper to join our dynamic, remote team! Enjoy the flexibility of working from home while collaborating with fellow bookkeeping experts to deliver top-notch service to our clients.



What You’ll Do:

*Manage bookkeeping for a variety of clients (and yes, we promise they can be fun to work with!)

*Collaborate with a team of bookkeeping pros to elevate our service and support each other in tackling challenges.

*Stay organized and keep deadlines in check while enjoying the perks of remote work.

* Performing bank account and credit card reconciliation.

* Conducting bookkeeping reviews.

* Paying and tracking bills/loans.

* Collecting and tracking invoices.

* Filing 1099(s).

* Calculating and collecting sales tax.

* Payroll filing and reporting.

* Maintaining spreadsheets of financial transactions.

* Submitting a detailed daily report to your direct supervisor.

* Keeping detailed time tracking per client.

* Using internal software to record client work completed.

* Performing other related duties as needed or agreed upon





What’s in it for You:

*Competitive pay: $7-$10/hour based on your experience, plus bonuses after your first 90 days!

*Part Time hours: 20 hours a week, then after 90 days transition to full-time hours: 40 hours a week, with the freedom to 

  work in your time zone.

*A supportive, fun work environment where you can learn and grow alongside industry experts!



What We’re Looking For:

*You must have a strong command of the English language ( Read, Write & Speak) 

*Computer-savvy with a reliable working computer.

*Super organized and able to juggle multiple clients with ease.

*Proficiency in QuickBooks Online is a MUST (1-3 years experience) 



If you’re ready to take your bookkeeping skills to new heights while enjoying the flexibility of remote work and teamwork, we want to hear from you! 



???? Apply today and fill out the application on this link:

title: AI Video Creator
description:
We're not hiring someone to "make videos." We're hiring someone to move humans — to take the raw clay of footage, AI tools, and a story worth telling, and shape it into something that makes a stranger stop scrolling, lean in, and feel something real. Then act on it.





Who You Are 



You're a Storyteller First, Technologist Second. Anyone can prompt an AI tool. Almost nobody can tell a story that makes a CFO tear up at a B2B brand video. That's the gap we're hiring for.



You're Emotionally Intelligent — and It Shows in the Work. You can read a room, read a brief, and read a person's face on camera and know when they're holding back. You understand that great marketing isn't about being clever — it's about being true. You make people feel seen. That's the unfair advantage you bring.







- You're Motivated by Impact, Not Output. You don't measure your week in videos shipped. You measure it in whether the work moved someone — a customer, a candidate, a community. You'd rather make 10 videos that matter than 100 that don't.



- You Have Range. You can make us laugh on Tuesday and make us cry on Thursday. You can write a 15-second hook and a 6-minute brand film. You can shoot on an iPhone in a coffee shop and direct a polished studio piece.



You Move Fast Without Breaking Trust. You ship. You iterate. You don't disappear into perfectionism for three weeks. But you also don't publish anything you wouldn't be proud to sign your name to.



You're a Pro With AI — Not a Tourist. You've already built workflows nobody taught you. You know which tools are hype and which are durable. You know when to use AI and, more importantly, when not to.







Non-Negotiables



- Portfolio that makes us jealous. Show us the work. We'd rather see three pieces you're proud of than a sizzle reel of everything you've ever touched.

- 3+ years producing video for marketing or brand, ideally in a fast-moving environment where you weren't handed a 50-page brand book.

- Demonstrated AI fluency. You can walk us through a video you made where AI was central to the workflow — and explain the choices, including the human ones.

- Strong writing. If you can't write, you can't make great video. Period.

- You actually like people. This job involves real humans on camera. They'll feel how you feel about them.



The chance to build something. A team that takes the work seriously and themselves lightly. Direct line to leadership — your ideas get heard, your wins get noticed, your work goes out the door. Compensation that matches the talent we're looking for. And the rare gift of doing work you can point to in five years and say: that one mattered.

title: Appointment Setter (Cold Calling) – Beauty Industry
description:
Appointment Setter (Cold Calling) – Beauty Industry



We are looking for a fluent English-speaking appointment setter to contact esthetic and cosmetology schools in the U.S.



Your role is to introduce our company and book qualified discovery calls with school decision-makers for potential training partnerships.



This is NOT a customer service role. You must be confident on the phone and comfortable initiating conversations with business owners and school administrators.



This is a results-driven role focused on booking qualified calls.



About Us:



Opulence Brows & Beauty is a luxury permanent makeup studio and training brand. We provide professional PMU education and are expanding into partnerships with esthetic schools to offer training within their curriculum.



Responsibilities:

Call esthetic and cosmetology schools in the U.S.

Ask for the program director, owner, or decision-maker

Introduce our PMU training partnership

Send follow-up
s

Book qualified discovery calls

Log all activity in a tracking sheet (CRM)



Requirements:

Fluent English with a clear, confident speaking voice

Experience with cold calling or appointment setting

Strong communication and listening skills

Professional and confident tone on calls

Comfortable handling rejection and continuing outreach

Reliable internet and quiet work environment



Compensation:

$3/hour base pay

$25 bonus per qualified call booked and attended

3% commission on closed contracts

Typical Earnings:

Part-time base: ~$240/month

Call bonuses: $250–$300/month

Commission: $300–$900+ per deal



Total monthly earnings typically range from $800–$1,400+ depending on performance.



???? Top performers consistently exceed $1,500+/month.



Daily Targets:

40–60 calls per day

1–2 booked calls per day

Consistent follow-up with leads

You’re a strong fit if you:

Can build quick rapport on the phone

Are confident asking for decision-makers

Can guide conversations toward booking a call

Understand this is a performance-based role

Have experience calling U.S.-based businesses (preferred)





To Apply:



Please include:



A short voice recording introducing yourself

Your experience with cold calling or appointment setting

Your favorite sales or customer interaction experience



To confirm attention to detail, include the word “Opulence” in your application. Applications without it will not be considered.

title: Operations Manager
description:
Operations Manager (Part-Time) – High-Level Operator Needed (Results-Driven Only)



Company: Sunset 

Position: Operations Manager (Part-Time)

Compensation: $550/month

Schedule: 1:00 PM – 5:30 PM EST (with 30-minute unpaid break)

Start Date: Immediate



?? READ THIS FIRST – WHO THIS ROLE IS FOR



This is NOT for someone who waits for instructions.



We are looking for a bullheaded operator—someone who:



Takes action without needing constant direction

Solves problems instead of just pointing them out

Moves fast, executes faster, and figures things out along the way

Brings structure into chaos

Thinks in outcomes, not tasks



If you are the type who:



Asks too many questions without taking action

Needs hand-holding or step-by-step instructions

Avoids ownership



This role is not for you.



???? ABOUT THE ROLE



You will act as the central operational hub of a fast-growing coaching & consulting business.



Your job is simple:

???? Make the business run efficiently without relying on leadership.



You will oversee operations across:



Marketing

Sales pipelines

Client fulfillment

Internal systems



This is a builder role, not a maintenance role.



???? CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

Own and manage daily business operations

Monitor and improve lead flow, CRM pipelines, and marketing performance

Build and optimize client onboarding and overall experience

Act as the bridge between leadership, team, and clients

Track payments, invoices, and financial records

Manage CRM systems, automations, and tools

Create and implement SOPs (standard operating procedures)

Coordinate tasks across tea
mbers and contractors

Support leadership with execution and priorities

Track KPIs and report performance insights



???? STRATEGIC RESPONSIBILITIES (HIGH IMPACT)

Build a centralized operational system (funding, credit, training)

Create scalable workflows with funding as the core revenue engine

Develop onboarding systems, SOPs, and internal documentation

Assist in hiring, onboarding, and training tea
mbers

Oversee social media operations (content planning, repurposing, execution)

Improve funnels, lead quality, and conversion rates

Reduce leadership involvement by building systems that run independently



???? PERFORMANCE EXPECTATIONS

Executes with minimal supervision

Takes full ownership of outcomes

Solves problems before escalating them

Builds systems that remove bottlenecks

Operates with urgency, precision, and accountability



???? ONBOARDING PROCESS

Review Loom videos, recordings, and current systems

Attend alignment call for priorities

Begin immediate execution and system structuring



???? COMPENSATION

$550/month

Paid twice monthly (5th and 20th)

Payment via PayPal or Wise



???? GROWTH OPPORTUNITY

Performance evaluation after 30 days

Potential full-time transition within 60–90 days

Increased compensation based on results and impact



? HOW TO APPLY (IMPORTANT)



To filter serious applicants, follow these steps:



Start your application with: “I EXECUTE”

Provide specific examples of:

A system you built from scratch

A problem you solved without being told what to do

A time you improved operations or efficiency

Include your portfolio or proof of work

Video introduction (about Operations Management Style and Experience)



Applications that do not follow instructions will be ignored.

title: PART TIME ACCOUNTANT
description:
Job Title:



Bookkeeper & Payroll Specialist (QuickBooks + Tax Support)



Job Overview:



We are a growing healthcare company seeking a reliable and detail-oriented Bookkeeper & Payroll Specialist to manage our financial records, maintain accurate reporting, and ensure timely payroll processing.



This role is essential to maintaining financial integrity as we scale. The ideal candidate is experienced in QuickBooks, understands U.S.-based accounting practices, and can confidently support tax preparation and compliance.



Key Responsibilities:



Bookkeeping & Financial Management



Maintain accurate and up-to-date financial records in QuickBooks

Categorize transactions correctly (income, expenses, transfers, etc.)

Reconcile bank and credit card accounts daily

Clean up and organize existing books

Track accounts payable and receivable



Payroll Management



Process payroll biweekly accurately and on time

Ensure all payroll records are properly documented

Coordinate with leadership to confirm hours, pay rates, and adjustments

Assist with contractor vs. employee tracking (1099 vs W-2 awareness preferred)



Tax & Compliance Support



Prepare financial reports needed for tax filing

Ensure compliance with basic U.S. accounting and payroll standards



Reporting & Communication



Provide weekly or biweekly financial summaries

Flag discrepancies, unusual transactions, or financial concerns

Maintain organized financial systems and documentation



Requirements:

Strong experience with QuickBooks (REQUIRED)

Proven bookkeeping and accounting experience

Understanding of U.S. payroll processes

Experience supporting tax preparation 

High attention to detail and accuracy

Strong communication skills

Ability to work independently and meet deadlines



Preferred Qualifications:

Experience with U.S.-based healthcare or service businesses

Familiarity with QUICKBOOKS

Experience cleaning up disorganized books

Basic understanding of tax compliance



Work Details:

Remote position (must have reliable internet)

Part-time to start, with opportunity to grow into full-time

Biweekly payroll responsibility is required

Flexible hours, but must meet deadlines



To Apply:

Please include:

A brief summary of your QuickBooks experience

Examples of bookkeeping or payroll work you’ve handled

Your availability and hourly rate

Confirmation you are comfortable with biweekly payroll processing

title: Tax Associate & Tax Technical Writer (Tax Preparation, Research & Strategy Support)
description:
We are seeking a highly experienced, detail-oriented tax professional to support our growing tax advisory and compliance team. 



This is not an entry-level bookkeeping or administrative role. We are specifically looking for someone with strong hands-on experience in tax preparation across multiple entity types, advanced tax research, technical memo writing, and clear client communication. 



This role is ideal for someone who understands how tax law applies in real-world situations, can prepare accurate returns, document positions thoroughly, and communicate confidently with both internal team members and clients. 



What You’ll Be Doing 

• Prepare technical tax documentation, memos, and supporting materials for client strategies 

• Translate complex tax concepts into clear, client-friendly language 

• Track and document 100-hour participation requirements 

• Ensure client activities are categorized properly (high, medium, low value) 

• Serve as liaison between tax strategy, internal team members, and clients 

• Follow up with clients to ensure required activities are completed and properly logged 

• Prepare and review tax returns including: 

• Individuals (1040) 

• Partnerships (1065) 

• S Corporations (1120S) 

• Additional entities as needed 

• Support tax return preparation during tax season (approximately 30%) 

• Conduct tax research and summarize findings clearly

• Interpret, apply, and document tax code for client situations 

• Maintain organized files, workpapers, and tax position support 

• Assist in implementing active participation and other strategic tax initiatives 

• Collaborate closely with leadership and internal tax team 



 



Key Requirements 

• Extensive experience in tax preparation across multiple entity types 

• Strong experience with: 

• Individual tax returns (1040) 

• Partnership returns (1065) 

• S Corporation returns (1120S) 

• Solid understanding of tax law, tax code application, and compliance requirements 

• Strong experience with tax research and preparing tax memorandums 

• Ability to interpret, apply, and clearly document tax code 

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills 

• Professional and polished client-facing communication 

• Strong internal communication and collaboration skills 

• Highly detail-oriented, organized, and deadline-driven 

• Ability to work independently and solve problems proactively 



Nice to Have 

• Preferred experience with Drake Tax Software 

• Experience preparing additional returns such as: 

• C Corporations (1120) 

• Trust returns 

• Multi-state filings 

• Experience writing internal tax guidance, SOPs, or client education materials 

• Experience with UltraTax, ProSeries, Lacerte, or similar tax software 

• Experience creating tax summaries, technical resources, or internal documentation 

• CPA, EA, or equivalent experience preferred but not required 



Role Details 

• Remote  

• Long-term opportunity 

• Mix of responsibilities: 

• 60% tax preparation & review support 

• 25% tax research / technical memos 

• 15% client communication & strategy support 



To Apply - Please include: 

• Your resume 

• A short introduction summarizing your tax preparation experience 

• Specific experience with 1040, 1065, and 1120S returns 

• Example of tax research or memo writing (if available) 

• Tax software experience (please specify Drake experience if applicable) 



Important Note 



***This is a specialized tax role, not a general admin or VA position.*** 



We are specifically seeking someone who can: 

• Prepare accurate tax returns across multiple entity types 

• Research tax issues and apply tax law correctly 

• Write clear, professional tax memos and documentation 

• Communicate confidently with clients and internal team members 

• Work independently with minimal oversight 

• Operate at a high level in a fast-paced professional environment

title: Tax Accountant
description:
Company Overview:

Protego Advisers is a dynamic and innovative Tax & Consulting Firm dedicated to building and protecting the impact entrepreneurs make in our world. With a commitment to excellence and passion, we strive to make a meaningful impact in the world.



Position Overview:

We are looking for a detail-oriented and analytical Tax Accountant to contribute to the efficient and effective operation of our finance department. This individual is not only well-versed in Drake Tax but also brings valuable experience in preparing and reviewing a wide range of complex U.S. tax returns for individuals and businesses. Their knowledge of tax laws, combined with the ability to work efficiently and accurately, makes them a trusted resource for clients seeking professional tax services.



Please apply to this position ONLY if you meet the following qualifications, professionals who do not have experience in using Drake Tax Software will not be considered for this role at this time:



Qualifications:



Tax Return Preparation:

-Prepare federal, state, and local tax returns (e.g., Forms 1040, 1120, 1120S, and 1065) for individuals, corporations, partnerships, and other entities.

-Review financial records, income statements, and other necessary documents provided by clients to ensure accuracy and completeness.

-Apply all eligible deductions, credits, and adjustments to minimize client tax liabilities.



Client Consultation:

-Meet with clients to gather tax-related information and discuss their financial situation, ensuring all necessary documentation is provided.

-Answer client questions related to tax filings, deductions, and potential refunds or liabilities.

Provide tax advice and planning strategies to help clients minimize their tax obligations and plan for the future.



Compliance and Accuracy:

-Ensure all tax returns comply with IRS regulations and guidelines, including state and local tax laws.

Verify that all tax filings are accurate and timely, helping clients avoid penalties or audits.

-Keep updated on changes in tax laws, regulations, and filing deadlines to maintain compliance.



Electronic Filing & Submission:

-Use tax preparation software to file returns electronically when possible and maintain proper records of submissions.

Provide clients with copies of filed returns and necessary follow-up documentation.



Audit Support:

-Assist clients in responding to IRS inquiries or audits by gathering necessary documentation and providing explanations for filings.

-Communicate with the IRS or state tax authorities as needed on behalf of clients.



Maintain Confidentiality:

-Handle sensitive client information with discretion and confidentiality.

-Follow all ethical standards related to client privacy and data security.



Qualifications:



Experience: Minimum of 3 years of experience in tax preparation for U.S. individuals and businesses.

Education: A degree in accounting, finance, or a related field is preferred, but not required. Certification in tax preparation (such as IRS Annual Filing Season Program or Enrolled Agent status) is a plus.

Tax Knowledge: Strong understanding of U.S. tax laws and regulations, including individual, corporate, partnership, and self-employment tax.

Software Proficiency: Experience using Drake Tax Software is required.

Communication Skills: Excellent written and verbal communication skills for interacting with clients and tax authorities.

Attention to Detail: Strong organizational skills and a keen eye for accuracy to ensure error-free tax filings.

Time Management: Ability to manage multiple clients and deadlines, especially during peak tax season.

Job Type: Full-time or part-time (seasonal opportunities available)

Compensation: Competitive salary or hourly wage (based on experience), with potential for performance-based bonuses during tax season.



If you are passionate about helping clients navigate their tax obligations and are looking to join a growing team, we encourage you to apply today!



For individuals extremely interested in this position wanting quick consideration, please send your resume to 
 subject line being "Tax Accountant - (your name)".

title: Amazon Listing Growth Specialist (DataDive Expert Required)
description:
PLEASE READ THIS JOB DESCRIPTION CAREFULLY - this job is not for everyone.    



Who We Are



Southshore Fine Linens is a fast-growing bedding brand with over 3,500 SKUs across Amazon, Shopify, and wholesale channels. We operate at scale—and we are serious about growth.

 We are not hiring a Virtual Assistant.

 We are hiring an Amazon operator who understands ranking, search intent, and 

conversion. A long-term employee who wants the stability and growth and understand it only comes with performance and results.



What This Role Really Is



You are responsible for one thing:  GROWING LISTINGS.  



Not maintaining them. Not “updating when asked.”

 

You will:

Diagnose why listings are not ranking

Use data to fix them

Execute improvements

Track impact

If you’ve used DataDive to reverse-engineer ranking and build listings around keyword clusters — you will thrive here.

 

What You Will Own:



Listing Optimization & Ranking Strategy



Build and optimize listings using DataDive-driven keyword clustering

Write titles, bullets, and descriptions based on search intent—not guesswork

Identify ranking gaps and fix them



Keyword & Market Analysis



Use DataDive, Helium 10, or similar tools to:

Identify high-value keywords

Analyze competitors

Track ranking movement



Catalog & Variation Management



Fix broken parent-child relationships

Optimize variation structures for ranking and conversion

Merge/split listings when needed



Listing Health & Issue Resolution



Resolve suppressed listings and compliance issues

Handle hijackers and listing conflicts

Work with Seller Support when required



Content & Conversion



Collaborate with the design team on images and A+ content

Improve CTR and conversion rate through better visuals and copy



Performance Ownership



Track:

Keyword ranking

Conversion rate

Listing health



Report weekly with insights and actions—not just data

Non-Negotiable Requirements



3–5+ years hands-on Amazon Seller Central experience

Direct experience using DataDive – (HELIUM 10 Experience will NOT substitute for DataDive).

Strong understanding of:

Ranking factors

Keyword clustering

Listing SEO

Variation structures



Experience managing large catalogs (500+ SKUs minimum)

Strong written English



Who This Is NOT For



Task-based Virtual Assistants

People who wait for instructions

Anyone who has not used DataDive or similar tools deeply

Who You Are



 You:



Understand how listings rank—and why they lose ranking

Can look at a listing and immediately spot what’s wrong

Use data, not opinions

Take ownership of results

What Success Looks Like (First 60 Days)



Listings rebuilt using DataDive insights

Ranking improvements on target keywords

Broken catalog structures fixed

Clear increase in visibility and conversion



How to Apply (IMPORTANT)



 Send:



Your resume

Screenshot of a DataDive analysis you performed

A short breakdown:

What was wrong with the listing

What you changed

What result you achieved



Applications without this will not be considered.

 Work Schedule

Time Doctor Set up is Mandatory and any manipulation with softwares or other means is automatic grounds for termination

 Full-time role

 Must be available during Pacific Standard Time (PST)

title: CapCut Short-Form Videos Editor
description:
Role Overview



We are looking for a detail-oriented and instruction-driven Video Editor to join our team at Exchange Life, a US-based fast growing travel jewelry brand, selling flag charms (Our website: 
). Your mission will be to create short, clean, high-performing organic videos for Instagram and TikTok, simple, native-looking content that blends into the feed and drives results.



These are fast edits: 7–15 second videos, simple text on screen, no effects or complex transitions. Each video takes around 15 minutes to make, while the focus needs to be on picking the best clips available from our clip library, which requires a good eye for aesthetics and visual quality.



If you have the eye for good aesthetics and have a knack for creating content that stops the scroll and engages audiences, we want to talk to you. This is a long-term, fully remote position for an editor who is passionate about the intersection of creativity and data, and is excited by the opportunity to have their work directly impact business results.



What You'll Do:



- Edit short videos (7–15 seconds) for organic TikTok and Instagram Reels using footage from our clip library.

- Select the strongest clips: the right 
nts, the right angles, the right feel.

- Keep edits clean: simple on-screen text, no heavy effects, natural pacing.

- Follow creative briefs closely and deliver consistently.

- Turn edits around quickly once an assignment comes in.



Who We're Looking For:



- A Strong Eye for Aesthetics: You can look at raw footage and immediately know what's aesthetic and what's not. Clip selection is the job, and you take it seriously.

- Precise and Instruction-Driven: You follow briefs closely, ask questions when something is unclear, and don't improvise when the direction is specific.

- Short-Form Native: Your recent work is TikTok and Reels, not YouTube, not long-form, not cinematic edits.

- Fast and Efficient: You move through this simple editing style quickly.

- CapCut or Premiere Pro Proficient: Comfortable in a fast-paced editing workflow.

- Reliable: Strong English, detail-oriented, and able to work independently with minimal back-and-forth.

- Available: Reliable internet and hardware to edit.



Compensation & Growth



We believe in rewarding top talent for outstanding results. This role offers a unique compensation structure designed to give you significant earning potential.



- Base Rate: You will start at 180 PHP per hour ($3/hour), averaging 6 hours of work a day, 6 days a week. Fully flexible, you manage your own time.

- Performance Bonuses: On top of your hourly rate, you will earn substantial monthly bonuses for creating viral videos and winning ads that meet or exceed performance targets. Our bonus structure is uncapped.

- Earning Potential: Our top-performing editors consistently earn $1,000-$1,500+ per month by combining base hours with performance bonuses.



This is a long-term position, not a gig. Most of our editors have been with us for over a year.



Why Join Our Team?



- Fully Remote: Work from anywhere in the world.

- No Fixed Schedule: You manage your own time. Deliver fast, and own your output.

- Direct Impact: See your creative work translate directly into business success and your own earnings.

- Growth & Stability: Join a team that values long-term relationships and provides ongoing training and opportunities.



Ready to Apply?



Complete our application form here: 
/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdAQYoFEF-bYEnew5NLwWUN0btdonff2rN7EremnA0bJQ8J8w/viewform?usp=header



The form includes everything. Do not
 or message us directly. All applications must go through the form.

 to:

 must include:

 Incomplete or rushed applications will not be considered.

title: Vacation Rentals Communications Manager
description:
Xeniastays must be kept at 5-star-rent ready to be successful on the market, and the communications lead will be responsible for ensuring teams work together to reach that goal, and remain there consistently.

It’s crucial for our communications lead to possess the skills to analyze and resolve problems quickly, while under pressure, creating win-win solutions, while developing preventative strategies, as possible.

The candidate must have the necessary skills to analyze data related to the position, prepare reports, and update teams and leadership, on a regular basis.

Flexibility is a must, along with a commitment for doing what it takes to get the job done. Communications lead will be responsible for working with cleaning team, our maintenance team and tending to more complicated matters and emergencies/urgent matters, as they arise.





Responsibilities



- Respond to guest inquiries and manage reservations through various online platforms such as Airbnb, Guesty, and VRBO.



- Coordinate with housekeeping and maintenance teams to ensure timely turnover of rental properties.



- Manage payment processing and ensure timely collection of rental fees and create monthly P&L accounting reports



- Provide guests with detailed information about the rental properties and surrounding areas.



- Respond to guest complaints and concerns and resolve any issues in a timely and professional manner.



- Conduct research on the vacation rental market and competition to inform pricing and marketing strategies.



- Maintain accurate records of bookings, payments, and expenses.



- Assist in creating and implementing marketing campaigns for our properties.



- Communicate with guests before, during, and after their stay to ensure a seamless experience.

title: Short-Form Reels Video Editor (Flexibe Hours, Fully Remote)
description:
Role Overview



We are looking for a highly creative and results-driven Performance Video Editor to join our team at Exchange Life, a travel-themed e-commerce brand selling jewelry (keychains and bracelets featuring country flag charms that people wear to show where they've been). Your mission will be to create high-performing organic videos for Instagram and TikTok that don’t look like an ad.



If you have a knack for creating content that stops the scroll and engages audiences, we want to talk to you. This is a long-term, fully remote position for an editor who is passionate about the intersection of creativity and data, and is excited by the opportunity to have their work directly impact business results.



What You'll Do:



- Edit raw footage into compelling, fast-paced short-form video tailored for organic TikToks and Instagram Reels.

- Incorporate viral trends, engaging hooks, and retention-focused pacing to maximize performance.

- Add dynamic captions, relevant B-roll, and sound design to create a native viewing experience.

- Collaborate with our marketing team to test new creative concepts and iterate on winning ads to scale campaigns.

- Analyze ad performance to understand what resonates with the audience and apply those insights to future edits.



Who We're Looking For:



- A Viral Content Creator, Not Just an Editor: You have proven experience creating organic short-form videos that have achieved significant reach. Specifically: at least one TikTok or Reel you edited has crossed 500,000 views in the last 2 months or an ad that performed well. You understand what makes content shareable and how to capture attention in the first three seconds, and you can explain exactly what you did to make that happen.

- A Performance-Minded Editor: You are excited by the idea of your creative work driving measurable results. You think about hook rate, watch time, and retention, not just aesthetics.

- A Short-Form Native: Your portfolio contains at least 3 recent TikTok or Reels edits from the last 30 days. Not YouTube. Not wedding videos. Not long-form. Short-form organic social, specifically.

- CapCut or Premiere Pro Whiz: You are highly proficient in a fast-paced editing workflow using modern tools.

- Excellent Communicator: You have strong English skills and are reliable, detail-oriented, and can work independently.

- Available & Ready: You have a reliable internet connection and can turn footage around quickly when it lands.



Compensation & Growth: Earn Based on Performance



We believe in rewarding top talent for outstanding results. This role offers a unique compensation structure designed to give you significant earning potential.



- Base Rate: You will start with a competitive base rate of $15 per hour, averaging around 18 hours of work per week, fully flexible, you manage your own time.

- Performance Bonuses: On top of your hourly rate, you will earn substantial monthly bonuses for creating viral videos and winning ads that meet or exceed performance targets. Our bonus structure is uncapped.

- Earning Potential: Our top-performing editors consistently earn $2,000-$3,000+ per month by combining base hours with performance bonuses.



This is not a gig. It is a long-term partnership. Most of our editors have been with us for over a year, and we are committed to investing in your growth and success.



Why Join Our Team?



- Fully Remote: Work from anywhere in the world.

- No Fixed Schedule: You manage your own time. Work when footage is ready, deliver fast, and own your output.

- Direct Impact: See your creative work translate directly into business success and your own earnings.

- Growth & Stability: Join a team that values long-term relationships and provides ongoing training and opportunities.



Ready to Apply?



Complete our application form here: 
/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfvFS3tYNm5k1NCaj55VQXd-lQdYXhPXeGAcUzyznDD5MAA8g/viewform?usp=header



The form includes everything, your portfolio, background questions, and two trial videos to submit. Do not
 us directly. All applications must go through the form.

title: Accounting Manager
description:
About the Role

We’re looking for a hands-on Accounting Manager who thrives in a fast-paced startup environment and has experience working with U.S. businesses. This role is ideal for someone who is highly organized, detail-oriented, and comfortable taking ownership across multiple areas in a growing company.

In a startup setting, accounting requires flexibility and a proactive mindset. The right candidate will be able to manage competing priorities, move fluidly between tasks, and stay closely involved in day-to-day accounting operations while helping ensure everything runs smoothly.

You will play a key role in maintaining accurate financial records, supporting reporting, and ensuring compliance with U.S. accounting standards.



Key Responsibilities



Manage and execute monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes

Prepare and review journal entries, account reconciliations, and financial statements

Maintain the general ledger and supporting schedules

Oversee and execute accounts payable and accounts receivable processes

Ensure accurate bookkeeping and financial records

Prepare monthly financial reports and supporting documentation

Support financial analysis and reporting for internal stakeholders

Assist with audits and maintain proper financial documentation

Operate effectively in a fast-paced, high-growth startup environment

Handle a wide range of accounting responsibilities simultaneously

Collaborate with operations and finance teams to keep processes running efficiently

Identify and support improvements in accounting workflows, systems, and documentation





Requirements



Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field

5+ years of accounting experience, preferably supporting U.S.-based companies

Experience working in a startup or high-growth environment

Strong experience with end-to-end accounting processes and month-end close

Advanced proficiency in Excel and accounting software (QuickBooks, NetSuite, Xero, or similar)





What We’re Looking For



A hands-on professional who is comfortable executing accounting tasks directly

Someone adaptable who can take ownership and operate across multiple areas

Strong multitasking and prioritization skills

High attention to detail and a strong sense of accountability

Comfortable working with U.S. financial processes and cross-functional teams





Nice to Have



CPA or equivalent certification

Experience with financial system implementations or process improvements

Experience working with remote or international teams

title: Amazon Demand Planning & Inventory Manager
description:
We are seeking an experienced Amazon Demand Planning & Inventory Manager to own end-to-end demand planning and inventory optimization across multiple Amazon marketplaces, including the US, UK, and CA.

This role is responsible for building accurate forecasts, maintaining healthy inventory levels, and driving operational planning across both FBA and FBM channels. You will work closely with operations, warehousing, and planning teams to ensure consistent product availability, optimize replenishment strategies, and effectively support high-volume periods such as Prime Day and Q4.

Key focus areas include demand forecasting, replenishment planning, data analysis, Amazon inventory health, and cross-functional collaboration to improve availability and operational efficiency.



Key Responsibilities

Demand Planning & Forecasting



Build and maintain monthly demand forecasts for Amazon marketplaces (US, UK, CA) at the Style-Color level

Manage a 9–12 month planning horizon with both weekly and monthly forecasting outputs

Leverage historical sales data, lead times, and master data to improve forecast accuracy

Apply forecasting models (seasonality, regression, exponential smoothing) and AI tools to predict demand

Amazon Operations & Inventory Management



Own replenishment planning, inbound shipments, and storage optimization for FBA and FBM

Partner with Amazon Operations to resolve shortages, chargebacks, and inventory discrepancies

Support FBA vs. FBM fulfillment strategy during peak periods such as Prime Day and Q4

Coordinate kitting requirements and work with Operations and 3PL partners to ensure timely inbound execution

Data Management & Analysis



Analyze sales and inventory data across Amazon Seller Central, NetSuite, and Looker

Perform data transformations to ensure accuracy and resolve data quality issues

Collaborate with data teams to validate inputs and maintain reliable inventory tracking

Cross-Functional Collaboration



Work closely with planning, warehousing, product, and sales teams to ensure strong inventory availability

Provide actionable insights for Amazon planning and maintain clear reporting on priorities and workload





Requirements



7+ years of experience in Amazon demand planning and FBA inventory management

Strong proficiency in Excel and Amazon Seller Central (FBA, FBM, Direct Import)

Experience with ERP systems (NetSuite preferred) and inventory management tools

Proven track record in forecasting, demand planning, and data-driven decision making

Strong understanding of Amazon inventory health metrics, FBA logistics, and storage optimization

Experience managing replenishment, inbound shipments, and 3PL coordination

Expertise in forecasting methodologies (seasonality, regression, exponential smoothing)

Experience managing 9–12 month demand planning horizons

Experience in multi-channel DTC + Amazon environments, preferably in apparel or consumer goods

Familiarity with data tools such as BigQuery, Looker, or similar analytics platforms

Experience managing inventory across multiple Amazon marketplaces (US, UK, CA)
