title: Virtual Process Engineer (US Real Estate)
description:
Location: 100% Remote (Candidates must be based in the Philippines)

Job Type: Full-time (Starts with a 90-Day Paid Training Period)



About Us:

We are a US-based property management company operating with a deep commitment to Continuous Improvement and Lean principles, modeled after the Toyota Production System. We use specific training curriculums (including Moodle and Gemba concepts) to guide our operations. We are looking for a dedicated tea
mber to become an expert in our systems and help us improve our daily processes.



The Role:

This is a Junior-level virtual position ideal for recent graduates in Engineering, IT, or Computer Science. We know you may not have work experience yet; we are looking for someone with a logical mind who is ready to learn.



You will begin with a 90-Day Process Engineering Training Program. During this time, you will receive a training allowance while you learn our systems and methods.



Once trained, you will help us solve operational problems, research solutions, and organize our data. This is a hands-on role for a highly organized individual who enjoys structure and troubleshooting.



Key Responsibilities:



Learning & Research (First 90 Days): Your main focus initially is to complete our Moodle-based training curriculum. You will need to research concepts and apply them to our specific business case to show you understand how we operate.

Database Administration: Manage our NocoDB operational dashboard (similar to Airtable or SmartSuite). You will use your technical background to keep our data organized and efficient.

Problem Solving: Help us identify bottlenecks in our work. You will research why a problem is happening and propose logical solutions to the management team.

Process Documentation: Work with our US-based team to document how they currently do tasks, then help create Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) to make those tasks easier and faster.

Performance Tracking: Help us track Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and prepare simple reports that show how our improvements are working.

Qualifications:



Education: Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Industrial, Civil, etc.), Computer Science, or Information Technology is preferred.

Experience: Fresh graduates are encouraged to apply. We are happy to train the right person.

Technical Skills: Comfortable learning new software quickly. Experience with databases or low-code platforms (like Airtable/NocoDB) is a plus.

Mindset: You should be curious, detailed-oriented, and enjoy fixing things.

Communication: Clear written English skills are essential for creating documentation and communicating with the team.

Discipline: As this is a remote role, you must be self-motivated and able to focus on your training modules from home.

Why Apply?



Training: Gain experience with Lean/Toyota Production Syste
thodologies—valuable skills for any future career in operations or engineering.

Growth: This is a career-track role. If you progress well, there is room to grow within the company.

Impact: You will be helping us solve real problems for a fast-paced real estate company.



Apply Here:

title: Licensed CPA
description:
About Harker Advisory

Harker Advisory is a U.S.-based tax and business advisory firm helping entrepreneurs legally reduce taxes while staying compliant and well-documented. We combine proactive tax planning, accurate bookkeeping, and strategic advisory so business owners can reinvest profits and grow with confidence.

We value clarity, integrity, documentation, and collaboration. Our team works closely together, respects each other’s time, and takes pride in high-quality work.



About the Role

We are seeking a detail-oriented Accounting Professional to support our U.S.-based CPA and advisory team. This role is highly focused on strategic growth in different phases of engagement. Primarily Reviewing previous Tax & Accounting records, identifying errors and missed opportunities; Correcting errors, cleaning up books and preparing taxes for previous years; managing and providing proactive support with a limited number of clients, catering to developing good rapport with the same clients. 

You must be proficient in QuickBooks Online bookkeeping, financial reporting, reviewing &/or maintaining clean, compliant records that support advanced tax strategies.

You will work within a team, maintain a workflow & fulfill tasks within SOP's directed by an Operations Manager/Executive Assistant & other bookkeepers.





Primary Responsibilities

- knowledge of IRS regulations, and willingness to learn industry-specific expertise (Dental, Veterinarian, Plastic Surgery, and other licensed professional/owner type businesses) 

- Be passionate about accomplishing tasks thoroughly and on time.

- Review Accounting within Quickbooks Online, Xero and other accounting software or bank statements

- Perform Tax & Financial Analysis and Audits

- Work with a controller and account executive to communicate with clients. 

- Follow and refine processes associated with cleaning up taxes & accounting

- Work with controller and account executive to transition new clients into a workflow with tasks to be completed by bookkeeper and or yourself.

- Proficient in written and spoken English

- Work as a member of a team

- Financial Analysis & Audits 

- In depth knowledge of QuickBooks Online

- Ability to proficiently review P & L's

- Reconcile accounts and identify discrepancies

- Maintain clean, audit-ready books

- Deliver books ready for US Tax Preparer/CPA

- Ensure bookkeeping aligns with tax strategies and compliance requirements

- Financial Reporting

- Produce and/or oversee, and review monthly financial reports for clients

- Prepare quarterly financial reports, including tax projections

- Ensure reports are accurate, timely, and clearly presented

- Advisory & Team Support

- Work with the controller and account executive to:

- Review financials prior to quarterly or annual client calls

- Discuss growth insights and financial trends

- Support tax planning by ensuring numbers are reliable and well-documented

- Client Communication Support

- Join client calls when needed or review recorded client communications to understand expectations

- Apply instructions accurately and ask clarifying questions when necessary

- Documentation & CRM Management

- Maintain detailed, clear notes in CRM, TaxDome

- Update tasks, client records, and statuses consistently

- Help keep the CRM clean and usable across the team

Escalate when

- tasks require US accounting review

- tasks require assistance from an Enrolled Agent

- you do not recognize certain expenses



Required Qualifications

- Licensed CPA, US Tax Preparation (not necessarily filing) experience

- Strong familiarity in using CRM's and internal documentation

- Minimum 5 years of experience with QuickBooks Online

- Excellent written English skills

- Strong understanding of accounting and financial terminology

- High attention to detail and accuracy



Relevant Certifications (Optional but advantageous):

- Enrolled Agent (EA): Specialized expertise in IRS representation.

- Certified Internal Auditor (CIA): Strong background in internal controls.

- Certified Information Systems Auditor (CISA): Expertise in financial systems. 



Ideal DISC Profile

This role is best suited for candidates with a Cd or CD DISC profile:

High C (Conscientiousness): Accuracy, compliance, documentation, attention to detail

High D (Dominance): On time, Responsive, independent, Takes pride in meeting deadlines and respond quickly to client needs.





Tools You’ll Use

- TaxDome

- QuickBooks Online

- Google Suite

- Notion

- Clickup

- Slack



Compensation & Growth

20+ hours per week - full time for the right candidate

Compensation based upon experience, speed and accuracy, ability to work within the team structure, pursuit of individual excellence while providing growth within the company



How to Apply

When applying, please include:

Your resume or CV

A short summary of your experience working as a US Tax Accountant/CPA

Your DISC profile (if available) or confirmation you align with Cdi /CD

The word “BOOKS” in your response to confirm you read the full job posting

Applications without the word BOOKS will not be reviewed.



Final Note

Our firm is in a significant growth phase. We need someone who is ready to work part time 20 hours now, and be able to work full time in the very near future. If you value precision, documentation, and being part of a team that cares about you as an individual as much as a job well done, we would love to hear from you.

title: Sales Assistant (Remote)
description:
We’re hiring a Sales Assistant to support our sales team by keeping leads organized, systems clean, and follow-ups moving. This role is not for closing sales—but it does directly support revenue, which means attention to detail and follow-through matter.

If you like structured work, clear processes, and helping a sales team perform at a high level, this role is a great fit.

You are required to work 8am-5pm MST Monday-Friday.

________________________________________

What You’ll Be Doing

Daily Tasks

• Clean and maintain lead lists in our CRM (Go High Level)

• Monitor and respond to incoming leads using pre-written responses

• Check sales workflows, SMS actions, and conversations

• Remove spam or invalid contacts

• Support LinkedIn and social engagement tasks

• Ensure outreach and automation tools are running properly

Weekly Tasks

• Prepare lead and call lists for the sales team

• Support sales and marketing check-ins

• Assist with reporting and lead tracking

Monthly Tasks

• Quality-check funnels,
s, and landing pages

• Review webinar registrants for lead quality

• Prepare pre- and post-webinar lead lists

• Assist with testing
 deliverability and basic reporting

You will also help document simple SOPs and checklists so tasks are done the same way every time.

________________________________________

Training Provided

You will receive step-by-step training on:

• Our sales process and lead flow

• CRM usage and lead tracking

• Sales follow-up support systems

• Internal tools and workflows

You are not expected to figure things out alone—we train you clearly and support you as you learn.

________________________________________

Who We’re Looking For

This role is ideal for someone who:

• Is reliable, organized, and detail-oriented

• Takes instructions seriously and follows processes

• Communicates clearly in written English

• Is motivated to support a sales team’s success

• Wants long-term, stable remote work

• Is comfortable working with CRMs or willing to learn quickly

You do NOT need sales closing experience. You do need a strong work ethic and attention to detail.

________________________________________

Required Application (Please Read Carefully)

To apply, submit BOTH of the following:

1. Your resume

2. A short video (maximum 3 minutes) answering these three questions:



Video Questions:

1. What about supporting the sales process excites you, and how do you think a sales assistant contributes to more deals being closed?

2. How do you stay organized when managing repetitive daily tasks?

3. Tell us about a time you supported a team or manager and helped them succeed.

Applications without a video will not be reviewed.

________________________________________

Pay & Growth

• $5–$6/hour, depending on experience

• Long-term role for the right person

title: GoHighLevel Specialist / Tech VA
description:
Hey there,

We're a fast growing marketing agency and we need a sharp VA who knows GoHighLevel inside and out. This isn't a click here, click there kind of role. We're looking for someone who can take a task, figure things out, and run with it.

If you need someone holding your hand on every step, this isn't the role for you. If you love building, automating, and getting stuff done without being told twice, keep reading.



What you'll be doing:



- Building and managing workflows in GoHighLevel

- Setting up funnels, forms, calendars, and pipelines

- Creating and maintaining
 and SMS automations

- Managing contacts, tags, custom fields, and CRM data

- Building out client sub accounts and snapshots

- Light reporting on automations, funnels, and campaign performance

- Helping with general agency tech tasks (Zapier, Make, basic integrations)



What we need from you:

- Strong written English, can communicate clearly without me having to explain things twice

- Self starter, takes initiative, figures things out on your own

Bonus points if you have:



- Experience with Meta Ads Manager

- Basic Canva or graphic skills or video editing skills

- Experience with cold
 tools like Apollo, Instantly, or Smartlead

- Experience working with US based agencies



What we offer:



- $2 to $5 per hour starting based on experience + Bonuses

- 20 to 25 hours per week to start

- Performance bonuses when we hit goals together

- Long term role with strong potential to grow into full time with MUCH higher pay

- A team that actually values good work and treats you like a real teammate



How to apply:

Please send me a message with all  of the following. If anything is missing your application will not be reviewed.

Thanks,

Manny

title: Medical Biller
description:
About Us

We are a US-based, AI-enabled virtual clinic. We provide remote patient monitoring, chronic care management, and other longitudinal care services to patients across the country. We're a small, fast-moving team building high-quality, preventive care — and our billing operations are central to making that work.



About the Role



We're looking for an experienced Medical Biller to join our revenue cycle team and partner directly with our billing consultant on day-to-day operations. This is a hands-on role focused on the work that actually moves money: determining eligibility, investigating claims denials, modifying claim submission details, and more.



What You'll Do



- Review denied and rejected claims, identify the reason, and take the appropriate corrective action

- Correct and resubmit claims, file appeals, and work claims through to resolution

- Follow up on aged accounts receivable and unpaid claims with payers via phone, portals, and written correspondence

- Verify patient eligibility and benefits when needed

- Document every action clearly in our systems so the team has a complete audit trail

- Work closely with our billing consultant to flag patterns, escalate trends, and improve our overall claim acceptance rate

- Maintain strict confidentiality and follow HIPAA standards in handling all patient information



What We're Looking For



- 2–5 years of experience in US healthcare medical billing or revenue cycle management

- Solid working knowledge of how claims, denials, appeals, and AR follow-up work in the US system

- Familiarity with Medicare, Medicaid, and major commercial payers

- Comfortable navigating EHRs, clearinghouses, and payer portals — we don't expect you to know our specific stack, just to pick up new tools quickly

- Strong written and spoken English

- Independent and self-motivated — looking to grow

- Naturally detail-oriented, organized, and persistent — you don't let things fall through the cracks

- Reliable home internet, a quiet workspace, and a workstation that meets basic security requirements

- Comfortable working independently while staying in close communication with a small remote team



What We Offer



- Long-term role with a growing US healthcare company

- Direct relationship with leadership

- Inside look at a company using the latest in Artificial Intelligence

- Opportunity to grow with the company as we scale our care operations

title: SEO Specialist — Local SEO, Content Strategy & AI Search Optimization
description:
SEO Specialist — Local SEO, Content Strategy & AI Search Optimization



We are looking for a talented, detail-oriented, and results-driven SEO Specialist to help improve our organic visibility, website traffic, lead generation, and search performance across multiple brands.



This role is for someone who understands modern SEO, including local SEO, technical SEO, on-page optimization, content strategy, Google Business Profile optimization, backlink strategy, AI search trends, and how search is changing with AI tools and generative search results.



We are looking for someone who can think strategically, execute consistently, and help us create SEO systems that support long-term growth.



About the Businesses



We work across multiple brands and industries, including:



Personal brand content

Entrepreneurship and business education

Construction and home service businesses

Real estate and property-related businesses

Local service businesses

Lead generation websites

Before-and-after project storytelling

Event and community-based offers



We need someone who can understand different audiences, research search intent, create content opportunities, and improve organic rankings for both local and national search.



Responsibilities



You will help with:



Creating and managing SEO strategies across multiple websites

Conducting keyword research and search intent analysis

Optimizing website pages, blog posts, service pages, landing pages, and location pages

Improving titles, meta descriptions, headings, internal links, image alt text, and page structure

Creating SEO content briefs for writers, editors, and content creators

Identifying blog topics, service page opportunities, FAQ content, and local SEO opportunities

Optimizing Google Business Profiles

Helping improve local map rankings

Reviewing competitors and identifying ranking opportunities

Auditing websites for technical SEO issues

Reviewing site speed, indexing, broken links, redirects, crawl errors, and mobile usability

Improving internal linking structure

Recommending backlink and authority-building opportunities

Tracking rankings, traffic, conversions, and SEO performance

Creating monthly SEO reports with clear insights and next steps

Using AI tools to support keyword research, content briefs, content optimization, schema ideas, and SEO analysis

Staying current with Google algorithm changes, AI search, and modern SEO best practices

Skills We Are Looking For



The right person should be:



Experienced with SEO strategy and implementation

Strong in keyword research and search intent

Comfortable with local SEO and Google Business Profile optimization

Able to optimize service-based business websites

Familiar with technical SEO basics

Good at creating clear SEO content plans

Able to use data to make recommendations

Comfortable using AI tools responsibly for SEO research, planning, and optimization

Detail-oriented and organized

Reliable with deadlines

Clear in communication

Able to explain what is working, what is not working, and what should be improved next

Interested in a long-term role, not just one-time SEO audits

Preferred Experience



Experience with any of the following is a plus:



Local service business SEO

Construction, home improvement, landscaping, or real estate SEO

Personal brand or business education SEO

Multi-location SEO

Google Business Profile optimization

Blog strategy and content planning

SEO for lead generation websites

Schema markup

Backlink outreach

Website audits

GoHighLevel / HighLevel websites and funnels

WordPress

Webflow

Shopify

AI-assisted SEO workflows

Tools





Type of Work



This will begin as a part-time role with the opportunity to grow into more hours over time.



We are looking for someone who can help us create SEO plans, optimize existing pages, identify new opportunities, and improve organic rankings consistently.



Pay



Please include your desired monthly salary or hourly rate in your application.



We are open to discussing pay based on experience, performance, and availability.



To Apply



Please send the following:



A short introduction about yourself

Your experience with SEO

Examples of websites or SEO projects you have worked on

Industries you have worked with

Your experience with local SEO and Google Business Profile optimization

Your experience using AI in keyword research, content planning, SEO audits, or optimization

The SEO tools you use regularly

Your desired monthly salary or hourly rate

Your availability and preferred working hours

The word “RANK” at the top of your application so we know you read the full post



Application Questions



Please answer these questions in your application:



What is your current approach to SEO in 2026?

How do you use AI to improve SEO strategy or workflow?

How do you d
 which keywords or pages to prioritize first?

What would you do if a website had traffic but was not generating leads?

What are the most important things you look at in a local SEO audit?

How do you improve rankings for a local service business?

What SEO tools do you use most often and why?





About Us



We are building purpose-driven brands across entrepreneurship, business growth, construction, real estate, customer experience, and community-based offers.



We are looking for someone who understands modern SEO, uses AI wisely, thinks strategically, and wants to grow with us long-term.

title: Senior Social Media Editor & Content Strategist VA
description:
Senior Social Media Editor & Content Strategist VA



Remote | Part-Time | Long-Term



We are looking for a seasoned Social Media Editor & Content Strategist VA to help grow and manage two connected brands:



Venta Furnishings (retail furniture brand)



VentaStyle (supplementary lifestyle & design brand)



This role is for someone with real experience in social media, short-form video editing, and content planning — not a beginner and not a trainee role.



You must already have your own editing tools and be comfortable working independently.



About the Role



We already have brand vision, offers, and content ideas. What we need is someone who can:



Turn ideas into consistent, high-quality content



Understand content strategy and brand positioning



Help both brands grow together in a cohesive way



You will not just “post content.”

You will help execute and scale the brand presence.



Core Responsibilities

1. Short-Form Video Editing (Primary Responsibility)



You will edit content for:



Instagram Reels



TikTok



YouTube Long form plus Shorts



You must be able to:



Edit independently using your own software (CapCut, Premiere Pro, Final Cut, or equivalent)



Add captions, text overlays, and engaging hooks



Format content correctly for each platform



Follow brand direction while contributing creative ideas



Clean, engaging, scroll-stopping content is the goal.



2. Social Media Management & Posting



You will help manage content across:



 Business Page



Instagram Business Account



TikTok



YouTube (Shorts to start)



This includes:



Scheduling posts



Tracking performance basics (what’s working / not working)



Maintaining consistent posting schedules



3. Content Strategy & Planning



You will assist with:



Organizing and managing a content calendar



Repurposing content across platforms



Helping turn brand goals into content themes



Supporting growth of both Venta Furnishings and VentaStyle



You should understand how content supports brand awareness, trust, and sales.



4. Brand Alignment Across Two Brands



You will help ensure:



Venta Furnishings remains sales-focused and practical



VentaStyle feels inspirational, lifestyle-driven, and complementary



Both brands feel connected but not repetitive



You are not the final decision-maker — but you must understand brand strategy well enough to execute it correctly.



Required Experience & Tools (Important)



You must:



Have prior experience managing and editing content for business or brand accounts



Be a seasoned short-form video editor



Already have access to your own video editing software

(CapCut, Premiere Pro, Final Cut, or equivalent)



Be comfortable editing without software provided



Have strong written English



Be organized, proactive, and reliable



Applicants without video samples or editing tools will not be considered.



Work Schedule & Pay



20–25 hours per week starting at $400 with opportunities for BONUS plus RAISES!



Part-time, long-term possibly moving to FULL TIME! 



Flexible schedule with clear deadlines



How to Apply



Please include:



A short introduction about yourself



Examples of short-form videos you’ve edited



Social media accounts or brands you’ve managed



Your weekly availability



Applications without video samples will not be considered.



Important Note



This role requires someone who:



Is experienced and self-sufficient



Already has the tools to do the job



Understands content strategy and brand growth



Can support two brands growing together



Application Filter (Highly Recommended)





***Please begin your application with the word “STRATEGY” so we know you read the full post.

title: Executive Assistant & Operations Manager - Direct-to-Consumer Brand
description:
I'm a founder running a premium direct-to-consumer brand and looking for a sharp, organized, and proactive Operations Manager / Executive Assistant to act as my right hand. You will work closely with me to manage external agencies, coordinate vendors, and execute day-to-day operations across marketing, web, fulfillment, and finance.



This is a high-trust role. You will represent me directly in
 and project management threads with our agencies and vendors, working under my name and with my signature. Strong written English, sound judgment, and the ability to make decisions quickly are essential.



CORE RESPONSIBILITIES



Vendor & Agency Management

- Be the primary point of contact for our marketing agency, web development agency, PR agency, photographer, and other external vendors

- Track deliverables, timelines, and invoices across all agency relationships

- Draft, send, and respond to
s on my behalf in my voice

- Run weekly check-ins, take notes, and circulate clear action items

- Push back, negotiate, and hold vendors accountable when timelines slip



Project & Operations Coordination

- Maintain a master tracker of all active workstreams (web, content, PR, production, logistics)

- Coordinate scheduling across multiple time zones (US, Europe, Asia)

- Keep documentation organized in Google Drive and Notion

- Build and update SOPs for repeatable processes

- Source new vendors when needed (packaging, fulfillment, freelancers) and run the comparison process



Marketing & Content Support

- Liaise with our social media agency on content calendars, captions, and approvals

- Track influencer outreach, gifting logistics, and follow-ups

- Manage
 marketing platform (Klaviyo) flows and campaign sends

- Keep our Shopify product catalog accurate and up-to-date



Finance & Admin

- Track and pay invoices through online banking

- Maintain a running budget tracker

- File receipts and forward to our accountant on a weekly basis

- Process customer service inquiries when they come in (low volume)



REQUIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

- 3+ years experience in an Executive Assistant, Project Manager, or Operations role at an early-stage company, ecommerce brand, or agency

- Excellent written English (you will be writing
s to US-based agencies and vendors)

- Strong working knowledge of: Google Workspace, Shopify, Notion, Slack, Klaviyo, Trello or Asana

- Comfortable using AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude) to accelerate work

- Experience working with US or EU clients and adjusting to time zones

- High attention to detail, especially in budgets, timelines, and vendor communication

- Self-directed, low-ego, and willing to take ownership rather than wait for instructions



NICE TO HAVE

- Prior experience with a fashion, apparel, beauty, or premium lifestyle brand

- Familiarity with international shipping, customs (DDP/DAP), and 3PL operations

- Experience managing PR or marketing agency relationships

- Light bookkeeping experience or familiarity with French accounting platforms (Pennylane) a plus

- Photo or video production coordination experience



WHAT YOU'RE WALKING INTO



We are pre-launch and moving fast. The first 60 days will be intense as we ship product, build the website, run the launch event, and execute press and influencer programs. After launch, the role stabilizes into a steady operations role with regular cadences and predictable workflows. You will report directly to me and have meaningful ownership over how things get built and run.



HOW TO APPLY



Please send the following to be considered:

1. Brief intro about yourself (1-2 paragraphs, no template responses)

2. Your CV

3. One example of a vendor or agency relationship you've successfully managed, including a specific challenge you handled

4. Your expected monthly rate (full-time, USD)

5. Available start date



Applications without all five items will not be reviewed.

title: Human Resources Generalist - Must Have US Experience
description:
Summary:



**MUST HAVE WORKDAY EXPERIENCE ALONG WITH SUPPORTING US BASED COMPANIES IN EITHER CALIFORNA OR WASHINGTON STATE**



Please apply through this form: forms.cloud.microsoft/r/GashwtwpbQ



Under the direction of the Human Resources Director, the Human Resources Generalist will support

multiple healthcare entities across various HR functions. Responsibilities include aiding the HR team in

developing and administering programs, procedures, projects, and processes throughout the

organization’s divisions, spanning the entire life cycle from recruitment and onboarding to development,

retention, and offboarding. This position will have the option to be mostly remote (work from home);

however, this position will require in-person attendance for new employee orientation, meetings,

trainings, or other collaborative activities as needed.



Essential functions & responsibilities

• Support the development and administration of programs, procedures, and guidelines to align the workforce with the strategic goals of the company, improve efficiency and effectiveness.

• Administer and assist with the rollout of new initiatives and projects across the spectrum of human resources and improvement of existing ones.

• Assist in various projects, initiatives, and processes, encompassing but not limited to annual audits, new product launches, job descriptions, performance reviews, and initiatives to enhance employee engagement.

• Ensure accurate and timely execution of administrative human resources tasks, including but not limited to maintaining employee files, managing correspondence, generating documents, overseeing systems, and uphold meticulous records

of employee information.

• Input new hire information into workday facilitate payroll and HR functions.

• Familiarize oneself with the union contract and personnel policies and procedures.

• Manage benefits administration duties, encompassing initial enrollment, change reporting, invoice approval, and communication of benefits information to employees.

• Execute position, personal, and wage changes for employees ensuring accuracy into Workday and other systems.

• Provide guidance and assistance to employees and management on various HR-related matters, including leaves compensation, and issue resolution, under the guidance of the Human Resources Director.

• Execute and support HR processes promptly, including leave administration, EEO-1 reporting, separations, L&I/Workers compensation, and unemployment claims.

• Handle sensitive employee matters with empathy, tact, and diplomacy. Assist in job postings, recruitment efforts, and the hiring/onboarding process for new employees.

• Provide administrative support to the HR department as needed.

• Stay informed about current trends, best practices, regulatory updates, and emerging

technologies in human resources talent management, and employment law.

• Promote the Mission, Vision and Values of company in all areas of responsibility, and demonstrate commitment to Culture of Excellence and strategic goals

• Other duties as assigned.

title: Talent Acquisition Specialist - Non-Profit Home Health Care Agency
description:
MUST HAVE EXPERIENCE WITH US BASED COMPANIES IN WASHINGTON STATE AND/OR CALIFORNIA. MUST HAVE EXPERIENCE WITH MEDICARE/MEDICARE, WELLSKY AND WORKDAY. 



Please apply through this form: 



Summary / objective 

The Talent Acquisition Specialist is responsible for driving the recruitment efforts for a home health care agency and recruitment initiatives, ensuring that we attract, engage, and hire top

talent to meet our organization needs. You will manage the end-to-end recruitment process, from 

sourcing and screening candidates to facilitating interviews and extending job offers. Your role will 

involve collaborating closely with hiring managers to understand their staffing requirements, 

developing effective recruitment strategies, and fostering a positive candidate experience. 



Essential functions & responsibilities

• Collaborate with hiring managers to understand specific hiring needs, job requirements, and

qualifications.

• Responsible for creating and managing job postings for open positions.

• Regularly update hiring managers on the recruitment progress, maintaining transparent

communication throughout the process.

• Monitor and assess the performance of job postings and promptly adjust recruitment strategies

as necessary to attract qualified candidates effectively.

• Review resumes, conduct initial screenings, and evaluate candidate’s qualifications to determine

suitability for open positions.

• Coordinate interviews, including phone screens, and virtual hiring events.

• Ensure a positive candidate experience throughout the recruitment process. This includes clear

communication, timely feedback, and a smooth onboarding process.

• Extend jobs offers and facilitate the onboarding process through the BambooHR system,

overseeing tasks such as contacting references, E-Verify, scheduling fingerprint appointments

and coordinating TB testing if necessary.

• Ensure all new hire paperwork is completed accurately and properly filed.

• Schedule and facilitate new hire orientations, collaborating with the training team to ensure a

seamless onboarding experience.

• Collect feedback from new hires and hiring managers to assess the effectiveness of the

onboarding process.

• Make recommendations for improvements to enhance the onboarding experience.

• Provide weekly summaries of recruiting activities and progress to Human Resources Director.

title: Content Reviewer & Editor
description:
2-Week Contract (Remote, Philippines)



**Pay:** $5.00 USD/hour (plus a small bonus per real error caught — details shared at kickoff)

**Hours:** Monday–Friday, 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM Philippine Time (7 working hours + 1 paid lunch hour)

**Start Date:** Week of May 3

**Contract Length:** 2 weeks, with potential for renewal or longer-term work for top performers

**Positions Available:** 4



---



## About Beyond The Fall



Beyond The Fall is a global preparedness brand built around an 852-page hardcover survival encyclopedia — 5 years in the making, with 80+ expert contributors from 50 countries. We sell direct-to-consumer and through Amazon across the US, UK, Canada, Australia, and Europe. Small, fast-moving team. Serious about the work, fun to work with.



## About the Role



We're prepping our first edition for a second-edition release and need sharp-eyed reviewers to make sure nothing embarrassing slips through. This isn't deep editing — it's a focused **error sweep**. We want the big stuff: misspellings, wrong words, broken sentences, factual mistakes, things that look visually off.



You'll work as part of a four-person team split into two review pairs. Each pair reads the same pages, so two sets of eyes are on every page. You read, you catch, you flag — directly in the file using **Adobe Acrobat Pro**.



## What We're Looking For



- A genuinely sharp eye — you catch what others skim past

- Strong command of written English

- Reliable, consistant, on-time

- Comfortable working closely with a review partner

- Prior proofreading or editing experience is a plus, not a must



## How to Apply



Send your **resume** along with a short cover letter that includes:



1. A quick summary of your relevant experience

2. A real example of a mistake you've caught in someone else's writing

3. Confirmation you can work Monday–Friday, 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM Philippine Time

4. Your earliest available start date



**Start your
 with the word PINEAPPLE** — that's how we know you read this all the way through. We are looking to begin interviews Wednesday & Thursday AM.  Our ideal candidates will be selected ASAP.



We move fast. If you're a fit, you'll hear from us quickly.

title: Billing & Procurement Account Manager
description:
About Edge360 / Staff360

Edge360 / Staff360 is a trusted Managed Service Provider (MSP) and Business Process Outsourcing (BPO) agency. We help businesses streamline operations, optimize technology, and scale efficiently by providing reliable IT solutions and outsourcing support.

We’re growing fast and looking for motivated professionals who want to be part of a team that drives digital transformation.



Position Overview

We’re hiring a Billing & Procurement Account Manager who will play a key role in managing billing, procurement, and financial operations. This role requires someone detail-oriented, proactive, and comfortable working in a fast-paced environment.

You’ll be the go-to person for ensuring accuracy in billing, smooth procurement processes, and strong vendor relationships—all while supporting our MSP and BPO projects.



Key Responsibilities

Prepare accurate and competitive quotes for client projects in collaboration with sales and technical teams.

Manage procurement: sourcing, ordering, and tracking hardware, software, and other project resources.

Handle billing and invoicing, ensuring compliance with company policies and client agreements.

Maintain project plans, timelines, and budgets to support efficient delivery.

Act as the main contact for accounts payable/receivable, resolving inquiries quickly.

Work with the accounting team on reconciliations, reporting, and audits.

Manage vendor relationships, negotiate contracts, and identify cost-saving opportunities.

Assist with financial forecasting, budgeting, and process improvements.

Support ad-hoc tasks related to billing, procurement, and project management.



Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Accounting, or related field (or equivalent experience).

At least 3 years of experience in billing, procurement, or financial management—preferably in MSP, BPO, or IT services.

Proficiency in financial software (QuickBooks, SAP, etc.) and project management tools (Microsoft Project, Asana, etc.).

Strong knowledge of procurement processes, vendor management, and invoicing best practices.

Excellent communication skills and the ability to work well with both internal teams and external partners.

High attention to detail, strong analytical skills, and problem-solving ability.

Ability to multitask and prioritize in a deadline-driven environment.

Familiarity with MSP/BPO industry standards is a plus.



What We Offer

Competitive salary with performance-based bonuses

Opportunities for career growth and professional development

Flexible remote work options

A collaborative and supportive team environment

Exposure to cutting-edge projects in a growing industry



How to Apply

If this sounds like the right fit for you, send us your resume and a short intro video highlighting your relevant experience.



We look forward to welcoming the right candidate to our team!

title: IT Manager / Field Supervisor
description:
Overview

We are seeking a seasoned IT Manager / Field Supervisor to lead our Managed Service Provider (MSP) operations with a strong focus on cloud infrastructure. This role will oversee helpdesk operations, field dispatch, and project management across a diverse client base. The ideal candidate will bring deep expertise in Azure, Microsoft 365, AWS, and hybrid environments, along with a proven ability to lead teams and deliver exceptional IT support in a fast-paced MSP setting.



Key Responsibilities

Lead and manage the MSP helpdesk, ensuring timely resolution of tickets and high client satisfaction

Coordinate and dispatch field technicians for on-site support and project implementations

Oversee cloud-centric IT projects including migrations, deployments, and infrastructure upgrades

Provide hands-on technical leadership in Azure, Microsoft 365, AWS, and hybrid environments

Mentor and manage a team of IT professionals, fostering collaboration and growth

Monitor SLAs and KPIs, and implement process improvements for operational efficiency

Collaborate with clients to assess needs, propose cloud-first solutions, and maintain strong relationships



Required Qualifications

Minimum of 10 years in IT management, with at least 5 years in an MSP helpdesk environment leading teams

Deep technical proficiency in:

Azure AD, Office 365, Intune, SharePoint, MS Exchange Server

AWS services including EC2, S3, IAM, and VPC configurations

Windows Server (on-prem and cloud-hosted)

Google Workspace and Google Cloud infrastructure

Setup and management of Ubiquiti, Cisco, SonicWall, Synology hardware

Strong project management experience in cloud migrations and IT deployments

Proven ability to manage ticket workflows and dispatch field resources effectively

Excellent leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills

Ability to work under pressure and adapt to evolving client needs



Preferred Qualifications

Certifications such as Microsoft Certified: Azure Administrator, AWS Certified Solutions Architect, Cisco CCNA

Experience with MSP tools like RMM and PSA platforms

Familiarity with cybersecurity best practices and compliance standards (e.g., HIPAA, GDPR)



Why Join Us

Lead a dynamic team in a growing MSP with a cloud-first approach

Competitive salary and benefits package

Access to cutting-edge technologies and ongoing professional development

Collaborative and innovative work culture



How to Apply

Please submit your resume, a cover letter detailing your relevant experience, and a short intro video. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled.



Equal Opportunity Employer

We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, or any other protected status.
